    53вҶ’| FR-6.1 | Gantt | Display timeline view (4-week, trains as rows) | P1 |
    54вҶ’| FR-6.2 | Gantt | Show maintenance blocks (color-coded PM1/PM2/HM) | P1 |
    55вҶ’| FR-6.3 | Gantt | Enable drag-drop scheduling | P1 |
    56вҶ’| FR-6.4 | Gantt | Validate constraints real-time | P1 |
    57вҶ’| FR-6.5 | Gantt | Show depot capacity per week | P1 |
    58вҶ’| FR-7.1 | Solver | Accept service request (ID, date, route profile) | P0 |
    59вҶ’| FR-7.2 | Solver | Filter available trains (exclude unfit, in-maintenance) | P0 |

   117вҶ’## Epic List
   118вҶ’
   119вҶ’| Epic | Title | Primary Persona | Priority | Stories |
   120вҶ’|------|-------|-----------------|----------|---------|
   121вҶ’| 1 | Project Foundation & Infrastructure | Developer | P0 | 6 |
   122вҶ’| 2 | Dashboard & KPIs | Gabriel (Manager) | P0 | 5 |
   123вҶ’| 3 | Map & Fleet Visualization | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 5 |
   124вҶ’| 4 | Allocation with Recommendations | Diana (Dispatcher) | P0 | 6 |
   125вҶ’| 5 | Km Balancing & Simulation | Paul (Planner) | P0 | 6 |

   128вҶ’---
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Infrastructure
   131вҶ’
   132вҶ’**Goal:** Establish the technical foundation enabling all feature development, including monorepo structure, database schema, API skeleton, and Python solver service.
   133вҶ’
   134вҶ’**Covers:** AR-1 to AR-12, NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3
   135вҶ’
   136вҶ’---
   137вҶ’
   138вҶ’### Story 1.1: Setup Monorepo Structure
   139вҶ’
   140вҶ’As a **developer**,
   141вҶ’I want **a properly structured monorepo with all required packages**,
   142вҶ’So that **I can develop frontend, backend, and solver in a coordinated way**.
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   145вҶ’
   146вҶ’**Given** an empty project directory
   147вҶ’**When** the scaffolding script completes
   148вҶ’**Then** the following structure exists:
   149вҶ’```
   150вҶ’mms/
   151вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json (workspaces config)
   152вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/web/ (Next.js 14)
   153вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/api/ (Fastify)
   154вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ packages/types/ (shared TypeScript)
   155вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver/ (Python)
   156вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ docker-compose.yml
   157вҶ’```
   158вҶ’**And** `bun install` succeeds without errors
   159вҶ’**And** TypeScript compilation passes for all packages
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’---
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’### Story 1.2: Configure Database Schema
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’As a **developer**,
   166вҶ’I want **the complete PostgreSQL schema with migrations**,
   167вҶ’So that **all entities are properly modeled and queryable**.
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   170вҶ’
   171вҶ’**Given** PostgreSQL 16 is running via Docker
   172вҶ’**When** migrations are applied
   173вҶ’**Then** the following tables exist with correct relationships:
   174вҶ’- `fleets` (id, name, manufacturer, pm1/pm2/hm intervals)
   175вҶ’- `depots` (id, name, code, lat/lon, capacity_tracks, capacity_manhours)
   176вҶ’- `trains` (id, train_id, fleet_id FK, base_depot_id FK, current_km, fitness_status)
   177вҶ’- `services` (id, service_code, origin, destination, distance_km, times)
   178вҶ’- `allocations` (id, service_id FK, train_id FK, date, status, justifications JSONB)
   179вҶ’- `mileage_history` (id, train_id FK, recorded_date, km_value)
   180вҶ’- `maintenance_schedules` (id, train_id FK, depot_id FK, type, date, status)
   181вҶ’**And** all indexes are created for performance
   182вҶ’**And** FK constraints enforce data integrity
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’---
   185вҶ’
   186вҶ’### Story 1.3: Create Seed Data Generator
   187вҶ’
   188вҶ’As a **developer**,
   189вҶ’I want **realistic seed data for demos**,
   190вҶ’So that **the system demonstrates with believable railway data**.
   191вҶ’
   192вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   193вҶ’
   194вҶ’**Given** an empty database with schema applied
   195вҶ’**When** the seed script runs
   196вҶ’**Then** the database contains:
   197вҶ’- 4-5 fleets (e.g., Metro Series 2200, Intercity Series 3400)
   198вҶ’- 12-15 depots with Portuguese city names and coordinates
   199вҶ’- 100-120 trains distributed across fleets with varied mileage (Вұ30% from average)
   200вҶ’- 200-300 daily services with realistic routes
   201вҶ’- 6 months of mileage history showing km progression
   202вҶ’**And** km variance across fleet is between 15-20% (to demonstrate balancing need)
   203вҶ’**And** 5-10 trains are marked as outliers (>25% deviation)
   204вҶ’
   205вҶ’---
   206вҶ’
   207вҶ’### Story 1.4: Implement Fastify API Skeleton
   208вҶ’
   209вҶ’As a **developer**,
   210вҶ’I want **a working Fastify server with route stubs**,
   211вҶ’So that **frontend can start integration early**.
   212вҶ’
   213вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   214вҶ’
   215вҶ’**Given** the API server starts successfully
   216вҶ’**When** health check endpoint is called
   217вҶ’**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO date }`
   218вҶ’**And** the following route files exist with stub implementations:
   219вҶ’- `/api/dashboard/*` (summary, depot-capacity)
   220вҶ’- `/api/trains/*` (list, details, history)
   221вҶ’- `/api/depots/*` (list)
   222вҶ’- `/api/services/*` (pending)
   223вҶ’- `/api/allocations/*` (recommend, create)
   224вҶ’- `/api/simulation/*` (create, run, compare)
   225вҶ’**And** each stub returns appropriate mock data
   226вҶ’**And** CORS is configured for `localhost:3000`
   227вҶ’
   228вҶ’---
   229вҶ’
   230вҶ’### Story 1.5: Setup Python Solver Service
   231вҶ’
   232вҶ’As a **developer**,
   233вҶ’I want **a FastAPI solver service with OR-Tools installed**,
   234вҶ’So that **recommendation engine development can begin**.
   235вҶ’
   236вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’**Given** Python 3.11 environment with requirements installed
   239вҶ’**When** the solver service starts
   240вҶ’**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', ortools_version: 'x.x' }`
   241вҶ’**And** `POST /recommend` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
   242вҶ’**And** `POST /simulate` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
   243вҶ’**And** response includes `solver_time_ms` field
   244вҶ’**And** service runs on port 8000
   245вҶ’
   246вҶ’---
   247вҶ’
   248вҶ’### Story 1.6: Configure Docker Compose Environment
   249вҶ’
   250вҶ’As a **developer**,
   251вҶ’I want **one-command local development startup**,
   252вҶ’So that **I can quickly reset and run the complete system**.
   253вҶ’
   254вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   255вҶ’
   256вҶ’**Given** Docker and Docker Compose are installed
   257вҶ’**When** `docker-compose up` is executed
   258вҶ’**Then** the following services start:
   259вҶ’- PostgreSQL 16 on port 5432
   260вҶ’- Redis 7 on port 6379
   261вҶ’**And** database is initialized with schema
   262вҶ’**And** `docker-compose down -v && docker-compose up` resets to clean state
   263вҶ’**And** `bun run seed` populates demo data after services are up
   264вҶ’
   265вҶ’---
   266вҶ’
   267вҶ’## Epic 2: Dashboard & KPIs
   268вҶ’
   269вҶ’**Goal:** Provide Gabriel (Fleet Manager) with an executive overview showing fleet health, mileage variance, and actionable alerts at a glance.
   270вҶ’
   271вҶ’**Covers:** FR-1.1 to FR-1.5, NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3
   272вҶ’
   273вҶ’**Primary Persona:** Gabriel (Fleet Manager)
   274вҶ’
   275вҶ’---
   276вҶ’
   277вҶ’### Story 2.1: Display Fleet Summary KPIs
   278вҶ’
   279вҶ’As a **Fleet Manager**,
   280вҶ’I want **to see total trains, available count, maintenance count, and km variance at a glance**,
   281вҶ’So that **I understand fleet health in seconds**.
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   284вҶ’
   285вҶ’**Given** the Dashboard page loads
   286вҶ’**When** data fetches from `/api/dashboard/summary`
   287вҶ’**Then** four KPI cards are displayed:
   288вҶ’- Total Trains: shows count (e.g., "112")
   289вҶ’- Available: shows count and percentage (e.g., "98 (87%)")
   290вҶ’- In Maintenance: shows count (e.g., "14")
   291вҶ’- Km Variance: shows percentage (e.g., "18%")
   292вҶ’**And** each card uses EN 15380 terminology (not "Vehicle" or "Asset")
   293вҶ’**And** page loads in < 3 seconds (NFR-1.2)
   294вҶ’
   295вҶ’---
   296вҶ’
   297вҶ’### Story 2.2: Show Variance Traffic-Light Indicator
   298вҶ’
   299вҶ’As a **Fleet Manager**,
   300вҶ’I want **the variance KPI to show green/yellow/red status**,
   301вҶ’So that **I immediately know if mileage distribution is healthy**.
   302вҶ’
   303вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   304вҶ’
   305вҶ’**Given** the Dashboard displays the Km Variance KPI
   306вҶ’**When** variance is вүӨ10%
   307вҶ’**Then** the indicator shows green color and checkmark icon
   308вҶ’**When** variance is between 10-20%
   309вҶ’**Then** the indicator shows yellow/amber color and warning icon
   310вҶ’**When** variance is >20%
   311вҶ’**Then** the indicator shows red color and alert icon
   312вҶ’**And** hovering shows tooltip: "Fleet mileage variance: X% (target: вүӨ10%)"
   313вҶ’
   314вҶ’---
   315вҶ’
   316вҶ’### Story 2.3: Display Depot Capacity Cards
   317вҶ’
   318вҶ’As a **Fleet Manager**,
   319вҶ’I want **to see each depot's current occupancy**,
   320вҶ’So that **I can identify capacity bottlenecks**.
   321вҶ’
   322вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   323вҶ’
   324вҶ’**Given** the Dashboard page loads
   325вҶ’**When** depot data fetches from `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity`
   326вҶ’**Then** depot cards are displayed in a grid (2-4 columns)
   327вҶ’**And** each card shows:
   328вҶ’- Depot name and code
   329вҶ’- Occupancy bar (current/max tracks)
   330вҶ’- Percentage used with color coding (green <70%, yellow 70-90%, red >90%)
   331вҶ’**And** clicking a depot card navigates to depot detail view
   332вҶ’
   333вҶ’---
   334вҶ’
   335вҶ’### Story 2.4: Show Maintenance Alerts
   336вҶ’
   337вҶ’As a **Fleet Manager**,

   590вҶ’### Story 4.6: Implement Solver Recommendation Engine
   591вҶ’

   854вҶ’# NEW EPICS - Added via SCP-001 (2025-12-28)
   855вҶ’
   856вҶ’## Epic 7: What-If Scenario Planning System
   857вҶ’
   858вҶ’**Priority:** Critical
   859вҶ’**Dependencies:** Epic 5 (Balancing foundation)
   860вҶ’**Added:** SCP-001 - Visual testing revealed What-If is non-functional stub
   861вҶ’
   862вҶ’### Description
   863вҶ’
   864вҶ’Complete scenario planning system allowing planners to create sandbox environments, test different allocation strategies, simulate workforce changes, and compare outcomes before committing to production.
   865вҶ’
   866вҶ’### Stories
   867вҶ’
   868вҶ’#### Story 7.1: Scenario Sandbox Mode
   869вҶ’**ID:** 7-1-scenario-sandbox-mode
   870вҶ’**Priority:** P0
   871вҶ’
   872вҶ’**As a** fleet planner
   873вҶ’**I want to** create a sandbox copy of current fleet state
   874вҶ’**So that** I can experiment with changes without affecting live data
   875вҶ’
   876вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   877вҶ’- [ ] "Create Scenario" button clones current state
   878вҶ’- [ ] Sandbox is visually distinguished (badge/border)
   879вҶ’- [ ] Changes in sandbox don't affect live data
   880вҶ’- [ ] Can discard sandbox anytime
   881вҶ’- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**
   882вҶ’
   883вҶ’---
   884вҶ’
   885вҶ’#### Story 7.2: Fleet Size Simulation
   886вҶ’**ID:** 7-2-fleet-size-simulation
   887вҶ’**Priority:** P1
   888вҶ’
   889вҶ’**As a** fleet planner
   890вҶ’**I want to** add/remove trains from a scenario
   891вҶ’**So that** I can see impact on km distribution and variance
   892вҶ’
   893вҶ’**Acceptance Criteria:**
   894вҶ’- [ ] Can add virtual trains to scenario
   895вҶ’- [ ] Can remove trains from scenario
   896вҶ’- [ ] KPIs update in real-time
   897вҶ’- [ ] Impact on variance displayed
   898вҶ’- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**
   899вҶ’
   900вҶ’---
   901вҶ’
   902вҶ’#### Story 7.3: Workforce/Vacation Planning
   903вҶ’**ID:** 7-3-workforce-vacation-planning
   904вҶ’**Priority:** P1
   905вҶ’
   906вҶ’**As a** maintenance manager

   908вҶ’**So that** I can test coverage during holidays

   915вҶ’- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**
   916вҶ’
   917вҶ’---
   918вҶ’
   919вҶ’#### Story 7.4: Budget Allocation Scenarios
   920вҶ’**ID:** 7-4-budget-allocation-scenarios
   921вҶ’**Priority:** P2
   922вҶ’
   923вҶ’**As a** fleet manager

   931вҶ’- [ ] ROI estimates displayed
   932вҶ’- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**
   933вҶ’
   934вҶ’---
   935вҶ’
   936вҶ’#### Story 7.5: Scenario Comparison Dashboard

file.content:
---
stepsCompleted: [1, 2, 3, 4]
inputDocuments:
  - _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prd.md
  - _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md
  - _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/ux-design-specification.md

---

# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System - Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for MMS, decomposing the requirements from the PRD, UX Design, and Architecture into implementable stories organized by user value delivery.

**Project Context:**
- 12-week demo timeline
- 15-minute presentation to win competitive bid
- Primary differentiator: Km balancing as primary objective with visible justifications

---

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

| ID | Module | Requirement | Priority |
|----|--------|-------------|----------|
| FR-1.1 | Dashboard | Display fleet summary KPIs (total, available, maintenance, variance) | P0 |
| FR-1.2 | Dashboard | Show variance indicator with traffic-light (вүӨ10% green, 10-20% yellow, >20% red) | P0 |
| FR-1.3 | Dashboard | Display depot capacity cards | P1 |
| FR-1.4 | Dashboard | Show maintenance alerts (upcoming PM/HM in 7 days) | P1 |
| FR-1.5 | Dashboard | Enable drill-down navigation | P0 |
| FR-2.1 | Map | Display train positions on map | P0 |
| FR-2.2 | Map | Color-code markers by status (available=green, in-service=blue, maintenance=red) | P0 |
| FR-2.3 | Map | Show depot locations with capacity indicator | P1 |
| FR-2.4 | Map | Enable train selection (click marker shows train card) | P0 |
| FR-2.5 | Map | Auto-refresh positions every 30 seconds | P1 |
| FR-3.1 | Allocation | List pending services | P0 |
| FR-3.2 | Allocation | Generate train recommendations (ranked list with scores) | P0 |
| FR-3.3 | Allocation | Display justifications (3+ reasons per recommendation) | P0 |
| FR-3.4 | Allocation | Confirm allocation (one-click assign) | P0 |
| FR-3.5 | Allocation | Filter trains by criteria (fleet, depot, km range) | P1 |
| FR-4.1 | Balancing | Visualize km distribution (histogram or box plot) | P0 |
| FR-4.2 | Balancing | Rank trains by delta (sorted list: most over/under) | P0 |
| FR-4.3 | Balancing | Show fleet average reference line | P0 |
| FR-4.4 | Balancing | Identify outliers (highlight >15% deviation) | P1 |
| FR-5.1 | Simulation | Create what-if scenario (clone current state) | P0 |
| FR-5.2 | Simulation | Modify future allocations in scenario | P0 |
| FR-5.3 | Simulation | Run simulation (calculate projected impact) | P0 |
| FR-5.4 | Simulation | Compare scenarios side-by-side | P0 |
| FR-5.5 | Simulation | Apply scenario to live plan | P1 |
| FR-6.1 | Gantt | Display timeline view (4-week, trains as rows) | P1 |
| FR-6.2 | Gantt | Show maintenance blocks (color-coded PM1/PM2/HM) | P1 |
| FR-6.3 | Gantt | Enable drag-drop scheduling | P1 |
| FR-6.4 | Gantt | Validate constraints real-time | P1 |
| FR-6.5 | Gantt | Show depot capacity per week | P1 |
| FR-7.1 | Solver | Accept service request (ID, date, route profile) | P0 |
| FR-7.2 | Solver | Filter available trains (exclude unfit, in-maintenance) | P0 |
| FR-7.3 | Solver | Score by balancing criteria (prioritize under-running) | P0 |
| FR-7.4 | Solver | Consider dead running (factor proximity) | P1 |
| FR-7.5 | Solver | Generate justifications (3+ human-readable reasons) | P0 |
| FR-7.6 | Solver | Return ranked list (top 5 recommendations) | P0 |

### Non-Functional Requirements

| ID | Category | Requirement | Target |
|----|----------|-------------|--------|
| NFR-1.1 | Performance | Allocation recommendation time | < 30 seconds |
| NFR-1.2 | Performance | Screen loading | < 3 seconds |
| NFR-1.3 | Performance | Map refresh | < 5 seconds |
| NFR-1.4 | Performance | Simulation response | < 10 seconds |
| NFR-1.5 | Performance | Database queries | < 500ms |
| NFR-2.1 | Scalability | Trains supported | 80-120 |
| NFR-2.2 | Scalability | Depots supported | 12-15 |
| NFR-2.3 | Scalability | Daily services | 200-300 |
| NFR-2.4 | Scalability | Concurrent users | 10-20 |
| NFR-3.1 | Reliability | Demo stability | 15 min no crash |
| NFR-3.2 | Reliability | Data consistency | No conflicts |
| NFR-3.3 | Reliability | Fallback screens | Available |
| NFR-4.1 | Usability | Allocation flow | < 2 min, < 5 clicks |
| NFR-4.2 | Usability | Learning curve | 5 min orientation |
| NFR-4.3 | Usability | Domain terminology | 100% EN 15380 |

### Additional Requirements (from Architecture)

| ID | Category | Requirement |
|----|----------|-------------|
| AR-1 | Infrastructure | Docker Compose development environment |
| AR-2 | Infrastructure | Monorepo structure (apps/web, apps/api, solver/) |
| AR-3 | Database | PostgreSQL 16 with defined schema |
| AR-4 | Database | Seed data (100-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services) |
| AR-5 | Backend | Fastify API server with routes per module |
| AR-6 | Backend | Redis caching with defined TTLs |
| AR-7 | Solver | Python FastAPI with OR-Tools CP-SAT |
| AR-8 | Frontend | Next.js 14 App Router structure |
| AR-9 | Frontend | @zazcart/toolkit for state management |
| AR-10 | Frontend | shadcn/ui component library |
| AR-11 | Resilience | Solver fallback (simple scoring if timeout) |
| AR-12 | Resilience | Error boundaries with retry |

---

### FR Coverage Map

| Epic | Functional Requirements Covered | NFRs Addressed |
|------|--------------------------------|----------------|

## Epic 1: Project Foundation & Infrastructure

**Goal:** Establish the technical foundation enabling all feature development, including monorepo structure, database schema, API skeleton, and Python solver service.

**Covers:** AR-1 to AR-12, NFR-3.1, NFR-3.3

---

### Story 1.1: Setup Monorepo Structure

As a **developer**,
I want **a properly structured monorepo with all required packages**,
So that **I can develop frontend, backend, and solver in a coordinated way**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty project directory
**When** the scaffolding script completes
**Then** the following structure exists:
```
mms/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json (workspaces config)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/web/ (Next.js 14)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/api/ (Fastify)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ packages/types/ (shared TypeScript)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver/ (Python)
в””в”Җв”Җ docker-compose.yml
```
**And** `bun install` succeeds without errors
**And** TypeScript compilation passes for all packages

---

### Story 1.2: Configure Database Schema

As a **developer**,
I want **the complete PostgreSQL schema with migrations**,
So that **all entities are properly modeled and queryable**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** PostgreSQL 16 is running via Docker
**When** migrations are applied
**Then** the following tables exist with correct relationships:
- `fleets` (id, name, manufacturer, pm1/pm2/hm intervals)
- `depots` (id, name, code, lat/lon, capacity_tracks, capacity_manhours)
- `trains` (id, train_id, fleet_id FK, base_depot_id FK, current_km, fitness_status)
- `services` (id, service_code, origin, destination, distance_km, times)
- `allocations` (id, service_id FK, train_id FK, date, status, justifications JSONB)
- `mileage_history` (id, train_id FK, recorded_date, km_value)
- `maintenance_schedules` (id, train_id FK, depot_id FK, type, date, status)
**And** all indexes are created for performance
**And** FK constraints enforce data integrity

---

### Story 1.3: Create Seed Data Generator

As a **developer**,
I want **realistic seed data for demos**,
So that **the system demonstrates with believable railway data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** an empty database with schema applied
**When** the seed script runs
**Then** the database contains:
- 4-5 fleets (e.g., Metro Series 2200, Intercity Series 3400)
- 12-15 depots with Portuguese city names and coordinates
- 100-120 trains distributed across fleets with varied mileage (Вұ30% from average)
- 200-300 daily services with realistic routes
- 6 months of mileage history showing km progression
**And** km variance across fleet is between 15-20% (to demonstrate balancing need)
**And** 5-10 trains are marked as outliers (>25% deviation)

---

### Story 1.4: Implement Fastify API Skeleton

As a **developer**,
I want **a working Fastify server with route stubs**,
So that **frontend can start integration early**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the API server starts successfully
**When** health check endpoint is called
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', timestamp: ISO date }`
**And** the following route files exist with stub implementations:
- `/api/dashboard/*` (summary, depot-capacity)
- `/api/trains/*` (list, details, history)
- `/api/depots/*` (list)
- `/api/services/*` (pending)
- `/api/allocations/*` (recommend, create)
- `/api/simulation/*` (create, run, compare)
**And** each stub returns appropriate mock data
**And** CORS is configured for `localhost:3000`

---

### Story 1.5: Setup Python Solver Service

As a **developer**,
I want **a FastAPI solver service with OR-Tools installed**,
So that **recommendation engine development can begin**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Python 3.11 environment with requirements installed
**When** the solver service starts
**Then** `GET /health` returns `{ status: 'ok', ortools_version: 'x.x' }`
**And** `POST /recommend` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** `POST /simulate` endpoint exists (returns mock response initially)
**And** response includes `solver_time_ms` field
**And** service runs on port 8000

---

### Story 1.6: Configure Docker Compose Environment

As a **developer**,
I want **one-command local development startup**,
So that **I can quickly reset and run the complete system**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Docker and Docker Compose are installed
**When** `docker-compose up` is executed
**Then** the following services start:
- PostgreSQL 16 on port 5432
- Redis 7 on port 6379
**And** database is initialized with schema
**And** `docker-compose down -v && docker-compose up` resets to clean state
**And** `bun run seed` populates demo data after services are up

---

## Epic 2: Dashboard & KPIs

**Goal:** Provide Gabriel (Fleet Manager) with an executive overview showing fleet health, mileage variance, and actionable alerts at a glance.

**Covers:** FR-1.1 to FR-1.5, NFR-1.2, NFR-4.3

**Primary Persona:** Gabriel (Fleet Manager)

---

### Story 2.1: Display Fleet Summary KPIs

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see total trains, available count, maintenance count, and km variance at a glance**,
So that **I understand fleet health in seconds**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/dashboard/summary`
**Then** four KPI cards are displayed:
- Total Trains: shows count (e.g., "112")
- Available: shows count and percentage (e.g., "98 (87%)")
- In Maintenance: shows count (e.g., "14")
- Km Variance: shows percentage (e.g., "18%")
**And** each card uses EN 15380 terminology (not "Vehicle" or "Asset")
**And** page loads in < 3 seconds (NFR-1.2)

---

### Story 2.2: Show Variance Traffic-Light Indicator

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **the variance KPI to show green/yellow/red status**,
So that **I immediately know if mileage distribution is healthy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard displays the Km Variance KPI
**When** variance is вүӨ10%
**Then** the indicator shows green color and checkmark icon
**When** variance is between 10-20%
**Then** the indicator shows yellow/amber color and warning icon
**When** variance is >20%
**Then** the indicator shows red color and alert icon
**And** hovering shows tooltip: "Fleet mileage variance: X% (target: вүӨ10%)"

---

### Story 2.3: Display Depot Capacity Cards

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see each depot's current occupancy**,
So that **I can identify capacity bottlenecks**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity`
**Then** depot cards are displayed in a grid (2-4 columns)
**And** each card shows:
- Depot name and code
- Occupancy bar (current/max tracks)
- Percentage used with color coding (green <70%, yellow 70-90%, red >90%)
**And** clicking a depot card navigates to depot detail view

---

### Story 2.4: Show Maintenance Alerts

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to see upcoming maintenance events**,
So that **I'm aware of planned capacity reductions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard page loads
**When** there are maintenance events scheduled in the next 7 days
**Then** an alerts panel shows:
- Count of upcoming PM1, PM2, HM events
- List of next 5 events with train ID, type, date, depot
**And** clicking an alert navigates to the Maintenance Gantt view
**And** if no upcoming maintenance, shows "No scheduled maintenance in next 7 days"

---

### Story 2.5: Enable Drill-Down Navigation

As a **Fleet Manager**,
I want **to click any element to see details**,
So that **I can investigate issues without navigating menus**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Dashboard is displayed
**When** I click on "Available" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by status=fit
**When** I click on "In Maintenance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Maintenance Gantt view
**When** I click on "Km Variance" KPI
**Then** I navigate to Balancing view with distribution chart
**When** I click on a depot card
**Then** I navigate to Fleet list filtered by depot
**And** all navigation maintains context (no page reload flash)

---

## Epic 3: Map & Fleet Visualization

**Goal:** Provide Diana (Dispatcher) with a real-time visual map showing train positions, depot locations, and quick access to train details.

**Covers:** FR-2.1 to FR-2.5, NFR-1.3

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 3.1: Display Train Positions on Map

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all active trains on an interactive map**,
So that **I understand current fleet distribution**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** train position data fetches from `/api/trains`
**Then** a map centered on Portugal displays
**And** each train appears as a marker at its lat/lon position
**And** trains without position show at their base depot location
**And** map renders in < 2 seconds

---

### Story 3.2: Color-Code Markers by Status

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **trains color-coded by operational status**,
So that **I quickly identify available trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** a train has fitness_status='fit' and no active service
**Then** marker is green (Available)
**When** a train has an active allocation today
**Then** marker is blue (In-Service)
**When** a train has fitness_status='unfit' or scheduled maintenance
**Then** marker is red (Maintenance)
**And** a legend shows the three status colors
**And** filter toggles allow showing/hiding each status

---

### Story 3.3: Show Depot Locations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **depot locations visible on the map**,
So that **I understand the maintenance network**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page loads
**When** depot data fetches from `/api/depots`
**Then** each depot appears as a distinct icon (not train marker)
**And** depot icon shows capacity usage (color-coded pie or bar)
**And** hovering depot shows tooltip: "Name | Tracks: X/Y | Manhours: Z available"
**And** depots can be toggled on/off via map controls

---

### Story 3.4: Enable Train Selection

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to click a train marker and see details**,
So that **I can assess train suitability quickly**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map displays train markers
**When** I click a train marker
**Then** a slide-out panel or popup card shows:
- Train ID (e.g., UTE-3400-012)
- Fleet type
- Current km and variance from average
- Fitness status
- Base depot
- Next scheduled maintenance (type, date, km)
**And** card includes "View Details" link to full train page
**And** card includes "Allocate" button (if train is available)

---

### Story 3.5: Auto-Refresh Positions

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **train positions to update automatically**,
So that **I see near-real-time fleet state**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Map page is open
**When** 30 seconds pass
**Then** train positions refresh automatically
**And** markers animate smoothly to new positions (no flicker)
**And** a "Last updated: HH:MM:SS" indicator shows refresh time
**And** manual "Refresh Now" button is available
**And** refresh completes in < 5 seconds (NFR-1.3)

---

## Epic 4: Allocation with Recommendations

**Goal:** Enable Diana (Dispatcher) to allocate trains to services efficiently using AI-powered recommendations with visible justifications.

**Covers:** FR-3.1 to FR-3.5, FR-7.1 to FR-7.6, NFR-1.1, NFR-4.1

**Primary Persona:** Diana (Dispatcher)

---

### Story 4.1: List Pending Services

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see all services needing train assignment**,
So that **I can prioritize my allocation work**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Allocation page loads
**When** data fetches from `/api/services/pending`
**Then** a list/table shows pending services with columns:
- Service Code (e.g., IC 501)
- Origin вҶ’ Destination
- Distance (km)
- Departure time
- Status (Unassigned/Assigned)
**And** list is sorted by departure time ascending
**And** I can filter by date using a date picker
**And** clicking a service selects it for recommendation

---

### Story 4.2: Generate Train Recommendations

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **the system to recommend suitable trains ranked by score**,
So that **I make better allocation decisions faster**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have selected a pending service
**When** I click "Recommend"
**Then** the system calls `/api/allocations/recommend`
**And** within 30 seconds (NFR-1.1), a ranked list of 5 trains appears
**And** each recommendation shows:
- Train ID
- Score (0-100)
- Km delta from average (e.g., "-12%")
- Dead running distance
**And** recommendations are sorted by score descending
**And** if solver times out, fallback simple ranking is shown with notice

---

### Story 4.3: Display Justifications

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to see why each train is recommended**,
So that **I trust the system's suggestions**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** recommendations are displayed
**When** I expand or hover a recommendation
**Then** I see 3+ justification reasons, such as:
- "Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)"
- "Service ends 5km from Porto depot"
- "PM1 scheduled in 3 days at destination depot"
- "Route compatible with train aptitude"
**And** each justification is a complete, human-readable sentence
**And** justifications use EN 15380 terminology

---

### Story 4.4: Confirm Allocation

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to assign a recommended train with one click**,
So that **I complete allocations efficiently**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I see the recommended trains list
**When** I click "Assign" on a recommendation
**Then** the system calls `POST /api/allocations`
**And** success toast shows "Train [ID] assigned to [Service]"
**And** the service moves from Pending to Assigned list
**And** the train's status updates on the Map
**And** entire flow completes in < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks (NFR-4.1)

---

### Story 4.5: Filter Trains by Criteria

As a **Dispatcher**,
I want **to filter available trains by fleet, depot, or km range**,
So that **I can find specific trains when needed**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Allocation page
**When** I open the filter panel
**Then** I can filter by:
- Fleet type (multi-select dropdown)
- Base depot (multi-select dropdown)
- Km range (min/max slider or inputs)
- Fitness status (fit only / show all)
**And** filters apply immediately to train list
**And** active filters are shown as chips that can be removed

---

### Story 4.6: Implement Solver Recommendation Engine

As a **system**,
I want **the Python solver to calculate optimal train assignments**,
So that **recommendations maximize mileage balancing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a `POST /recommend` request with service and candidates
**When** the OR-Tools CP-SAT solver runs
**Then** the response includes:
- Top 5 ranked trains by optimization score
- 3+ justification strings per train
- Dead running km calculation
- Solver execution time in ms
**And** solver respects constraints:
1. Exclude unfit trains (hard constraint)
2. Exclude trains with overlapping allocations (hard constraint)
3. Prioritize under-running trains (soft constraint, weight: high)
4. Minimize dead running (soft constraint, weight: medium)
5. Prefer trains ending near maintenance depot (soft constraint, weight: low)
**And** solver completes within 30 seconds or returns partial results

---

## Epic 5: Km Balancing & Simulation

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize fleet mileage distribution, identify imbalances, and simulate allocation strategies to optimize balancing.

**Covers:** FR-4.1 to FR-4.4, FR-5.1 to FR-5.5, NFR-1.4

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 5.1: Visualize Km Distribution

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see the km distribution across the fleet as a chart**,
So that **I understand the mileage spread**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** distribution data fetches from `/api/balancing/distribution`
**Then** a histogram or box plot displays showing:
- X-axis: km ranges (buckets)
- Y-axis: number of trains
- Fleet average line clearly marked
- Target range highlighted (Вұ10% of average)
**And** chart is interactive (hover shows train count per bucket)
**And** I can filter by fleet type

---

### Story 5.2: Rank Trains by Delta

As a **Planner**,
I want **a sorted list showing trains by km deviation**,
So that **I quickly identify over/under-running trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Analysis/Balancing page loads
**When** train ranking is displayed
**Then** a sortable table shows:
- Train ID
- Current km
- Fleet average km
- Delta (km and %)
- Status indicator (over/under/balanced)
**And** default sort is by absolute delta descending (worst first)
**And** clicking a train row shows details or navigates to train page
**And** top 5 over-running and top 5 under-running are highlighted

---

### Story 5.3: Identify Outliers

As a **Planner**,
I want **outlier trains visually highlighted**,
So that **I focus attention on problem trains**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the distribution chart is displayed
**When** trains deviate >15% from fleet average
**Then** their histogram bar or point is highlighted in distinct color
**And** outlier trains are listed in a separate "Outliers" panel
**And** each outlier shows delta and recommended action:
- Over-running: "Reduce high-km allocations"
- Under-running: "Prioritize for upcoming services"

---

### Story 5.4: Create What-If Scenario

As a **Planner**,
I want **to create a simulation scenario**,
So that **I can test allocation strategies without affecting live data**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am on the Simulation section
**When** I click "New Scenario"
**Then** a dialog asks for scenario name
**And** clicking "Create" calls `POST /api/simulation/create`
**And** a sandbox copy of current allocations is created
**And** I enter "scenario mode" with visual indicator
**And** changes in scenario mode don't affect production data

---

### Story 5.5: Modify and Run Simulation

As a **Planner**,
I want **to modify allocations in a scenario and see projected impact**,
So that **I can evaluate strategies before committing**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am in scenario mode
**When** I reassign trains to different services
**Then** changes are tracked in the scenario
**And** clicking "Run Simulation" calls `POST /api/simulation/:id/run`
**And** within 10 seconds (NFR-1.4), results show:
- Projected variance after 30 days
- Projected dead running total
- Change from current state (better/worse indicators)
**And** I can run multiple iterations with different changes

---

### Story 5.6: Compare Scenarios

As a **Planner**,
I want **to compare scenarios side-by-side**,
So that **I choose the best strategy**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I have created multiple scenarios
**When** I select 2-3 scenarios to compare
**Then** a comparison view shows:
- Metrics in columns (variance, dead running, cost impact)
- Best value highlighted green in each row
- Chart overlay showing distribution difference
**And** I can select a scenario and click "Apply to Live"
**And** applying scenario updates the production allocation plan

---

## Epic 6: Maintenance Gantt

**Goal:** Enable Paul (Planner) to visualize and schedule preventive maintenance using an interactive Gantt timeline with conflict validation.

**Covers:** FR-6.1 to FR-6.5, NFR-4.2

**Primary Persona:** Paul (Maintenance Planner)

---

### Story 6.1: Display Timeline View

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see maintenance schedules on a Gantt timeline**,
So that **I understand the maintenance calendar**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Schedule/Gantt page loads
**When** maintenance data fetches
**Then** a Gantt chart displays:
- Y-axis: Train IDs (rows)
- X-axis: 4-week timeline (days as columns)
- Horizontal scroll for extended view
- Today line clearly marked
**And** I can zoom in/out (week view, 2-week view, 4-week view)
**And** I can filter trains by fleet or depot

---

### Story 6.2: Show Maintenance Blocks

As a **Planner**,
I want **maintenance events as color-coded blocks**,
So that **I distinguish maintenance types**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt timeline is displayed
**When** trains have scheduled maintenance
**Then** blocks appear on the timeline:
- PM1: Light blue
- PM2: Dark blue
- HM: Red
- Overhaul: Purple
**And** block width corresponds to duration (days)
**And** hovering block shows:
- Maintenance type
- Scheduled date(s)
- Depot
- Km at maintenance
**And** legend shows color meanings

---

### Story 6.3: Enable Drag-Drop Scheduling

As a **Planner**,
I want **to drag maintenance blocks to different dates**,
So that **I can adjust schedules interactively**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt displays maintenance blocks
**When** I drag a maintenance block to a new date
**Then** the block moves visually following cursor
**And** drop zones highlight valid/invalid dates
**And** dropping on valid date updates the schedule
**And** system calls API to persist change
**And** undo option available for 10 seconds after change

---

### Story 6.4: Validate Constraints Real-Time

As a **Planner**,
I want **immediate feedback if a schedule change violates constraints**,
So that **I avoid creating invalid schedules**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** I am dragging a maintenance block
**When** I hover over a date that would cause conflict
**Then** the drop zone shows red indicator
**And** tooltip explains the conflict:
- "Depot at capacity on this date"
- "Outside maintenance window tolerance"
- "Overlaps with another maintenance"
**And** if I drop on invalid date, change is rejected with explanation
**And** valid changes show green confirmation

---

### Story 6.5: Show Depot Capacity per Week

As a **Planner**,
I want **to see aggregate depot capacity usage**,
So that **I avoid overloading maintenance facilities**.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the Gantt page loads
**When** I view the capacity panel
**Then** a heatmap or bar chart shows:
- Each depot as a row
- Each week as a column
- Color intensity indicates utilization (green < 70%, yellow 70-90%, red > 90%)
**And** clicking a cell shows trains scheduled at that depot that week
**And** if any depot/week is at 100%, warning icon appears

---

# NEW EPICS - Added via SCP-001 (2025-12-28)

## Epic 7: What-If Scenario Planning System

**Priority:** Critical
**Dependencies:** Epic 5 (Balancing foundation)
**Added:** SCP-001 - Visual testing revealed What-If is non-functional stub

### Description

Complete scenario planning system allowing planners to create sandbox environments, test different allocation strategies, simulate workforce changes, and compare outcomes before committing to production.

### Stories

#### Story 7.1: Scenario Sandbox Mode
**ID:** 7-1-scenario-sandbox-mode
**Priority:** P0

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** create a sandbox copy of current fleet state
**So that** I can experiment with changes without affecting live data

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] "Create Scenario" button clones current state
- [ ] Sandbox is visually distinguished (badge/border)
- [ ] Changes in sandbox don't affect live data
- [ ] Can discard sandbox anytime
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.2: Fleet Size Simulation
**ID:** 7-2-fleet-size-simulation
**Priority:** P1

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** add/remove trains from a scenario
**So that** I can see impact on km distribution and variance

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can add virtual trains to scenario
- [ ] Can remove trains from scenario
- [ ] KPIs update in real-time
- [ ] Impact on variance displayed
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.3: Workforce/Vacation Planning
**ID:** 7-3-workforce-vacation-planning
**Priority:** P1

**As a** maintenance manager
**I want to** simulate staff absences
**So that** I can test coverage during holidays

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can mark dates as reduced capacity
- [ ] Depot capacity reflects reductions
- [ ] Conflict warnings if over-scheduled
- [ ] Calendar visualization
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.4: Budget Allocation Scenarios
**ID:** 7-4-budget-allocation-scenarios
**Priority:** P2

**As a** fleet manager
**I want to** test different budget distributions
**So that** I can optimize maintenance spending

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Can set budget per depot/period
- [ ] Shows maintenance coverage at budget
- [ ] Highlights unfunded maintenance
- [ ] ROI estimates displayed
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.5: Scenario Comparison Dashboard
**ID:** 7-5-scenario-comparison-dashboard
**Priority:** P0

**As a** fleet planner
**I want to** compare 2-3 scenarios side-by-side
**So that** I can choose the best strategy

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Select multiple scenarios for comparison
- [ ] Side-by-side KPI display
- [ ] Best/worst values highlighted (green/red)
- [ ] Delta percentages shown
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 7.6: Apply Scenario to Live
**ID:** 7-6-apply-scenario-to-live
**Priority:** P1

**As a** fleet manager
**I want to** commit an approved scenario
**So that** changes become the new baseline

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] "Apply to Live" button with confirmation
- [ ] Audit trail of changes created
- [ ] Rollback capability within 24h
- [ ] Notification to stakeholders
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

## Epic 8: Visual Redesign & Canvas Migration

**Priority:** Critical
**Dependencies:** None (foundation work)
**Added:** SCP-001 - Visual testing revealed canvas broken and design inconsistent

### Description

Unify the design system with a single source of truth, fix all dark/light mode inconsistencies, and migrate from broken Pixi.js canvas to deck.gl with proper semantic zoom.

### Stories

#### Story 8.1: Unified Design Tokens
**ID:** 8-1-unified-design-tokens
**Priority:** P0

**As a** developer
**I want** a single source of truth for colors and spacing
**So that** all components are visually consistent

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] CSS variables defined in globals.css
- [ ] Tailwind config uses same palette
- [ ] All components use tokens, not hardcoded colors
- [ ] Design token documentation created
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification all pages**

---

#### Story 8.2: Typography and Terminology
**ID:** 8-2-typography-and-terminology
**Priority:** P1

**As a** user
**I want** consistent font sizes and railway terminology
**So that** the interface is professional

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Font scale defined (xs to 3xl)
- [ ] Railway terminology standardized (PM1/PM2/HM/OVH)
- [ ] Labels use correct terminology
- [ ] Typography documentation created
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 8.3: Component Dark Mode Fix
**ID:** 8-3-component-dark-mode-fix
**Priority:** P0

**As a** user
**I want** all components in dark mode
**So that** the interface is consistent

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] MaintenanceGantt.tsx uses dark background
- [ ] Layout.tsx uses dark background
- [ ] No white/light backgrounds anywhere
- [ ] All text has proper contrast
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright screenshots all pages**

---

#### Story 8.4: deck.gl Canvas Migration
**ID:** 8-4-deck-gl-canvas-migration
**Priority:** P0

**As a** developer
**I want to** replace Pixi.js with deck.gl
**So that** canvas rendering works properly

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Schedule canvas renders train blocks
- [ ] Pan and zoom work smoothly
- [ ] Performance acceptable (60fps)
- [ ] WebGL2 backend configured
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright visual verification**

---

#### Story 8.5: Semantic Zoom LOD
**ID:** 8-5-semantic-zoom-lod
**Priority:** P1

**As a** user
**I want** different detail levels at different zoom scales
**So that** I see appropriate information

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] MACRO level (6+ months): Fleet summary only
- [ ] MESO level (1-6 months): Individual trains, grouped events
- [ ] MICRO level (<1 month): Full detail with times
- [ ] Smooth transitions between levels
- [ ] **MUST include Playwright zoom interaction tests**

---

#### Story 8.6: Interaction Testing Suite
**ID:** 8-6-interaction-testing-suite
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want** automated interaction tests
**So that** all user actions are verified

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Pan tests (drag canvas)
- [ ] Zoom tests (scroll wheel, pinch)
- [ ] Click tests (all buttons, links)
- [ ] Drag-drop tests (schedule editing)
- [ ] Form input tests (what-if parameters)
- [ ] Tests run in CI pipeline

---

## Epic 9: Quality Assurance Sprint

**Priority:** High
**Dependencies:** None
**Added:** SCP-001 - Verify all existing stories were properly implemented

### Description

Systematic audit of all previously "completed" stories using Playwright MCP visual verification. Each story must have screenshot evidence proving it works.

### Stories

#### Story 9.1: Audit Epic 1 Stories
**ID:** 9-1-audit-epic-1-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 1 stories
**So that** foundation is confirmed working

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each story tested with Playwright
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each
- [ ] Test log created
- [ ] Issues documented

---

#### Story 9.2: Audit Epic 2 Stories
**ID:** 9-2-audit-epic-2-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 2 stories
**So that** Dashboard is confirmed working

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Each KPI verified on screen
- [ ] Drill-down navigation tested
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each
- [ ] Issues documented

---

#### Story 9.3: Audit Epic 3 Stories
**ID:** 9-3-audit-epic-3-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 3 stories
**So that** Map and Fleet pages work

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Map renders with markers
- [ ] Fleet page shows train list (NOT stub)
- [ ] Train selection works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.4: Audit Epic 4 Stories
**ID:** 9-4-audit-epic-4-stories
**Priority:** P1

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 4 stories
**So that** Allocation works

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Services show times (not "--:--")
- [ ] Recommendations appear when service selected
- [ ] Allocation confirmation works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.5: Audit Epic 5 Stories
**ID:** 9-5-audit-epic-5-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 5 stories
**So that** Balancing and What-If work

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Histogram renders correctly
- [ ] What-If creates actual scenarios
- [ ] Scenarios can be modified
- [ ] Comparison works
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

#### Story 9.6: Audit Epic 6 Stories
**ID:** 9-6-audit-epic-6-stories
**Priority:** P0

**As a** QA engineer
**I want to** verify all Epic 6 stories
**So that** Maintenance Gantt works

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Canvas shows maintenance blocks
- [ ] Drag-drop scheduling works
- [ ] Depot capacity accurate
- [ ] Screenshot evidence for each

---

## Appendix: Story Dependencies

```
Epic 1 (Foundation)
   в””в”Җв”Җ All other epics depend on Epic 1 completion

Epic 2 (Dashboard) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                        в”ңв”Җв”Җ Epic 4 (Allocation) uses train data
Epic 3 (Map) в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

   в””в”Җв”Җ Requires Solver (Story 4.6)

Epic 5 (Simulation)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Requires Balancing data (depends on Epic 4)

Epic 6 (Gantt)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Independent after Epic 1

# NEW DEPENDENCIES (SCP-001)

Epic 7 (What-If)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Requires Epic 5 foundation (Balancing KPIs)

Epic 8 (Visual Redesign)
   в””в”Җв”Җ Foundation work, no dependencies
   в””в”Җв”Җ Should be done before Epic 6 fixes (canvas)

Epic 9 (QA Audit)
   в””в”Җв”Җ No dependencies, runs in parallel
```

---

## Sprint Recommendation

    10вҶ’# UX Design Specification - MMS Metro Mileage Management System
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
    13вҶ’**Date:** 2025-12-28
    14вҶ’
    15вҶ’---
    16вҶ’
    17вҶ’## 1. Design Philosophy
    18вҶ’
    19вҶ’### 1.1 Core Principles
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’| Principle | Application |
    22вҶ’|-----------|-------------|
    23вҶ’| **Domain Authenticity** | Use railway terminology (EN 15380), not generic software terms |
    24вҶ’| **Decision Support** | Every screen helps users make better decisions faster |
    25вҶ’| **Visual Trust** | Show data, not just results - users understand WHY |
    26вҶ’| **Progressive Disclosure** | Summary first, details on demand |
    27вҶ’| **Operational Efficiency** | < 5 clicks for critical tasks |
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### 1.2 Design Mantra
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."
    32вҶ’
    33вҶ’---
    34вҶ’
    35вҶ’## 2. User Personas
    36вҶ’
    37вҶ’### 2.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner
    38вҶ’
    39вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    40вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    41вҶ’| **Age** | 45 years |
    42вҶ’| **Daily System Usage** | 4-8 hours |
    43вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop with large monitor |
    44вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Schedule maintenance, balance workload, manage depot capacity |
    45вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Excel spreadsheets, lack of visibility, constant rework |
    46вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality |
    47вҶ’
    48вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    49вҶ’- Large data tables with sorting and filtering
    50вҶ’- Gantt view as primary planning interface
    51вҶ’- Keyboard shortcuts for power users
    52вҶ’- Dense information display (comfort with complexity)
    53вҶ’
    54вҶ’### 2.2 Diana - The Dispatcher
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    57вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    58вҶ’| **Age** | 38 years |
    59вҶ’| **Daily System Usage** | Continuous during shift |
    60вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop with multiple monitors |
    61вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Allocate trains to services in real-time |
    62вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Slow systems, lack of information for quick decisions |
    63вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Response time < 3 seconds |
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    66вҶ’- Quick action panels, single-click confirmations
    67вҶ’- Map always visible on secondary monitor
    68вҶ’- Status at a glance (traffic light indicators)
    69вҶ’- Minimal navigation for common tasks
    70вҶ’
    71вҶ’### 2.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager
    72вҶ’
    73вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    74вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    75вҶ’| **Age** | 52 years |
    76вҶ’| **Weekly System Usage** | 30-60 minutes |
    77вҶ’| **Primary Device** | Desktop or tablet |
    78вҶ’| **Key Tasks** | Monitor KPIs, make strategic decisions |
    79вҶ’| **Pain Points** | Scattered data, lack of drill-down capability |
    80вҶ’| **Critical Need** | Dashboard with executive view |
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’**Design Implications:**
    83вҶ’- Large KPI cards, minimal text
    84вҶ’- Trend visualizations over time
    85вҶ’- Touch-friendly for tablet use
    86вҶ’- Export to PDF for presentations
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’---
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’## 3. Information Architecture
    91вҶ’

   119вҶ’```

# UX Design Specification - MMS Metro Mileage Management System

**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
**Date:** 2025-12-28

---

## 1. Design Philosophy

### 1.1 Core Principles

| Principle | Application |
|-----------|-------------|
| **Domain Authenticity** | Use railway terminology (EN 15380), not generic software terms |
| **Decision Support** | Every screen helps users make better decisions faster |
| **Visual Trust** | Show data, not just results - users understand WHY |
| **Progressive Disclosure** | Summary first, details on demand |
| **Operational Efficiency** | < 5 clicks for critical tasks |

### 1.2 Design Mantra

> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."

---

## 2. User Personas

### 2.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 45 years |
| **Daily System Usage** | 4-8 hours |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop with large monitor |
| **Key Tasks** | Schedule maintenance, balance workload, manage depot capacity |
| **Pain Points** | Excel spreadsheets, lack of visibility, constant rework |
| **Critical Need** | Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality |

**Design Implications:**
- Large data tables with sorting and filtering
- Gantt view as primary planning interface
- Keyboard shortcuts for power users
- Dense information display (comfort with complexity)

### 2.2 Diana - The Dispatcher

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 38 years |
| **Daily System Usage** | Continuous during shift |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop with multiple monitors |
| **Key Tasks** | Allocate trains to services in real-time |
| **Pain Points** | Slow systems, lack of information for quick decisions |
| **Critical Need** | Response time < 3 seconds |

**Design Implications:**
- Quick action panels, single-click confirmations
- Map always visible on secondary monitor
- Status at a glance (traffic light indicators)
- Minimal navigation for common tasks

### 2.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Age** | 52 years |
| **Weekly System Usage** | 30-60 minutes |
| **Primary Device** | Desktop or tablet |
| **Key Tasks** | Monitor KPIs, make strategic decisions |
| **Pain Points** | Scattered data, lack of drill-down capability |
| **Critical Need** | Dashboard with executive view |

**Design Implications:**
- Large KPI cards, minimal text
- Trend visualizations over time
- Touch-friendly for tablet use
- Export to PDF for presentations

**KPI Card Specification:**

| Element | Specification |
|---------|---------------|
| Size | 120px Г— 100px minimum |
| Primary Number | 48px, Bold |
| Label | 14px, Gray |
| Secondary | 12px, Color-coded |
| Click Action | Navigate to detail view |

**Color Coding (Traffic Light):**
- рҹҹў Green: Normal (Variance вүӨ 10%, Capacity вүӨ 70%)
- рҹҹЎ Yellow: Warning (Variance 10-20%, Capacity 70-90%)
- рҹ”ҙ Red: Alert (Variance > 20%, Capacity > 90%)

```

**Justification Card:**
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ Why UTE-3400-012?                       в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ вң“ Km balancing: 12% below fleet average в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ

в”Ӯ   Porto depot (base depot for this fleet)в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вң“ Maintenance: PM1 scheduled in 3 days  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   at Porto depot (available slot)       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ                                         в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ вң“ Aptitude: Confirmed for North line    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ   (max speed 220 km/h compatible)       в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ
в”Ӯ Score: 94/100                           в”Ӯ

в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

### 4.4 Balancing Analysis Screen

**Purpose:** Visualize km distribution, identify imbalances

**Layout:**

в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

1вҶ’/**
     2вҶ’ * MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
     3вҶ’ * Database Schema using Drizzle ORM
     4вҶ’ *
     5вҶ’ * Based on Architecture Decision Document (2025-12-28)
     6вҶ’ */
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’import {
     9вҶ’  pgTable,
    10вҶ’  uuid,
    11вҶ’  varchar,
    12вҶ’  integer,
    13вҶ’  decimal,
    14вҶ’  timestamp,
    15вҶ’  date,
    16вҶ’  time,
    17вҶ’  jsonb,
    18вҶ’  index,
    19вҶ’  uniqueIndex,
    20вҶ’} from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core';
    21вҶ’import { relations } from 'drizzle-orm';
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’// =============================================================================
    24вҶ’// Fleets Table
    25вҶ’// =============================================================================
    26вҶ’export const fleets = pgTable('fleets', {
    27вҶ’  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    28вҶ’  name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    29вҶ’  manufacturer: varchar('manufacturer', { length: 50 }),
    30вҶ’  pm1IntervalKm: integer('pm1_interval_km').notNull(),
    31вҶ’  pm2IntervalKm: integer('pm2_interval_km').notNull(),
    32вҶ’  hmIntervalKm: integer('hm_interval_km').notNull(),
    33вҶ’  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
    34вҶ’});
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’// =============================================================================
    37вҶ’// Depots Table
    38вҶ’// =============================================================================
    39вҶ’export const depots = pgTable(
    40вҶ’  'depots',
    41вҶ’  {
    42вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    43вҶ’    name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    44вҶ’    code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull(),
    45вҶ’    latitude: decimal('latitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }),
    46вҶ’    longitude: decimal('longitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }),
    47вҶ’    capacityTracks: integer('capacity_tracks').default(5),
    48вҶ’    capacityManhours: integer('capacity_manhours').default(80),
    49вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
    50вҶ’  },
    51вҶ’  (table) => [
    52вҶ’    uniqueIndex('depots_code_idx').on(table.code),
    53вҶ’  ]
    54вҶ’);
    55вҶ’
    56вҶ’// =============================================================================
    57вҶ’// Trains Table
    58вҶ’// =============================================================================
    59вҶ’export const trains = pgTable(
    60вҶ’  'trains',
    61вҶ’  {
    62вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    63вҶ’    trainId: varchar('train_id', { length: 20 }).notNull(), // Business ID: UTE-3400-012
    64вҶ’    fleetId: uuid('fleet_id').references(() => fleets.id),
    65вҶ’    baseDepotId: uuid('base_depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
    66вҶ’    currentKm: integer('current_km').default(0),
    67вҶ’    targetKm: integer('target_km').default(0),
    68вҶ’    fitnessStatus: varchar('fitness_status', { length: 10 }).default('fit'),
    69вҶ’    lastMaintenanceDate: date('last_maintenance_date'),
    70вҶ’    nextMaintenanceKm: integer('next_maintenance_km'),
    71вҶ’    latitude: decimal('latitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }),
    72вҶ’    longitude: decimal('longitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }),
    73вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
    74вҶ’    updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow(),
    75вҶ’  },
    76вҶ’  (table) => [
    77вҶ’    uniqueIndex('trains_train_id_idx').on(table.trainId),
    78вҶ’    index('trains_fleet_idx').on(table.fleetId),
    79вҶ’    index('trains_depot_idx').on(table.baseDepotId),
    80вҶ’  ]
    81вҶ’);
    82вҶ’
    83вҶ’// =============================================================================
    84вҶ’// Services Table
    85вҶ’// =============================================================================
    86вҶ’export const services = pgTable(
    87вҶ’  'services',
    88вҶ’  {
    89вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
    90вҶ’    serviceCode: varchar('service_code', { length: 20 }).notNull(), // IC 501
    91вҶ’    origin: varchar('origin', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    92вҶ’    destination: varchar('destination', { length: 100 }).notNull(),
    93вҶ’    distanceKm: integer('distance_km').notNull(),
    94вҶ’    departureTime: time('departure_time').notNull(),
    95вҶ’    arrivalTime: time('arrival_time').notNull(),
    96вҶ’    serviceType: varchar('service_type', { length: 20 }).default('regional'),
    97вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
    98вҶ’  },
    99вҶ’  (table) => [
   100вҶ’    uniqueIndex('services_code_idx').on(table.serviceCode),
   101вҶ’  ]
   102вҶ’);
   103вҶ’
   104вҶ’// =============================================================================
   105вҶ’// Allocations Table
   106вҶ’// =============================================================================
   107вҶ’export const allocations = pgTable(
   108вҶ’  'allocations',
   109вҶ’  {
   110вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   111вҶ’    serviceId: uuid('service_id').references(() => services.id),
   112вҶ’    trainId: uuid('train_id').references(() => trains.id),
   113вҶ’    allocationDate: date('allocation_date').notNull(),
   114вҶ’    status: varchar('status', { length: 20 }).default('pending'),
   115вҶ’    score: integer('score'),
   116вҶ’    justifications: jsonb('justifications').$type<string[]>(),
   117вҶ’    createdBy: varchar('created_by', { length: 100 }),
   118вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
   119вҶ’  },
   120вҶ’  (table) => [
   121вҶ’    uniqueIndex('allocations_service_date_idx').on(table.serviceId, table.allocationDate),
   122вҶ’    index('allocations_date_idx').on(table.allocationDate),
   123вҶ’    index('allocations_status_idx').on(table.status),
   124вҶ’  ]
   125вҶ’);
   126вҶ’
   127вҶ’// =============================================================================
   128вҶ’// Mileage History Table
   129вҶ’// =============================================================================
   130вҶ’export const mileageHistory = pgTable(
   131вҶ’  'mileage_history',
   132вҶ’  {
   133вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   134вҶ’    trainId: uuid('train_id').references(() => trains.id),
   135вҶ’    recordedDate: date('recorded_date').notNull(),
   136вҶ’    kmValue: integer('km_value').notNull(),
   137вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
   138вҶ’  },
   139вҶ’  (table) => [
   140вҶ’    index('mileage_train_date_idx').on(table.trainId, table.recordedDate),
   141вҶ’  ]
   142вҶ’);
   143вҶ’
   144вҶ’// =============================================================================
   145вҶ’// Maintenance Schedules Table
   146вҶ’// =============================================================================
   147вҶ’export const maintenanceSchedules = pgTable(
   148вҶ’  'maintenance_schedules',
   149вҶ’  {
   150вҶ’    id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
   151вҶ’    trainId: uuid('train_id').references(() => trains.id),
   152вҶ’    depotId: uuid('depot_id').references(() => depots.id),
   153вҶ’    maintenanceType: varchar('maintenance_type', { length: 10 }).notNull(), // PM1, PM2, HM, OVH
   154вҶ’    scheduledDate: date('scheduled_date').notNull(),
   155вҶ’    durationDays: integer('duration_days').default(1),
   156вҶ’    status: varchar('status', { length: 20 }).default('scheduled'),
   157вҶ’    createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
   158вҶ’  },
   159вҶ’  (table) => [
   160вҶ’    index('maintenance_train_idx').on(table.trainId),
   161вҶ’    index('maintenance_depot_idx').on(table.depotId),
   162вҶ’    index('maintenance_date_idx').on(table.scheduledDate),
   163вҶ’  ]
   164вҶ’);
   165вҶ’
   166вҶ’// =============================================================================
   167вҶ’// Relations
   168вҶ’// =============================================================================
   169вҶ’
   170вҶ’export const fleetsRelations = relations(fleets, ({ many }) => ({
   171вҶ’  trains: many(trains),
   172вҶ’}));
   173вҶ’
   174вҶ’export const depotsRelations = relations(depots, ({ many }) => ({
   175вҶ’  trains: many(trains),
   176вҶ’  maintenanceSchedules: many(maintenanceSchedules),
   177вҶ’}));
   178вҶ’
   179вҶ’export const trainsRelations = relations(trains, ({ one, many }) => ({
   180вҶ’  fleet: one(fleets, {
   181вҶ’    fields: [trains.fleetId],
   182вҶ’    references: [fleets.id],
   183вҶ’  }),
   184вҶ’  baseDepot: one(depots, {
   185вҶ’    fields: [trains.baseDepotId],
   186вҶ’    references: [depots.id],
   187вҶ’  }),
   188вҶ’  allocations: many(allocations),
   189вҶ’  mileageHistory: many(mileageHistory),
   190вҶ’  maintenanceSchedules: many(maintenanceSchedules),
   191вҶ’}));
   192вҶ’
   193вҶ’export const servicesRelations = relations(services, ({ many }) => ({
   194вҶ’  allocations: many(allocations),
   195вҶ’}));
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’export const allocationsRelations = relations(allocations, ({ one }) => ({
   198вҶ’  service: one(services, {
   199вҶ’    fields: [allocations.serviceId],
   200вҶ’    references: [services.id],
   201вҶ’  }),
   202вҶ’  train: one(trains, {
   203вҶ’    fields: [allocations.trainId],
   204вҶ’    references: [trains.id],
   205вҶ’  }),
   206вҶ’}));
   207вҶ’
   208вҶ’export const mileageHistoryRelations = relations(mileageHistory, ({ one }) => ({
   209вҶ’  train: one(trains, {
   210вҶ’    fields: [mileageHistory.trainId],
   211вҶ’    references: [trains.id],
   212вҶ’  }),
   213вҶ’}));
   214вҶ’
   215вҶ’export const maintenanceSchedulesRelations = relations(maintenanceSchedules, ({ one }) => ({
   216вҶ’  train: one(trains, {
   217вҶ’    fields: [maintenanceSchedules.trainId],
   218вҶ’    references: [trains.id],
   219вҶ’  }),
   220вҶ’  depot: one(depots, {
   221вҶ’    fields: [maintenanceSchedules.depotId],
   222вҶ’    references: [depots.id],
   223вҶ’  }),
   224вҶ’}));
   225вҶ’

input.content:
# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
## Complete Product & Technical Specification

**Version:** 2.0.0
**Date:** 2026-01-05
**Status:** Definitive Specification for Rebuild
**Audience:** UX/Product Team, Development Team, Stakeholders

---

## Document Purpose

This document serves as the **Single Source of Truth** for rebuilding the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) from scratch. It consolidates all requirements, architecture decisions, UX specifications, and lessons learned from the initial Claude Code implementation.

The original implementation encountered complexity issues that resulted in incomplete features and inconsistent behavior. This specification provides a complete, unambiguous reference for human teams to deliver a production-ready demo.

---

## Table of Contents

### Part I: Business Context & Strategy

#### 1. Executive Summary
- 1.1 Project Overview
- 1.2 Business Objective
- 1.3 Success Metrics
- 1.4 Timeline & Constraints

> **Covers:** High-level project goals, the competitive bid context, what success looks like, and the 12-week timeline with key milestones.

#### 2. Railway Domain Fundamentals
- 2.1 Essential Glossary
- 2.2 Business Entities & Relationships
- 2.3 Train Lifecycle & Maintenance Hierarchy
- 2.4 Critical Domain Rules (Unknown Unknowns)
- 2.5 What the System Does NOT Do

> **Covers:** Railway-specific terminology (EN 15380), entity relationships, maintenance intervals (PM1/PM2/HM), and domain constraints that are critical for credibility.

#### 3. Market Analysis & Competitive Positioning
- 3.1 Competitor Landscape
- 3.2 SignatureRail Deep Analysis
- 3.3 Market Gaps & Opportunities
- 3.4 MMS Differentiators

> **Covers:** Analysis of DELMIA Quintiq, Tracsis, Railnova, SignatureRail; identified gaps in the market; and how MMS positions itself.

#### 4. Demo Strategy & Presentation
- 4.1 Core Principle: Depth Over Breadth
- 4.2 Wow Moments
- 4.3 Credibility Checklist
- 4.4 Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- 4.5 Presentation Script (15 minutes)
- 4.6 FAQ & Fallback Plans

> **Covers:** How to win the bid through impressive demo, key moments that create impact, presentation flow, and contingency plans.

---

### Part II: User Experience & Design

#### 5. User Personas
- 5.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner
- 5.2 Diana - The Dispatcher
- 5.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager
- 5.4 Design Implications per Persona

> **Covers:** Detailed persona profiles including goals, frustrations, device preferences, and how each persona influences UI decisions.

#### 6. User Journeys
- 6.1 Journey 1: Allocating a Train to Service (Diana)
- 6.2 Journey 2: Identifying and Resolving Imbalance (Paul)
- 6.3 Journey 3: Planning Maintenance Schedule (Paul)
- 6.4 Journey 4: Executive Review (Gabriel)
- 6.5 Journey Requirements Matrix

> **Covers:** Step-by-step user flows with scenes, expected times, click counts, and requirements revealed by each journey.

#### 7. Information Architecture
- 7.1 Navigation Structure
- 7.2 Drill-Down Hierarchy
- 7.3 Screen Inventory
- 7.4 Component Relationships

> **Covers:** How screens connect, navigation patterns, progressive disclosure hierarchy, and mental model alignment.

#### 8. Visual Design System
- 8.1 Design Principles
- 8.2 Color Palette & Semantic Colors
- 8.3 Typography Scale
- 8.4 Spacing & Layout Grid
- 8.5 Component Library (Blueprint.js v6)
- 8.6 Dark Mode Specification
- 8.7 Railway-Specific Visual Patterns

> **Covers:** Complete design token system, Blueprint.js integration, dark mode requirements, and enterprise UI patterns.

#### 9. Screen Specifications
- 9.1 Dashboard (Home)
- 9.2 Map View
- 9.3 Allocation Screen
- 9.4 Balancing Analysis
- 9.5 Simulation / What-If
- 9.6 Maintenance Gantt
- 9.7 Fleet List
- 9.8 Train Details

> **Covers:** Wireframes, component placement, data requirements, interactions, and acceptance criteria for each screen.

---

### Part III: Functional Requirements

#### 10. Requirements Overview
- 10.1 Functional Requirements Matrix
- 10.2 Non-Functional Requirements
- 10.3 Priority Classification (P0/P1/P2)
- 10.4 Scope Boundaries

> **Covers:** Complete FR/NFR tables with priorities, what's in scope vs explicitly out of scope.

#### 11. Module: Dashboard & KPIs
- 11.1 KPI Cards Specification
- 11.2 Traffic-Light Indicators
- 11.3 Depot Capacity Visualization
- 11.4 Maintenance Alerts
- 11.5 Drill-Down Navigation

> **Covers:** Detailed specs for the dashboard module with acceptance criteria and edge cases.

#### 12. Module: Map & Fleet Visualization
- 12.1 Map Provider & Configuration
- 12.2 Train Markers & Status Colors
- 12.3 Depot Markers & Capacity Indicators
- 12.4 Train Selection & Popup Cards
- 12.5 Auto-Refresh Mechanism
- 12.6 Clustering Behavior

> **Covers:** Map implementation details, marker specifications, and real-time update requirements.

#### 13. Module: Allocation with Recommendations
- 13.1 Service List & Filtering
- 13.2 Recommendation Engine Integration
- 13.3 Justification Display
- 13.4 One-Click Allocation
- 13.5 Train Filtering Criteria

> **Covers:** The core allocation workflow, solver integration, and justification presentation.

#### 14. Module: Km Balancing & Simulation
- 14.1 Distribution Visualization (Histogram)
- 14.2 Train Ranking by Delta
- 14.3 Outlier Identification
- 14.4 Scenario Creation
- 14.5 Simulation Execution
- 14.6 Scenario Comparison

> **Covers:** Balancing analytics, what-if simulation system, and scenario management.

#### 15. Module: Maintenance Gantt
- 15.1 Timeline View Options
- 15.2 Maintenance Blocks (Color-Coded)
- 15.3 Drag-Drop Scheduling
- 15.4 Constraint Validation
- 15.5 Depot Capacity Heatmap

> **Covers:** Gantt chart implementation, drag-drop interactions, and conflict detection.

---

### Part IV: Technical Architecture

#### 16. System Architecture
- 16.1 High-Level Architecture Diagram
- 16.2 Technology Stack
- 16.3 Monorepo Structure
- 16.4 Service Communication
- 16.5 Key Architectural Decisions

> **Covers:** Overall system design, technology choices with rationale, and project structure.

#### 17. Frontend Architecture
- 17.1 Next.js 14 App Router Structure
- 17.2 Component Organization
- 17.3 State Management (@zazcart/toolkit)
- 17.4 API Client Pattern
- 17.5 Blueprint.js Integration
- 17.6 Styling Strategy

> **Covers:** Frontend technical details, component patterns, and state management approach.

#### 18. Backend Architecture
- 18.1 Fastify Server Structure
- 18.2 Route Organization
- 18.3 Service Layer Pattern
- 18.4 Database Access (Drizzle ORM)
- 18.5 Caching Strategy (Redis)
- 18.6 Error Handling

> **Covers:** Backend implementation patterns, API design, and data access layer.

#### 19. Database Design
- 19.1 Entity-Relationship Diagram
- 19.2 Schema Definition (All Tables)
- 19.3 Index Strategy
- 19.4 Data Types & Constraints
- 19.5 Seed Data Requirements

> **Covers:** Complete database schema, relationships, indexes, and realistic seed data specifications.

#### 20. Solver Architecture (Python/OR-Tools)
- 20.1 FastAPI Service Structure
- 20.2 CP-SAT Optimization Model
- 20.3 Objective Function
- 20.4 Constraints Definition
- 20.5 Justification Generation
- 20.6 Fallback Strategy

> **Covers:** Python solver implementation, OR-Tools model, and recommendation engine logic.

#### 21. API Specification
- 21.1 REST Endpoints Inventory
- 21.2 Request/Response Schemas
- 21.3 Error Response Format
- 21.4 Authentication (Demo Scope)

> **Covers:** Complete API documentation with all endpoints, schemas, and examples.

---

### Part V: Implementation Guide

#### 22. Epic & Story Breakdown
- 22.1 Epic Overview
- 22.2 Epic 1: Project Foundation
- 22.3 Epic 2: Dashboard & KPIs
- 22.4 Epic 3: Map & Fleet Visualization
- 22.5 Epic 4: Allocation with Recommendations
- 22.6 Epic 5: Km Balancing & Simulation
- 22.7 Epic 6: Maintenance Gantt
- 22.8 Story Dependencies

> **Covers:** Complete breakdown of work into implementable stories with acceptance criteria.

#### 23. Development Setup
- 23.1 Prerequisites
- 23.2 Environment Variables
- 23.3 Docker Compose Configuration
- 23.4 Database Setup & Seeding
- 23.5 Running the Development Environment

> **Covers:** Step-by-step setup instructions for new developers.

#### 24. Testing Strategy
- 24.1 Unit Testing (Vitest)
- 24.2 E2E Testing (Playwright)
- 24.3 Visual Testing Requirements
- 24.4 Test Coverage Goals

> **Covers:** Testing approach, tools, and mandatory verification requirements.

#### 25. Deployment & Demo Environment
- 25.1 Demo Laptop Setup
- 25.2 Docker Compose Production
- 25.3 Demo Reset Script
- 25.4 Fallback Screens

> **Covers:** How to deploy for demo, reset procedures, and contingency materials.

---

### Part VI: Lessons Learned & Recovery

#### 26. Original Implementation Issues
- 26.1 What Went Wrong
- 26.2 Broken Features Analysis
- 26.3 Technical Debt Accumulated
- 26.4 Root Cause Analysis

> **Covers:** Honest assessment of Claude Code implementation issues to avoid repeating mistakes.

#### 27. Recovery Recommendations
- 27.1 Recommended Rebuild Approach
- 27.2 Priority Order for Features
- 27.3 Risk Mitigation Strategies
- 27.4 Quality Gates

> **Covers:** Specific recommendations for the human team rebuild based on lessons learned.

---

### Appendices

#### Appendix A: Complete Glossary
> Railway terminology reference with EN 15380 compliance

#### Appendix B: Competitor Screenshots Analysis
> Visual analysis of SignatureRail, Tracsis, Railnova, DELMIA Quintiq

#### Appendix C: Seed Data Samples
> Example JSON/SQL for realistic demo data

#### Appendix D: API Response Examples
> Complete request/response samples for all endpoints

#### Appendix E: Component Props Reference
> Blueprint.js component usage patterns

#### Appendix F: Presentation Materials
> Demo script, FAQ, fallback slides

---

## Document Conventions

| Convention | Meaning |
|------------|---------|
| **P0** | Must Have - Required for demo |
| **P1** | Should Have - Enhances demo |
| **P2** | Could Have - Nice to have |
| `monospace` | Code, file names, commands |
| > Quote block | Important callouts |
| вҡ пёҸ | Warning or critical note |
| вң… | Completed or verified |
| вқҢ | Failed or broken |

---

## Version History

| Version | Date | Author | Changes |
|---------|------|--------|---------|
| 1.0.0 | 2025-12-28 | BMAD/Claude Code | Initial BMAD artifacts |
| 2.0.0 | 2026-01-05 | Consolidation | Complete specification for rebuild |

---

*End of Table of Contents - Chapters follow below*

---
