**Shared Components:**

| Component | Used In | Data Source |
|-----------|---------|-------------|
| TrainCard | Map, Allocation, Analysis | `/api/trains/:id` |
| DepotCard | Dashboard, Schedule | `/api/depots` |
| KPICard | Dashboard | `/api/dashboard/summary` |
| DataTable | Fleet, Allocation | Various |
| Chart (Histogram) | Analysis | `/api/balancing/distribution` |
| Chart (Timeline) | Train Details | `/api/trains/:id/history` |
| GanttBlock | Schedule | `/api/maintenance` |

## Chapter 8: Visual Design System

### 8.1 Design Principles

| Principle | Application | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| Domain Authenticity | Use railway terminology everywhere | "Train" not "Vehicle" |
| Decision Support | Every screen helps make better decisions | Justifications on recommendations |
| Visual Trust | Show data, not just conclusions | Distribution chart, not just "bad variance" |
| Progressive Disclosure | Summary first, details on demand | KPI вҶ’ Drill-down |
| Operational Efficiency | Minimize clicks for common tasks | < 5 clicks for allocation |

**Design Mantra:**

> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."

### 8.2 Color Palette & Semantic Colors

**Brand Colors:**

| Name | Hex | Usage |
|------|-----|-------|
| Primary | `#2563EB` | Interactive elements, primary buttons |
| Primary Dark | `#1D4ED8` | Hover states |
| Primary Light | `#3B82F6` | Highlights, links |

**Semantic Colors (Status):**

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| Available / Success | Green | `#22C55E` | Fit trains, successful actions |
| In-Service / Info | Blue | `#3B82F6` | Active trains, informational |
| Warning | Amber | `#F59E0B` | Variance 10-20%, approaching limits |
| Maintenance / Danger | Red | `#EF4444` | Unfit trains, exceeded limits |
| Neutral | Gray | `#6B7280` | Disabled, secondary info |

**Maintenance Type Colors:**

| Type | Color | Hex |
|------|-------|-----|
| PM1 | Light Blue | `#93C5FD` |
| PM2 | Dark Blue | `#3B82F6` |
| HM | Red | `#EF4444` |
| OVH | Purple | `#A855F7` |

**Background Colors (Dark Mode):**

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Page Background | Dark Gray | `#1F2937` |
| Card Background | Slightly Lighter | `#374151` |
| Panel Background | Darker | `#111827` |
| Border | Subtle | `#4B5563` |

### 8.3 Typography Scale

**Font Family:** Inter (primary), system-ui (fallback)

| Scale | Size | Weight | Line Height | Usage |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|-------|
| xs | 12px | 400 | 1.5 | Labels, metadata |
| sm | 14px | 400 | 1.5 | Body text, table cells |
| base | 16px | 400 | 1.5 | Default body |
| lg | 18px | 500 | 1.4 | Subheadings |
| xl | 20px | 600 | 1.3 | Card titles |
| 2xl | 24px | 600 | 1.2 | Page headings |
| 3xl | 30px | 700 | 1.1 | Dashboard KPI numbers |
| 4xl | 48px | 700 | 1.0 | Hero numbers |

**Monospace:** For train IDs, codes, technical values
- Font: `JetBrains Mono` or `Fira Code`
- Example: `UTE-3400-012`, `45,230 km`

### 8.4 Spacing & Layout Grid

**Spacing Scale (8px base):**

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| space-1 | 4px | Tight spacing within components |
| space-2 | 8px | Default component padding |
| space-3 | 12px | Between related elements |
| space-4 | 16px | Between components |
| space-6 | 24px | Section spacing |
| space-8 | 32px | Page sections |
| space-12 | 48px | Major sections |

**Layout Grid:**

- Container max-width: 1440px
- Gutter: 24px
- Columns: 12
- Responsive breakpoints:
  - sm: 640px
  - md: 768px
  - lg: 1024px
  - xl: 1280px
  - 2xl: 1536px

### 8.5 Component Library

**Recommended: Blueprint.js v5+**

Blueprint.js is chosen for its:
- Enterprise-ready components
- Dark mode support out of box
- Data-dense table components
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Consistent with industrial applications

**Core Components Used:**

| Component | Blueprint Name | Usage |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Button | Button | Actions, confirmations |
| Card | Card, Elevation | Content containers |
| Table | HTMLTable, Table2 | Data grids |
| Select | Select, MultiSelect | Dropdowns, filters |
| DatePicker | DatePicker, DateRangeInput | Date selection |
| Dialog | Dialog, Alert | Modals, confirmations |
| Toast | Toaster, Toast | Notifications |
| Menu | Menu, MenuItem | Context menus |
| Tabs | Tabs, Tab | Page sections |
| Tag | Tag | Status indicators |
| ProgressBar | ProgressBar | Capacity indicators |
| Spinner | Spinner | Loading states |

### 8.6 Dark Mode Specification

**Requirement:** Application MUST be dark mode by default.

**Rationale:**
- Operators work long shifts (8+ hours)
- Control room environments are often dimly lit
- Reduces eye strain
- Professional appearance

**Implementation:**

```css
:root {
  --bg-primary: #1F2937;
  --bg-secondary: #374151;
  --bg-tertiary: #111827;
  --text-primary: #F9FAFB;
  --text-secondary: #D1D5DB;
  --text-muted: #9CA3AF;
  --border-color: #4B5563;
}
```

**Rules:**
1. NO white backgrounds anywhere
2. All text must meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1)
3. Status colors must be visible on dark backgrounds
4. Charts must use dark theme

**Components:**

| Component | Props | Data Source |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| KPICard | title, value, subtitle, status, onClick | `/api/dashboard/summary` |
| FleetMap | trains[], depots[], onTrainClick | `/api/trains`, `/api/depots` |
| MaintenanceAlerts | upcoming[] | `/api/dashboard/alerts` |
| DepotCapacityCards | depots[] | `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity` |

**KPI Card Specification:**

| Card | Value | Subtitle | Status Logic |
|------|-------|----------|--------------|
| Total Trains | count | - | Always neutral |
| Available | count | percentage | Green if >80%, Yellow if 70-80%, Red if <70% |
| In Maintenance | count | - | Always neutral |
| Km Variance | percentage | target | Green if вүӨ10%, Yellow if 10-20%, Red if >20% |

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Page loads in < 3 seconds
- [ ] All 4 KPI cards display correct values
- [ ] Clicking any KPI navigates to appropriate detail screen
- [ ] Map shows train markers with correct status colors
- [ ] Clicking train marker shows popup with train info
- [ ] Depot capacity bars show correct percentages
- [ ] Upcoming maintenance shows events for next 7 days

**Drag-Drop Behavior:**

1. User clicks and holds a maintenance block
2. Block becomes draggable, cursor changes
3. Drop zones highlight: green (valid) / red (invalid)
4. On drop, system validates constraints
5. If valid: block moves, toast confirms
6. If invalid: block returns, error explains why

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Gantt displays 4 weeks by default
- [ ] Trains grouped by fleet
- [ ] Maintenance blocks are color-coded by type
- [ ] Hovering block shows details tooltip
- [ ] Drag-drop allows rescheduling
- [ ] Constraints validated in real-time during drag
- [ ] Depot capacity heatmap shows utilization
- [ ] Cells > 90% capacity show warning

   990вҶ’### 5.4 Design Implications per Persona

  1027вҶ’## Chapter 6: User Journeys
  1028вҶ’
  1029вҶ’### 6.1 Journey 1: Allocating a Train to Service (Diana)
  1030вҶ’
  1031вҶ’**Context:**
  1032вҶ’Diana arrives at the operations center for her morning shift. The timetable shows that service IC 501 (Lisbon to Porto, 336 km) needs a train assigned for tomorrow's departure.
  1033вҶ’
  1034вҶ’**Scene 1: The Request**
  1035вҶ’
  1036вҶ’Diana opens MMS and navigates to the Allocation screen. She selects tomorrow's date using the date picker.
  1037вҶ’
  1038вҶ’*Screen State:*
  1039вҶ’- Left panel: List of pending services for selected date
  1040вҶ’- IC 501 highlighted: "Lisbon вҶ’ Porto | 336 km | Dep: 08:15"
  1041вҶ’- Status: Unassigned (red indicator)
  1042вҶ’
  1043вҶ’**Scene 2: Service Selection**
  1044вҶ’
  1045вҶ’Diana clicks on IC 501. The service details panel expands showing:
  1046вҶ’- Route: Lisbon Santa ApolГіnia вҶ’ Porto CampanhГЈ
  1047вҶ’- Distance: 336 km
  1048вҶ’- Departure: 08:15
  1049вҶ’- Arrival: 11:45
  1050вҶ’- Required aptitude: North Line (220 km/h capable)
  1051вҶ’
  1052вҶ’*Action:* Diana clicks "Get Recommendations"
  1053вҶ’
  1054вҶ’**Scene 3: The Recommendation**

  1106вҶ’- NFR-4.1: < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks

  1367вҶ’3. "87% availability is on target"
  1368вҶ’4. "14 trains in maintenance today is normal for a Monday"
  1369вҶ’
  1370вҶ’**Journey Metrics:**
  1371вҶ’- Total time: < 5 minutes
  1372вҶ’- Screens viewed: 1 (with drill-downs)
  1373вҶ’- Data points absorbed: 8+
  1374вҶ’- Confidence for meeting: High
  1375вҶ’
  1376вҶ’**Requirements Revealed:**
  1377вҶ’- FR-1.1: Display fleet summary KPIs
  1378вҶ’- FR-1.2: Show variance indicator
  1379вҶ’- FR-1.3: Display depot capacity
  1380вҶ’- FR-1.5: Enable drill-down navigation
  1381вҶ’- NFR-1.2: < 3 seconds screen loading
  1382вҶ’
  1383вҶ’### 6.5 Journey Requirements Matrix
  1384вҶ’
  1385вҶ’| Journey | Persona | Primary Module | Key Capabilities | Time | Clicks |
  1386вҶ’|---------|---------|----------------|------------------|------|--------|
  1387вҶ’| Allocate Train | Diana | Allocation | Recommendations, Justifications | < 2 min | 4 |
  1388вҶ’| Resolve Imbalance | Paul | Balancing + Simulation | Distribution chart, What-if | < 10 min | 8 |
  1389вҶ’| Plan Maintenance | Paul | Gantt | Drag-drop, Validation | < 5 min | 3 |
  1390вҶ’| Executive Review | Gabriel | Dashboard | KPIs, Drill-down | < 5 min | 2 |
  1391вҶ’
  1392вҶ’**Critical Capabilities Identified:**
  1393вҶ’
  1394вҶ’1. **Recommendation Engine** - Ranked train suggestions with justifications
  1395вҶ’2. **Km Balancing Visualization** - Distribution charts, variance indicators
  1396вҶ’3. **Scenario Simulation** - What-if testing with side-by-side comparison
  1397вҶ’4. **Maintenance Gantt** - Timeline view with drag-drop scheduling
  1398вҶ’5. **Dashboard KPIs** - Traffic-light status, trend visualization
  1399вҶ’6. **Drill-Down Navigation** - Fleet вҶ’ Train вҶ’ Component вҶ’ History
  1400вҶ’7. **Real-Time Validation** - Instant feedback on changes

  1538вҶ’## Chapter 8: Visual Design System
  1539вҶ’
  1540вҶ’### 8.1 Design Principles
  1541вҶ’
  1542вҶ’| Principle | Application | Example |
  1543вҶ’|-----------|-------------|---------|
  1544вҶ’| Domain Authenticity | Use railway terminology everywhere | "Train" not "Vehicle" |
  1545вҶ’| Decision Support | Every screen helps make better decisions | Justifications on recommendations |
  1546вҶ’| Visual Trust | Show data, not just conclusions | Distribution chart, not just "bad variance" |
  1547вҶ’| Progressive Disclosure | Summary first, details on demand | KPI вҶ’ Drill-down |
  1548вҶ’| Operational Efficiency | Minimize clicks for common tasks | < 5 clicks for allocation |
  1549вҶ’
  1550вҶ’**Design Mantra:**
  1551вҶ’
  1552вҶ’> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."
  1553вҶ’

  1637вҶ’### 8.5 Component Library
  1638вҶ’
  1639вҶ’**Recommended: Blueprint.js v5+**
  1640вҶ’
  1641вҶ’Blueprint.js is chosen for its:
  1642вҶ’- Enterprise-ready components
  1643вҶ’- Dark mode support out of box
  1644вҶ’- Data-dense table components
  1645вҶ’- Excellent TypeScript support
  1646вҶ’- Consistent with industrial applications
  1647вҶ’
  1648вҶ’**Core Components Used:**
  1649вҶ’
  1650вҶ’| Component | Blueprint Name | Usage |
  1651вҶ’|-----------|---------------|-------|
  1652вҶ’| Button | Button | Actions, confirmations |
  1653вҶ’| Card | Card, Elevation | Content containers |
  1654вҶ’| Table | HTMLTable, Table2 | Data grids |
  1655вҶ’| Select | Select, MultiSelect | Dropdowns, filters |
  1656вҶ’| DatePicker | DatePicker, DateRangeInput | Date selection |
  1657вҶ’| Dialog | Dialog, Alert | Modals, confirmations |
  1658вҶ’| Toast | Toaster, Toast | Notifications |
  1659вҶ’| Menu | Menu, MenuItem | Context menus |
  1660вҶ’| Tabs | Tabs, Tab | Page sections |
  1661вҶ’| Tag | Tag | Status indicators |
  1662вҶ’| ProgressBar | ProgressBar | Capacity indicators |
  1663вҶ’| Spinner | Spinner | Loading states |
  1664вҶ’
  1665вҶ’### 8.6 Dark Mode Specification
  1666вҶ’
  1667вҶ’**Requirement:** Application MUST be dark mode by default.
  1668вҶ’
  1669вҶ’**Rationale:**
  1670вҶ’- Operators work long shifts (8+ hours)
  1671вҶ’- Control room environments are often dimly lit
  1672вҶ’- Reduces eye strain
  1673вҶ’- Professional appearance
  1674вҶ’
  1675вҶ’**Implementation:**
  1676вҶ’
  1677вҶ’```css
  1678вҶ’:root {
  1679вҶ’  --bg-primary: #1F2937;
  1680вҶ’  --bg-secondary: #374151;
  1681вҶ’  --bg-tertiary: #111827;
  1682вҶ’  --text-primary: #F9FAFB;
  1683вҶ’  --text-secondary: #D1D5DB;
  1684вҶ’  --text-muted: #9CA3AF;
  1685вҶ’  --border-color: #4B5563;
  1686вҶ’}
  1687вҶ’```
  1688вҶ’
  1689вҶ’**Rules:**
  1690вҶ’1. NO white backgrounds anywhere
  1691вҶ’2. All text must meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1)
  1692вҶ’3. Status colors must be visible on dark backgrounds
  1693вҶ’4. Charts must use dark theme
  1694вҶ’
  1695вҶ’### 8.7 Railway-Specific Visual Patterns
  1696вҶ’
  1697вҶ’**Train ID Display:**
  1698вҶ’
  1699вҶ’```
  1700вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

# PART II: USER EXPERIENCE & DESIGN

---

## Chapter 5: User Personas

### 5.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner

**Profile:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Name | Paul |
| Age | 45 years |
| Role | Maintenance Planner |
| Experience | 18 years in railway maintenance |
| Education | Mechanical Engineering degree |

**Work Context:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Daily System Usage | 4-8 hours |
| Primary Device | Desktop with large monitor (27"+) |
| Secondary Device | Tablet for depot floor checks |
| Work Hours | 07:00 - 16:00 (day shift) |
| Team Size | Reports to Maintenance Manager, works with 3 other planners |

**Goals:**

1. Schedule preventive maintenance minimizing operational impact
2. Balance depot capacity across weeks
3. Ensure no train exceeds maintenance tolerance windows
4. Coordinate with Operations to minimize service disruption
5. Reduce dead running for maintenance movements

**Frustrations:**

1. "I spend hours in Excel trying to balance the schedule"
2. "Operations changes plans without telling me"
3. "I can't see impact of changes until it's too late"
4. "Every depot is managed separately, no fleet-wide view"
5. "Constant firefighting because maintenance gets pushed"

**Success Moment:**
> "I can see the whole fleet's km distribution and immediately know which trains need attention."

**Critical Need:** Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality showing depot capacity

### 5.2 Diana - The Dispatcher

**Profile:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Name | Diana |
| Age | 38 years |
| Role | Dispatcher (Operations Controller) |
| Experience | 12 years in railway operations |
| Education | Transportation Management diploma |

**Work Context:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Daily System Usage | Continuous during 8-hour shift |
| Primary Device | Desktop with multiple monitors (2-3) |
| Secondary Device | Radio/phone for depot communication |
| Work Hours | Rotating shifts (06:00-14:00, 14:00-22:00, 22:00-06:00) |
| Team Size | 2-3 dispatchers per shift |

**Goals:**

1. Allocate trains to services as quickly as possible
2. Minimize service delays due to train unavailability
3. Make good decisions with limited information
4. Handle disruptions efficiently
5. Communicate clearly with depot staff

**Frustrations:**

1. "The system takes forever to show me available trains"
2. "I don't know why one train is better than another"
3. "I have to call depots to check if trains are actually available"
4. "When something goes wrong, I'm blamed for poor train choice"
5. "No time to analyze, just react"

**Success Moment:**
> "The system told me exactly why this train was recommended, so I can defend my decision."

**Critical Need:** Response time < 3 seconds, clear justifications

### 5.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager

**Profile:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Name | Gabriel |
| Age | 52 years |
| Role | Fleet Manager |
| Experience | 25 years in railway industry |
| Education | MBA, Engineering background |

**Work Context:**

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Weekly System Usage | 30-60 minutes |
| Primary Device | Desktop or tablet |
| Secondary Device | Mobile phone for alerts |
| Meeting Load | Heavy - needs quick data access |
| Reports To | Operations Director |

**Goals:**

1. Monitor fleet KPIs for board reporting
2. Make strategic decisions on fleet utilization
3. Justify maintenance budgets with data
4. Identify trends before they become problems
5. Benchmark performance across depots

**Frustrations:**

1. "Data is scattered across 5 different systems"
2. "I need my analysts to build every report"
3. "Can't drill down from summary to details"
4. "Numbers don't match between systems"
5. "Takes days to answer simple board questions"

**Success Moment:**
> "I walked into the board meeting and had all the answers at my fingertips."

**Critical Need:** Dashboard with executive view, no navigation required

### 5.4 Design Implications per Persona

**Paul (Planner) Design Requirements:**

| Need | Design Solution |
|------|-----------------|
| Long sessions (4-8h) | Dark mode to reduce eye strain |
| Complex data | Dense information display, data tables |
| Frequent actions | Keyboard shortcuts for power users |
| Pattern recognition | Visual indicators, color coding |
| Planning horizon | Timeline views (Gantt), calendar integration |
| Depot context | Capacity heatmaps, utilization charts |

**Diana (Dispatcher) Design Requirements:**

| Need | Design Solution |
|------|-----------------|
| Speed critical | Instant responses (< 3s) |
| Quick decisions | Single-click actions, confirmation shortcuts |
| Spatial awareness | Map always visible |
| Trust in recommendations | Visible justifications |
| Shift handover | Status at a glance, no hidden states |
| Error recovery | Undo actions, clear error messages |

**Gabriel (Manager) Design Requirements:**

| Need | Design Solution |
|------|-----------------|
| Limited time | KPIs front and center |
| Big picture | Aggregated metrics, trends |
| Touch friendly | Large tap targets for tablet |
| Presentation ready | Clean visuals, exportable |
| Drill down | Click any number for details |
| Mobile alerts | Responsive design, notification support |

## Chapter 6: User Journeys

### 6.1 Journey 1: Allocating a Train to Service (Diana)

**Context:**
Diana arrives at the operations center for her morning shift. The timetable shows that service IC 501 (Lisbon to Porto, 336 km) needs a train assigned for tomorrow's departure.

**Scene 1: The Request**

Diana opens MMS and navigates to the Allocation screen. She selects tomorrow's date using the date picker.

*Screen State:*
- Left panel: List of pending services for selected date
- IC 501 highlighted: "Lisbon вҶ’ Porto | 336 km | Dep: 08:15"
- Status: Unassigned (red indicator)

**Scene 2: Service Selection**

Diana clicks on IC 501. The service details panel expands showing:
- Route: Lisbon Santa ApolГіnia вҶ’ Porto CampanhГЈ
- Distance: 336 km
- Departure: 08:15
- Arrival: 11:45
- Required aptitude: North Line (220 km/h capable)

*Action:* Diana clicks "Get Recommendations"

**Scene 4: Confirmation**

- Toast: "PM1 for UTE-3400-012 rescheduled to Jan 13"

    в”Ӯ

    в”Ӯ

    в”Ӯ

## Chapter 8: Visual Design System

### 8.1 Design Principles

| Principle | Application | Example |
|-----------|-------------|---------|
| Domain Authenticity | Use railway terminology everywhere | "Train" not "Vehicle" |
| Decision Support | Every screen helps make better decisions | Justifications on recommendations |
| Visual Trust | Show data, not just conclusions | Distribution chart, not just "bad variance" |
| Progressive Disclosure | Summary first, details on demand | KPI вҶ’ Drill-down |
| Operational Efficiency | Minimize clicks for common tasks | < 5 clicks for allocation |

**Design Mantra:**

> "The interface should feel like it was built by someone who has spent time in a railway control room."

### 8.2 Color Palette & Semantic Colors

**Brand Colors:**

| Name | Hex | Usage |
|------|-----|-------|
| Primary | `#2563EB` | Interactive elements, primary buttons |
| Primary Dark | `#1D4ED8` | Hover states |
| Primary Light | `#3B82F6` | Highlights, links |

**Semantic Colors (Status):**

| Status | Color | Hex | Usage |
|--------|-------|-----|-------|
| Available / Success | Green | `#22C55E` | Fit trains, successful actions |
| In-Service / Info | Blue | `#3B82F6` | Active trains, informational |
| Warning | Amber | `#F59E0B` | Variance 10-20%, approaching limits |
| Maintenance / Danger | Red | `#EF4444` | Unfit trains, exceeded limits |
| Neutral | Gray | `#6B7280` | Disabled, secondary info |

**Maintenance Type Colors:**

| Type | Color | Hex |
|------|-------|-----|
| PM1 | Light Blue | `#93C5FD` |
| PM2 | Dark Blue | `#3B82F6` |
| HM | Red | `#EF4444` |
| OVH | Purple | `#A855F7` |

**Background Colors (Dark Mode):**

| Element | Color | Hex |
|---------|-------|-----|
| Page Background | Dark Gray | `#1F2937` |
| Card Background | Slightly Lighter | `#374151` |
| Panel Background | Darker | `#111827` |
| Border | Subtle | `#4B5563` |

### 8.3 Typography Scale

**Font Family:** Inter (primary), system-ui (fallback)

| Scale | Size | Weight | Line Height | Usage |
|-------|------|--------|-------------|-------|
| xs | 12px | 400 | 1.5 | Labels, metadata |
| sm | 14px | 400 | 1.5 | Body text, table cells |
| base | 16px | 400 | 1.5 | Default body |
| lg | 18px | 500 | 1.4 | Subheadings |
| xl | 20px | 600 | 1.3 | Card titles |
| 2xl | 24px | 600 | 1.2 | Page headings |
| 3xl | 30px | 700 | 1.1 | Dashboard KPI numbers |
| 4xl | 48px | 700 | 1.0 | Hero numbers |

**Monospace:** For train IDs, codes, technical values
- Font: `JetBrains Mono` or `Fira Code`
- Example: `UTE-3400-012`, `45,230 km`

### 8.4 Spacing & Layout Grid

**Spacing Scale (8px base):**

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| space-1 | 4px | Tight spacing within components |
| space-2 | 8px | Default component padding |
| space-3 | 12px | Between related elements |
| space-4 | 16px | Between components |
| space-6 | 24px | Section spacing |
| space-8 | 32px | Page sections |
| space-12 | 48px | Major sections |

**Layout Grid:**

- Container max-width: 1440px
- Gutter: 24px
- Columns: 12
- Responsive breakpoints:
  - sm: 640px
  - md: 768px
  - lg: 1024px
  - xl: 1280px
  - 2xl: 1536px

### 8.5 Component Library

**Recommended: Blueprint.js v5+**

Blueprint.js is chosen for its:
- Enterprise-ready components
- Dark mode support out of box
- Data-dense table components
- Excellent TypeScript support
- Consistent with industrial applications

**Core Components Used:**

| Component | Blueprint Name | Usage |
|-----------|---------------|-------|
| Button | Button | Actions, confirmations |
| Card | Card, Elevation | Content containers |
| Table | HTMLTable, Table2 | Data grids |
| Select | Select, MultiSelect | Dropdowns, filters |
| DatePicker | DatePicker, DateRangeInput | Date selection |
| Dialog | Dialog, Alert | Modals, confirmations |
| Toast | Toaster, Toast | Notifications |
| Menu | Menu, MenuItem | Context menus |
| Tabs | Tabs, Tab | Page sections |
| Tag | Tag | Status indicators |
| ProgressBar | ProgressBar | Capacity indicators |
| Spinner | Spinner | Loading states |

### 8.6 Dark Mode Specification

**Requirement:** Application MUST be dark mode by default.

**Rationale:**
- Operators work long shifts (8+ hours)
- Control room environments are often dimly lit
- Reduces eye strain
- Professional appearance

**Implementation:**

```css
:root {
  --bg-primary: #1F2937;
  --bg-secondary: #374151;
  --bg-tertiary: #111827;
  --text-primary: #F9FAFB;
  --text-secondary: #D1D5DB;
  --text-muted: #9CA3AF;
  --border-color: #4B5563;
}
```

**Rules:**
1. NO white backgrounds anywhere
2. All text must meet WCAG AA contrast (4.5:1)
3. Status colors must be visible on dark backgrounds
4. Charts must use dark theme

| Component | Props | Data Source |
|-----------|-------|-------------|
| KPICard | title, value, subtitle, status, onClick | `/api/dashboard/summary` |
| FleetMap | trains[], depots[], onTrainClick | `/api/trains`, `/api/depots` |
| MaintenanceAlerts | upcoming[] | `/api/dashboard/alerts` |
| DepotCapacityCards | depots[] | `/api/dashboard/depot-capacity` |

**KPI Card Specification:**

| Card | Value | Subtitle | Status Logic |
|------|-------|----------|--------------|
| Total Trains | count | - | Always neutral |
| Available | count | percentage | Green if >80%, Yellow if 70-80%, Red if <70% |
| In Maintenance | count | - | Always neutral |
| Km Variance | percentage | target | Green if вүӨ10%, Yellow if 10-20%, Red if >20% |

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Page loads in < 3 seconds
- [ ] All 4 KPI cards display correct values
- [ ] Clicking any KPI navigates to appropriate detail screen
- [ ] Map shows train markers with correct status colors
- [ ] Clicking train marker shows popup with train info
- [ ] Depot capacity bars show correct percentages
- [ ] Upcoming maintenance shows events for next 7 days

**Drag-Drop Behavior:**

1. User clicks and holds a maintenance block
2. Block becomes draggable, cursor changes
3. Drop zones highlight: green (valid) / red (invalid)
4. On drop, system validates constraints
5. If valid: block moves, toast confirms
6. If invalid: block returns, error explains why

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Gantt displays 4 weeks by default
- [ ] Trains grouped by fleet
- [ ] Maintenance blocks are color-coded by type
- [ ] Hovering block shows details tooltip
- [ ] Drag-drop allows rescheduling
- [ ] Constraints validated in real-time during drag
- [ ] Depot capacity heatmap shows utilization
- [ ] Cells > 90% capacity show warning

# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
## Complete Product & Technical Specification

**Version:** 2.0.0
**Date:** 2026-01-05
**Status:** Definitive Specification for Rebuild
**Audience:** UX/Product Team, Development Team, Stakeholders

---

## Document Purpose

This document serves as the **Single Source of Truth** for rebuilding the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) from scratch. It consolidates all requirements, architecture decisions, UX specifications, and lessons learned from the initial Claude Code implementation.

The original implementation encountered complexity issues that resulted in incomplete features and inconsistent behavior. This specification provides a complete, unambiguous reference for human teams to deliver a production-ready demo.

---

---

# PART I: BUSINESS CONTEXT & STRATEGY

---

## Chapter 1: Executive Summary

### 1.1 Project Overview

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution designed to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets exceeding 100 units. The system bridges the gap between Operations and Maintenance departments through predictive, data-driven planning.

**Core Problem Statement:**

Railway operators managing large fleets face three critical pain points:

1. **Over-running trains** - Units exceeding target mileage degrade faster, increasing maintenance costs and reducing asset lifespan
2. **Under-running trains** - Units falling below target mileage represent idle capital with poor return on investment
3. **Manual planning limitations** - Excel-based planning does not scale for 100+ train fleets and cannot optimize across multiple constraints

**Solution Vision:**

MMS addresses all three pain points through:
- Intelligent mileage balancing across the entire fleet
- AI-powered train recommendations with visible justifications
- Predictive maintenance scheduling coordinated with operations
- What-if simulation for strategic planning

### 1.2 Business Objective

**Primary Goal:** Win a competitive bid for a railway fleet management contract through a compelling 15-minute demo presentation.

**Target Audience:**
- Operations Director (decision maker)
- Client's CTO (technical validator)
- Maintenance leadership (domain experts)

**Success Statement:**

> After 15 minutes of demo, the client should think: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**

**Key Differentiators from Competitors:**

| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
|----------------|----------------|
| Km balancing as PRIMARY objective | Competitors treat this as a secondary feature |
| Unified Maintenance вҶ” Operations interface | Eliminates departmental silos, reduces rework |
| Visible dead running cost | Hidden cost made explicit and optimizable |
| Accessible what-if simulation | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
| Transparent decision justification | Trust building through explainability, not black box AI |

### 1.3 Success Metrics

**User Success Metrics:**

| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| Planner | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
| Planner | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
| Planner | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
| Dispatcher | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
| Dispatcher | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
| Manager | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
| All Users | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |

**Business Success Metrics:**

| Metric | Target |
|--------|--------|
| Demo Duration | 15 minutes maximum |
| Demo Stability | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures |
| Domain Terminology | 100% correct EN 15380 railway terms |
| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" |

**Technical Success Metrics:**

| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in live demo |
| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactive feel |
| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Critical for presentation |

### 1.4 Timeline & Constraints

**Project Timeline:** 12 weeks

**Milestone Schedule:**

| Week | Milestone | Deliverable |
|------|-----------|-------------|
| Week 4 | Core solver working | Proof of technical capability |
| Week 6 | Navigable UI with mocked data | Visual credibility established |
| Week 8 | End-to-end flow working | Integration proof complete |
| Week 10 | Specialist validation passed | Domain credibility confirmed |
| Week 12 | Demo-ready with fallbacks | Risk mitigation complete |

**Technical Constraints:**

| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
|------------|--------|------------|
| 12-week timeline | Limited feature scope | Focus exclusively on P0 features |
| Demo-only scope | No production hardening | Use seed data, skip authentication |
| Python solver requirement | Cross-language integration | JSON REST API between services |
| OR-Tools learning curve | Development complexity | Simplify model, use greedy fallback |

**Key Assumptions:**

1. Demo environment runs on stable network (no offline mode needed)
2. Seed data is pre-generated (no real EAM integration)
3. Single-tenant, no multi-organization support required
4. English UI only (no internationalization)
5. Desktop-primary, tablet-secondary (no mobile optimization)

## Chapter 2: Railway Domain Fundamentals

### 2.1 Essential Glossary

All UI labels and system messages MUST use standard EN 15380 railway terminology. Using incorrect terms destroys credibility with railway professionals.

**Core Terms:**

| Term | Definition | NOT Acceptable |
|------|------------|----------------|
| Rolling Stock | Railway vehicles requiring maintenance (trains, carriages, locomotives) | Vehicle, Asset, Unit |
| Fleet | Group of rolling stock sharing the same manufacturer/model and maintenance profile | Group, Class, Collection |
| Depot | Designated maintenance facility for a fleet | Garage, Workshop, Station |
| Train Fitness | Binary operational readiness status (fit/unfit) | Availability, Health, Status |
| Run Assignment | Daily allocation of train IDs to routes/services | Dispatch, Schedule, Trip |
| Dead Running | Non-revenue movement of train between locations | Empty movement, Repositioning |
| Service | A scheduled revenue-generating journey from origin to destination | Route, Trip, Run |
| Mileage Balancing | Strategy to equalize wear across fleet by controlling km distribution | Usage balancing, Wear distribution |

**Maintenance Terms:**

| Term | Definition | Typical Interval |
|------|------------|------------------|
| PM1 | Preventive Maintenance Level 1 (light inspection) | Every 15,000 km |
| PM2 | Preventive Maintenance Level 2 (medium service) | Every 45,000 km |
| HM | Heavy Maintenance (major overhaul) | Every 90,000 km |
| OVH | Overhaul (complete refurbishment) | Every 6 years or 500,000 km |

**Operational Terms:**

| Term | Definition |
|------|------------|
| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership over fleet lifecycle |
| Overrun | Train that has exceeded its target mileage |
| Underrun | Train that falls short of its target mileage |
| Variance | Statistical measure of mileage distribution spread across fleet |
| Aptitude | Train's certification for specific line/speed requirements |

**Cardinality Rules:**

- One Fleet has many Trains (1:N)
- One Train belongs to one Fleet (N:1)
- One Depot can host trains from multiple Fleets (N:M)
- One Train has one Base Depot (N:1)
- One Service can have many Allocations (different dates) (1:N)
- One Train can have many Allocations (different services) (1:N)
- One Train can have many Maintenance Schedules (1:N)

### 2.4 Critical Domain Rules (Unknown Unknowns)

These are domain constraints that seasoned railway professionals know intuitively but are rarely documented. Violating these rules destroys credibility instantly.

**Hard Constraints (Never Violate):**

1. **Unfit trains are NEVER assigned to service** - No exception, no override
2. **Maintenance windows are sacred** - A train scheduled for PM cannot be reassigned
3. **Aptitude requirements are binary** - A train either can or cannot run on a line
4. **Depot capacity is physical** - You cannot exceed track slots, period
5. **Service cannot be cancelled to balance km** - Operations > Optimization

**Soft Constraints (Optimize Within):**

1. **Km balancing priority** - Under-running trains should get high-km services
2. **Dead running minimization** - Prefer trains that end near next service start
3. **Maintenance depot proximity** - Schedule services that end near maintenance depot
4. **Crew familiarity** - Same crews prefer same train types (not modeled in MVP)

**Critical Timing Rules:**

| Rule | Specification |
|------|---------------|
| PM1 tolerance window | Can be Вұ500 km from trigger |
| PM2 tolerance window | Can be Вұ1,000 km from trigger |
| HM tolerance window | Can be Вұ2,000 km from trigger |
| Overrun alert | Train at 95% of next PM triggers warning |
| Critical overrun | Train at 105% of next PM triggers alert |

### 2.5 What the System Does NOT Do

Explicit scope boundaries to prevent scope creep and set correct expectations:

**Phase 2+ (Not in Demo):**

| Feature | Reason for Exclusion | Target Phase |
|---------|---------------------|--------------|
| MILP long-term optimization | Exceeds complexity budget | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM system integration | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption management | Different architecture needed | Phase 3 |
| Integrated crew scheduling | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native mobile application | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-language support | English sufficient for demo | Production |
| Real-time GPS tracking | Simulated positions sufficient | Production |
| Predictive maintenance ML | Beyond demo scope | Phase 3 |

**Explicitly NOT Goals:**

- This is NOT a full EAM (Enterprise Asset Management) system
- This is NOT a crew scheduling system
- This is NOT a timetabling system
- This is NOT a revenue management system
- This does NOT handle disruption/delay management
- This does NOT integrate with signaling systems

## Chapter 3: Market Analysis & Competitive Positioning

### 3.1 Competitor Landscape

**Primary Competitors:**

| Competitor | Focus Area | Strength | Weakness |
|------------|------------|----------|----------|
| DELMIA Quintiq | Large-scale optimization | Powerful solver, enterprise scale | Complex, expensive, long implementation |
| Tracsis | UK rail operations | Strong timetabling, UK market presence | Less focus on maintenance integration |
| Railnova | Rail fleet telematics | Real-time data, IoT integration | Less sophisticated optimization |
| SignatureRail | Fleet maintenance | Clean UI, workflow focus | Limited optimization, basic balancing |

### 3.2 SignatureRail Deep Analysis

SignatureRail represents the most direct competitor in terms of user experience and feature set.

**SignatureRail Strengths:**

- Clean, modern enterprise UI
- Well-organized workflow panels
- Good use of data tables
- Professional color scheme
- Clear navigation structure

**SignatureRail Weaknesses (MMS Opportunities):**

1. **Basic km tracking without optimization** - Shows variance but doesn't recommend actions
2. **No visible justifications** - Recommendations are black box
3. **Limited simulation capability** - Cannot run what-if scenarios
4. **Separate maintenance and operations** - Silos remain
5. **No map visualization** - Spatial context missing

### 3.3 Market Gaps & Opportunities

**Gap Analysis:**

| Gap | Market Need | MMS Solution |
|-----|-------------|--------------|
| Passive vs Active Balancing | Systems show variance but don't optimize | AI-powered recommendations with justifications |
| Black Box Decisions | Users don't understand why system recommends | Transparent 3+ reason justifications |
| Operations/Maintenance Silos | Separate systems, manual coordination | Unified interface for both departments |
| Strategic Planning | Day-to-day focus, no simulation | What-if scenarios for planning |
| Dead Running Visibility | Hidden cost, rarely tracked | Explicit display and optimization |

### 3.4 MMS Differentiators

**Primary Differentiators:**

1. **Km Balancing as Core Objective**
   - Not a secondary feature or afterthought
   - Drives all allocation recommendations
   - Visible in dashboard KPIs

2. **Transparent Justifications**
   - Every recommendation shows 3+ reasons
   - Human-readable explanations
   - Builds trust vs black box

3. **Unified Maintenance вҶ” Operations**
   - Single interface for both departments
   - Recommendations consider maintenance schedule
   - Reduces coordination overhead

4. **Accessible Simulation**
   - Decision-makers can test scenarios
   - Side-by-side comparison
   - No technical expertise required

5. **Visible Dead Running**
   - Hidden cost made explicit
   - Factored into optimization
   - ROI clearly demonstrable

## Chapter 4: Demo Strategy & Presentation

### 4.1 Core Principle: Depth Over Breadth

> **Mantra:** "Solving 1 pain point extremely well > solving 10 pain points poorly"

**Strategic Focus:**

The demo should demonstrate deep understanding of the km balancing problem rather than showcase many features superficially. Every feature shown must:

1. Be 100% functional (no "coming soon")
2. Use correct railway terminology
3. Show real (simulated but realistic) data
4. Complete end-to-end without errors

### 4.2 Wow Moments

These are specific moments in the demo designed to create impact:

**Wow Moment 1: The Variance Discovery**
- Dashboard loads showing 18% variance (yellow warning)
- Client recognizes their exact problem
- "They're showing us what we live every day"

**Wow Moment 2: The Justification Card**
- Recommendation shows not just WHAT but WHY
- 3+ human-readable reasons
- "Finally, a system that explains itself"

**Wow Moment 3: The What-If**
- Create scenario, adjust allocations
- See projected impact in < 10 seconds
- "I can test my ideas before committing"

**Wow Moment 4: The Map Context**
- See where all trains are spatially
- Click train, see km delta immediately
- "This gives us operational awareness"

### 4.3 Credibility Checklist

Before demo, verify:

- [ ] All terminology is EN 15380 compliant
- [ ] Depot names are real Portuguese locations
- [ ] Service codes follow realistic patterns (IC, R, U)
- [ ] Maintenance intervals are realistic (PM1: 15k km)
- [ ] Train IDs follow realistic naming (UTE-3400-xxx)
- [ ] Km values are in plausible ranges (30k-60k)
- [ ] Variance percentages are realistic (10-25%)
- [ ] Map shows Portugal correctly positioned

### 4.4 Anti-Patterns to Avoid

**NEVER do these in demo:**

| Anti-Pattern | Why It Fails | Alternative |
|--------------|--------------|-------------|
| Show stub screens | Destroys credibility | Show fewer, complete features |
| Use placeholder data | "Lorem ipsum" is unprofessional | Use realistic railway data |
| Demo features that don't work | Client will try to break it | Only show stable features |
| Use generic software terms | "Vehicle" instead of "Train" | Use railway terminology |
| Show loading spinners | Suggests slow system | Pre-cache or mock fast responses |
| Apologize for missing features | Shows lack of confidence | Present what exists as intentional |

### 4.5 Presentation Script (15 minutes)

**Minutes 0-2: Context Setting**
- "We understand your km balancing challenge"
- Show dashboard with variance indicator
- "This is what you see every day"

**Minutes 2-5: The Problem Quantified**
- Click variance to see distribution
- Show outliers (5 over-running, 4 under-running)
- "These 9 trains are costing you money"

**Minutes 5-8: The Solution in Action**
- Navigate to Allocation
- Select a pending service
- Show recommendations with justifications
- "Not just WHAT to assign, but WHY"
- Confirm allocation with one click

**Minutes 8-11: Planning Ahead**
- Navigate to Simulation
- Create what-if scenario
- Adjust allocations for under-running trains
- Show projected variance improvement
- "Test your strategy before committing"

**Minutes 11-13: The Big Picture**
- Return to Dashboard
- Show map with train positions
- Show depot capacity
- "Everything in one view"

**Minutes 13-15: Q&A Buffer**
- Prepared questions if no questions asked
- Have fallback screens ready

### 4.6 FAQ & Fallback Plans

**Anticipated Questions:**

| Question | Prepared Answer |
|----------|-----------------|
| "How does the solver work?" | "CP-SAT optimization balancing multiple constraints - we can show the logic" |
| "Can it integrate with our EAM?" | "Yes, REST API design allows integration with any EAM system" |

| Database connection lost | Static JSON fallback data |
| Map fails to load | Table view of train positions |
| Browser crash | Second laptop with same setup |

file.content:
# MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
## Complete Product & Technical Specification

**Version:** 2.0.0
**Date:** 2026-01-05
**Status:** Definitive Specification for Rebuild
**Audience:** UX/Product Team, Development Team, Stakeholders

---

## Document Purpose

This document serves as the **Single Source of Truth** for rebuilding the MMS (Metro Mileage Management System) from scratch. It consolidates all requirements, architecture decisions, UX specifications, and lessons learned from the initial Claude Code implementation.

The original implementation encountered complexity issues that resulted in incomplete features and inconsistent behavior. This specification provides a complete, unambiguous reference for human teams to deliver a production-ready demo.

---

## Table of Contents

### Part I: Business Context & Strategy

#### 1. Executive Summary
- 1.1 Project Overview
- 1.2 Business Objective
- 1.3 Success Metrics
- 1.4 Timeline & Constraints

> **Covers:** High-level project goals, the competitive bid context, what success looks like, and the 12-week timeline with key milestones.

#### 2. Railway Domain Fundamentals
- 2.1 Essential Glossary
- 2.2 Business Entities & Relationships
- 2.3 Train Lifecycle & Maintenance Hierarchy
- 2.4 Critical Domain Rules (Unknown Unknowns)
- 2.5 What the System Does NOT Do

> **Covers:** Railway-specific terminology (EN 15380), entity relationships, maintenance intervals (PM1/PM2/HM), and domain constraints that are critical for credibility.

#### 3. Market Analysis & Competitive Positioning
- 3.1 Competitor Landscape
- 3.2 SignatureRail Deep Analysis
- 3.3 Market Gaps & Opportunities
- 3.4 MMS Differentiators

> **Covers:** Analysis of DELMIA Quintiq, Tracsis, Railnova, SignatureRail; identified gaps in the market; and how MMS positions itself.

#### 4. Demo Strategy & Presentation
- 4.1 Core Principle: Depth Over Breadth
- 4.2 Wow Moments
- 4.3 Credibility Checklist
- 4.4 Anti-Patterns to Avoid
- 4.5 Presentation Script (15 minutes)
- 4.6 FAQ & Fallback Plans

> **Covers:** How to win the bid through impressive demo, key moments that create impact, presentation flow, and contingency plans.

---

### Part II: User Experience & Design

#### 5. User Personas
- 5.1 Paul - The Maintenance Planner
- 5.2 Diana - The Dispatcher
- 5.3 Gabriel - The Fleet Manager
- 5.4 Design Implications per Persona

> **Covers:** Detailed persona profiles including goals, frustrations, device preferences, and how each persona influences UI decisions.

#### 6. User Journeys
- 6.1 Journey 1: Allocating a Train to Service (Diana)
- 6.2 Journey 2: Identifying and Resolving Imbalance (Paul)
- 6.3 Journey 3: Planning Maintenance Schedule (Paul)
- 6.4 Journey 4: Executive Review (Gabriel)
- 6.5 Journey Requirements Matrix

> **Covers:** Step-by-step user flows with scenes, expected times, click counts, and requirements revealed by each journey.

#### 7. Information Architecture
- 7.1 Navigation Structure
- 7.2 Drill-Down Hierarchy
- 7.3 Screen Inventory
- 7.4 Component Relationships

> **Covers:** How screens connect, navigation patterns, progressive disclosure hierarchy, and mental model alignment.

#### 8. Visual Design System
- 8.1 Design Principles
- 8.2 Color Palette & Semantic Colors
- 8.3 Typography Scale
- 8.4 Spacing & Layout Grid
- 8.5 Component Library (Blueprint.js v6)
- 8.6 Dark Mode Specification
- 8.7 Railway-Specific Visual Patterns

> **Covers:** Complete design token system, Blueprint.js integration, dark mode requirements, and enterprise UI patterns.

#### 9. Screen Specifications
- 9.1 Dashboard (Home)
- 9.2 Map View

**Drag-Drop Behavior:**

1. User clicks and holds a maintenance block
2. Block becomes draggable, cursor changes
3. Drop zones highlight: green (valid) / red (invalid)
4. On drop, system validates constraints
5. If valid: block moves, toast confirms
6. If invalid: block returns, error explains why

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Gantt displays 4 weeks by default
- [ ] Trains grouped by fleet
- [ ] Maintenance blocks are color-coded by type
- [ ] Hovering block shows details tooltip
- [ ] Drag-drop allows rescheduling
- [ ] Constraints validated in real-time during drag
- [ ] Depot capacity heatmap shows utilization
- [ ] Cells > 90% capacity show warning

    14вҶ’---
    15вҶ’
    16вҶ’# Product Requirements Document - MMS - Metro Mileage Management System
    17вҶ’
    18вҶ’**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
    19вҶ’**Date:** 2025-12-28
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’---
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’## Executive Summary
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units), bridging Operations and Maintenance via predictive, data-driven planning.
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’### Vision
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’Create a functional demo in **3 months** that:
    30вҶ’- Demonstrates technical competence in railway fleet management
    31вҶ’- Proves deep understanding of the railway domain
    32вҶ’- Impresses decision-makers (Operations Director, client's CTO)
    33вҶ’
    34вҶ’### Success Metric
    35вҶ’
    36вҶ’> After 15 minutes of demo, the client thinks: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**
    37вҶ’
    38вҶ’### Core Problem
    39вҶ’
    40вҶ’Railway operators face three critical pain points:
    41вҶ’1. **Over-running trains** degrade faster, increasing maintenance cost
    42вҶ’2. **Under-running trains** represent idle capital
    43вҶ’3. **Manual planning in Excel** doesn't scale for 100+ train fleets
    44вҶ’
    45вҶ’MMS addresses all three points through intelligent mileage balancing, maintenance scheduling, and operations coordination.
    46вҶ’
    47вҶ’### What Makes This Special
    48вҶ’
    49вҶ’| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
    50вҶ’|----------------|----------------|
    51вҶ’| **Km balancing as primary objective** | Competitors treat as secondary feature |
    52вҶ’| **Unified Maintenance вҶ” Operations interface** | Eliminates silos, reduces rework |
    53вҶ’| **Visible dead running cost** | Hidden cost made explicit and optimized |
    54вҶ’| **Accessible what-if simulation** | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
    55вҶ’| **Visible decision justification** | Trust, not black box - every recommendation explains why |
    56вҶ’
    57вҶ’---
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’## Project Classification
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’| Attribute | Value |
    62вҶ’|-----------|-------|
    63вҶ’| **Technical Type** | SaaS B2B (Web Application with API Backend + Python Solver) |
    64вҶ’| **Domain** | Railway/Transportation Fleet Management |
    65вҶ’| **Complexity** | High |
    66вҶ’| **Project Context** | Greenfield - new demo for competitive bid |
    67вҶ’| **Demo Duration** | 15 minutes |
    68вҶ’| **Development Timeline** | 12 weeks |
    69вҶ’
    70вҶ’### Technical Stack Overview
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’| Layer | Technology |
    73вҶ’|-------|------------|
    74вҶ’| Frontend | Next.js 14 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui |
    75вҶ’| Backend | Node.js + Fastify + TypeScript |
    76вҶ’| Solver | Python 3.11 + Google OR-Tools (CP-SAT) |
    77вҶ’| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
    78вҶ’| Cache | Redis |
    79вҶ’| Maps | Mapbox or Leaflet |
    80вҶ’| Charts | Recharts or Chart.js |
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’---
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’## Success Criteria
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’### User Success
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’**Primary Users:** Maintenance Planners, Dispatchers (Operations Controllers), Fleet Managers
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
    91вҶ’|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
    92вҶ’| **Planner** | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
    93вҶ’| **Planner** | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
    94вҶ’| **Planner** | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
    95вҶ’| **Dispatcher** | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
    96вҶ’| **Dispatcher** | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
    97вҶ’| **Manager** | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
    98вҶ’| **All Users** | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |
    99вҶ’
   100вҶ’**User Success Moments:**
   101вҶ’- "I can see the km imbalance immediately" вҶ’ Dashboard shows variance indicator
   102вҶ’- "The system tells me WHY this train" вҶ’ Justification card explains reasoning
   103вҶ’- "I can test my ideas before committing" вҶ’ What-if simulation works
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’### Business Success
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’**Demo Objective:** Win the competitive bid
   108вҶ’
   109вҶ’| Metric | Target | Timeframe |
   110вҶ’|--------|--------|-----------|
   111вҶ’| Demo Duration | 15 minutes | Presentation day |
   112вҶ’| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" | Post-demo |
   113вҶ’| Bug-Free Demo | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures | Week 12 |
   114вҶ’| Domain Terminology | 100% correct railway terms | Throughout UI |
   115вҶ’
   116вҶ’**Business Milestones:**
   117вҶ’- Week 4: Core solver working (proof of technical capability)
   118вҶ’- Week 6: Navigable UI with mocked data (visual credibility)
   119вҶ’- Week 8: End-to-end flow working (integration proof)
   120вҶ’- Week 10: Specialist validation passed (domain credibility)
   121вҶ’- Week 12: Demo-ready with fallback screens (risk mitigation)
   122вҶ’
   123вҶ’### Technical Success
   124вҶ’

# Product Requirements Document - MMS - Metro Mileage Management System

**Author:** MMS_PROGRAMMER
**Date:** 2025-12-28

---

## Executive Summary

**MMS (Metro Mileage Management System)** is an enterprise solution to centralize and automate mileage management for mixed rolling stock fleets (>100 units), bridging Operations and Maintenance via predictive, data-driven planning.

### Vision

Create a functional demo in **3 months** that:
- Demonstrates technical competence in railway fleet management
- Proves deep understanding of the railway domain
- Impresses decision-makers (Operations Director, client's CTO)

### Success Metric

> After 15 minutes of demo, the client thinks: **"They understand our problem better than the others."**

### Core Problem

Railway operators face three critical pain points:
1. **Over-running trains** degrade faster, increasing maintenance cost
2. **Under-running trains** represent idle capital
3. **Manual planning in Excel** doesn't scale for 100+ train fleets

MMS addresses all three points through intelligent mileage balancing, maintenance scheduling, and operations coordination.

### What Makes This Special

| Differentiator | Why It Matters |
|----------------|----------------|
| **Km balancing as primary objective** | Competitors treat as secondary feature |
| **Unified Maintenance вҶ” Operations interface** | Eliminates silos, reduces rework |
| **Visible dead running cost** | Hidden cost made explicit and optimized |
| **Accessible what-if simulation** | Decision-makers can "play" with scenarios |
| **Visible decision justification** | Trust, not black box - every recommendation explains why |

---

## Project Classification

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Technical Type** | SaaS B2B (Web Application with API Backend + Python Solver) |
| **Domain** | Railway/Transportation Fleet Management |
| **Complexity** | High |
| **Project Context** | Greenfield - new demo for competitive bid |
| **Demo Duration** | 15 minutes |
| **Development Timeline** | 12 weeks |

### Technical Stack Overview

| Layer | Technology |
|-------|------------|
| Frontend | Next.js 14 + React 18 + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui |
| Backend | Node.js + Fastify + TypeScript |
| Solver | Python 3.11 + Google OR-Tools (CP-SAT) |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 |
| Cache | Redis |
| Maps | Mapbox or Leaflet |
| Charts | Recharts or Chart.js |

---

## Success Criteria

### User Success

**Primary Users:** Maintenance Planners, Dispatchers (Operations Controllers), Fleet Managers

| User Role | Success Indicator | Measurement |
|-----------|-------------------|-------------|
| **Planner** | Visualize fleet km distribution in seconds | < 5s to see complete distribution |
| **Planner** | Receive justified train recommendation | 3+ reasons per recommendation |
| **Planner** | Simulate scenarios before deciding | Side-by-side comparison in < 10s |
| **Dispatcher** | Allocate train to service quickly | < 2 minutes, < 5 clicks |
| **Dispatcher** | See current train positions on map | Real-time or < 30s refresh |
| **Manager** | Executive dashboard view | All KPIs visible without navigation |
| **All Users** | Trust the system recommendations | Decision justification always visible |

**User Success Moments:**
- "I can see the km imbalance immediately" вҶ’ Dashboard shows variance indicator
- "The system tells me WHY this train" вҶ’ Justification card explains reasoning
- "I can test my ideas before committing" вҶ’ What-if simulation works

### Business Success

**Demo Objective:** Win the competitive bid

| Metric | Target | Timeframe |
|--------|--------|-----------|
| Demo Duration | 15 minutes | Presentation day |
| Client Reaction | "They understand our problem better than the others" | Post-demo |
| Bug-Free Demo | 3 consecutive rehearsals without failures | Week 12 |
| Domain Terminology | 100% correct railway terms | Throughout UI |

**Business Milestones:**
- Week 4: Core solver working (proof of technical capability)
- Week 6: Navigable UI with mocked data (visual credibility)
- Week 8: End-to-end flow working (integration proof)
- Week 10: Specialist validation passed (domain credibility)
- Week 12: Demo-ready with fallback screens (risk mitigation)

### Technical Success

| Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|-------------|--------|-----------|
| Allocation Time | < 30 seconds for 100 trains | Dispatcher won't wait longer |
| Screen Loading | < 3 seconds | Credibility in demo |
| Map Update | < 5 seconds | "Real-time" perception |
| Simulation Response | < 10 seconds | Interactivity maintained |
| Demo Stability | No crashes for 15 minutes | Presentation reliability |

### Measurable Outcomes

**Quantitative:**
- System handles 80-120 trains, 12-15 depots, 200-300 services/day
- Solver produces 3+ justification reasons per recommendation
- UI uses validated EN 15380 railway terminology
- Km variance visualization shows fleet distribution clearly

**Qualitative:**
- Client perceives depth over breadth ("solving 1 pain point well > 10 poorly")
- Demonstration feels domain-authentic, not generic software
- Decision justifications are understandable by non-technical stakeholders

---

## Product Scope

### MVP - Minimum Viable Product (Must Have)

| Feature | Priority | Justification |
|---------|----------|---------------|
| Dashboard with KPIs | P0 | First impression, executive view |
| Map with train positions | P0 | Visual wow, operational context |
| Km balancing visualization | P0 | Main pain point demonstration |
| Working allocation engine | P0 | Proof of technical capability |
| Recommendation justification | P0 | Trust building, not black box |
| Scenario simulation | P0 | Client interaction, decision support |

### Growth Features (Should Have - Demo Enhancement)

| Feature | Priority | Justification |
|---------|----------|---------------|
| Maintenance Gantt | P1 | Market expectation for planning |
| Visual alerts | P1 | Demonstrates proactivity |
| Depot capacity visualization | P1 | Operational realism |
| Train ranking by km | P1 | Quick identification of outliers |

### Vision (Could Have - Nice for Demo)

| Feature | Priority | Justification |
|---------|----------|---------------|
| PDF/Excel export | P2 | Standard reporting expectation |
| Wheelset balancing | P2 | Advanced domain feature |
| Demo presentation script | P2 | Professional delivery |

### Explicitly Out of Scope (Phase 2+)

| Feature | Why Out | When |
|---------|---------|------|
| MILP optimization (long-term) | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM integration | Depends on client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption management | Different architecture needed | Phase 3 |
| Integrated crew scheduling | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native mobile app | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-language | English sufficient for demo | Production |

---

## User Journeys

### Personas

#### Paul - The Maintenance Planner

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Name** | Paul, 45 years old |
| **Role** | Maintenance Planner |
| **Goal** | Schedule maintenances minimizing operational impact |
| **Frequency** | Daily, 4-8 hours in system |
| **Device** | Desktop, large monitor |
| **Frustrations** | Excel spreadsheets, lack of visibility, constant rework |
| **Critical Need** | Gantt chart with drag-drop functionality |

#### Diana - The Dispatcher

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Name** | Diana, 38 years old |
| **Role** | Dispatcher (Operations Controller) |
| **Goal** | Allocate trains to services in real-time |
| **Frequency** | Continuous during shift |
| **Device** | Desktop, multiple monitors |
| **Frustrations** | Slow systems, lack of information for quick decisions |
| **Critical Need** | Response time < 3 seconds |

#### Gabriel - The Fleet Manager

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| **Name** | Gabriel, 52 years old |
| **Role** | Fleet Manager |
| **Goal** | Monitor KPIs, make strategic decisions |
| **Frequency** | Weekly, 30-60 minutes |
| **Device** | Desktop or tablet |
| **Frustrations** | Scattered data across systems, lack of drill-down |
| **Critical Need** | Dashboard with executive view |

---

### Journey 1: Diana - Allocating a Train to Service

**The Story:**
Diana arrives at the operations center for her morning shift. The timetable shows that service IC 501 (Lisbon to Porto, 336 km) needs a train assigned for tomorrow's departure. She opens MMS and navigates to the Allocation screen.

**Scene 1: The Request**
Diana selects service IC 501 from the service list. The system shows the service details: departure at 08:15, expected arrival 11:45, route profile indicating high-speed segments.

**Scene 2: The Recommendation**
With one click on "Recommend," MMS presents her with a ranked list of 5 suitable trains. The top recommendation, UTE-3400-012, shows a score of 94/100 with clear justifications:
- "Km 12% below fleet average (balancing priority)"
- "Service ends 5km from Porto maintenance depot"
- "PM1 scheduled in 3 days, Porto depot has available slot"
- "Aptitude confirmed for North line"

**Scene 3: The Decision**
Diana reviews the justifications and appreciates that the system explains WHY this train is recommended. She confirms the allocation with a single click.

**Scene 4: The Resolution**
The system updates the allocation status, and Diana moves on to the next service. Total time: under 2 minutes, fewer than 5 clicks.

**Requirements Revealed:**
- Service selection interface
- Recommendation engine with ranking
- Justification display component
- One-click allocation confirmation
- Real-time status updates

---

### Journey 2: Paul - Identifying and Resolving Mileage Imbalance

**The Story:**
Paul starts his Monday morning by checking the fleet health. He's been worried about the mileage variance creeping up over the past few weeks.

**Scene 1: The Discovery**
Opening the Dashboard, Paul immediately sees the km variance indicator showing 18% in yellow - above the ideal 10% threshold. He clicks on the indicator for details.

**Scene 2: The Analysis**
The Balancing screen shows a histogram of fleet km distribution. Paul can clearly see 5 trains well above average (over-running) and 4 trains significantly below (under-running). He hovers over train UTE-2200-015 and sees it's 25% below the fleet average.

**Scene 3: The Simulation**
Paul clicks "New Scenario" to test his hypothesis. He adjusts future allocations to prioritize the under-running trains for high-km services. The simulation runs in under 10 seconds and shows the projected impact: variance dropping from 18% to 14% over the next 30 days.

**Scene 4: The Comparison**
Using side-by-side comparison, Paul sees that his adjustment reduces variance but increases dead running by 3%. He decides the trade-off is worth it.

**Scene 5: The Resolution**
Paul applies the scenario changes and documents his decision rationale. Total time: under 10 minutes.

**Requirements Revealed:**
- Dashboard with variance indicators
- Km distribution visualization (histogram/box plot)
- Train ranking by km delta
- Scenario creation and simulation
- Side-by-side comparison view
- Apply scenario functionality

---

### Journey 3: Paul - Planning Maintenance Schedule

**The Story:**
Paul needs to schedule preventive maintenances for the coming month. He wants to ensure depot capacity isn't exceeded.

**Scene 1: The Overview**
Paul opens the Maintenance Gantt view showing the next 4 weeks. Each train has a timeline bar, and scheduled maintenances appear as colored blocks (PM1 in light blue, PM2 in dark blue, HM in red).

## Part III: Functional Requirements

### Chapter 10: Requirements Overview

This chapter establishes the complete requirements framework for MMS, organizing functional and non-functional requirements with clear priority classifications.

#### 10.1 Functional Requirements Matrix

**Core Requirements (P0 - Must Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-001 | Display fleet-wide KPI summary on dashboard | Dashboard | As a Fleet Manager, I need to see fleet health at a glance |
| FR-002 | Show km variance indicator with threshold alerts | Dashboard | As a Planner, I need to spot imbalances immediately |
| FR-003 | Display train positions on interactive map | Map | As a Dispatcher, I need to see where trains are |
| FR-004 | Show depot locations with capacity indicators | Map | As a Planner, I need to see depot availability |
| FR-005 | Generate train recommendations for service allocation | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need suggestions ranked by suitability |
| FR-006 | Display justification reasons for recommendations | Allocation | As a User, I need to understand WHY a train is recommended |
| FR-007 | Confirm allocation with single action | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need to allocate quickly |
| FR-008 | Visualize km distribution across fleet | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to see the spread |
| FR-009 | Identify over-running and under-running trains | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to find outliers |
| FR-010 | Create and run what-if scenarios | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to test decisions before committing |
| FR-011 | Compare scenarios side-by-side | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to evaluate trade-offs |

**Enhancement Requirements (P1 - Should Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-012 | Display maintenance timeline in Gantt view | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to see scheduled maintenances |
| FR-013 | Color-code maintenance types (PM1/PM2/HM) | Gantt | As a User, I need visual distinction of maintenance types |
| FR-014 | Enable drag-drop rescheduling | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to adjust schedules visually |
| FR-015 | Validate scheduling constraints on drag | Gantt | As a User, I need feedback on invalid moves |
| FR-016 | Show depot capacity utilization heatmap | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to avoid overbooking depots |
| FR-017 | Filter trains by fleet, depot, status | Fleet | As a User, I need to narrow down train lists |
| FR-018 | Display train history chart | Details | As a Planner, I need to see km trends over time |
| FR-019 | Show upcoming maintenance for each train | Details | As a Planner, I need to see what's due |
| FR-020 | Visual alerts for threshold breaches | All | As a User, I need attention drawn to problems |

**Nice-to-Have Requirements (P2 - Could Have)**

| ID | Requirement | Module | User Story |
|----|-------------|--------|------------|
| FR-021 | Export data to CSV/Excel | All | As a User, I need to share data externally |
| FR-022 | Print-friendly dashboard summary | Dashboard | As a Manager, I need paper reports for meetings |
| FR-023 | Wheelset km balancing visualization | Balancing | As a Planner, I need component-level detail |
| FR-024 | Dead running distance reporting | Reports | As a Planner, I need to minimize empty km |

#### 10.2 Non-Functional Requirements

**Performance Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-001 | Page load time | < 3 seconds | Demo credibility, user patience threshold |
| NFR-002 | Allocation recommendation | < 30 seconds | Dispatcher workflow efficiency |
| NFR-003 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds | Perceived real-time experience |
| NFR-004 | Simulation execution | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactivity during demo |
| NFR-005 | Gantt render | < 2 seconds | Smooth scrolling expected |
| NFR-006 | API response (avg) | < 500ms | UI responsiveness |

**Scalability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-007 | Train count | 80-120 trains | Typical metro operator fleet |
| NFR-008 | Depot count | 12-15 depots | Regional coverage |
| NFR-009 | Services per day | 200-300 services | Weekday peak operations |
| NFR-010 | Historical data | 12 months | Trend analysis requirements |
| NFR-011 | Concurrent users | 10-20 users | Typical operations team size |

**Reliability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-012 | Demo stability | 15+ minutes no crashes | Must survive presentation |
| NFR-013 | Error recovery | Graceful degradation | Show fallback, not blank screen |
| NFR-014 | Data consistency | No corruption | Trust in system integrity |

**Usability Requirements**

| ID | Requirement | Target | Rationale |
|----|-------------|--------|-----------|
| NFR-015 | Click depth | Max 3 clicks to any function | Information accessibility |
| NFR-016 | Learning curve | Basic use within 10 minutes | Demo walkthrough time |
| NFR-017 | Terminology | 100% EN 15380 compliance | Railway domain credibility |
| NFR-018 | Visual feedback | Action confirmation < 300ms | User confidence |

#### 10.3 Priority Classification (P0/P1/P2)

**Priority Definitions**

| Priority | Label | Definition | Demo Impact |
|----------|-------|------------|-------------|
| **P0** | Critical | Without this, demo fails | Must work flawlessly |
| **P1** | Important | Significantly enhances impression | Should work if time permits |
| **P2** | Nice-to-Have | Shows polish if present | Implement only if core is solid |

**Priority Assignment Rationale**

```
P0 Decision Tree:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Does the client specifically expect this feature?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Yes вҶ’ P0
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ Continue
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Does the demo fail without it?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Yes вҶ’ P0
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ Continue
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Is it a core differentiator?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Yes вҶ’ P0
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ P1 or P2

P1 Decision Tree:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Is it a market-standard feature?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Yes вҶ’ P1
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ Continue
в”ңв”Җв”Җ Does it significantly improve UX?
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ Yes вҶ’ P1
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ P2
```

**P0 Feature Summary (12 items)**

1. Dashboard with KPI cards
2. Km variance indicator (traffic light)
3. Map with train positions
4. Depot markers on map
5. Allocation recommendation engine
6. Justification display (3+ reasons)
7. One-click allocation
8. Km distribution visualization
9. Train outlier identification
10. Scenario creation
11. Simulation execution
12. Scenario comparison

**P1 Feature Summary (8 items)**

1. Maintenance Gantt chart
2. Drag-drop scheduling
3. Constraint validation
4. Depot capacity heatmap
5. Train list filtering
6. Train details with history
7. Visual alerts system
8. Maintenance type color coding

#### 10.4 Scope Boundaries

**Explicitly IN Scope**

| Area | Included | Notes |
|------|----------|-------|
| Train Allocation | Single-day allocation workflow | One train to one service |
| Km Balancing | Fleet-level visualization | Histogram, rankings, deltas |
| Simulation | What-if scenario system | Create, run, compare |
| Maintenance View | Read and reschedule | Gantt with drag-drop |
| Map | Current positions | Train and depot markers |
| Data | Demo dataset | 80-120 trains, seeded |

**Explicitly OUT of Scope**

| Area | Excluded | Why | Future Phase |
|------|----------|-----|--------------|
| Multi-Day Optimization | MILP solver for week/month | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
| Real EAM Integration | SAP PM, Maximo connections | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
| Disruption Management | Real-time failure handling | Different architecture | Phase 3 |
| Crew Scheduling | Driver allocation | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
| Native Mobile | iOS/Android apps | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
| Multi-Language | UI translations | English for demo | Production |
| User Authentication | Login, roles, permissions | Demo runs as single user | Production |
| Audit Trail | Change history logging | Not visible in demo | Production |
| Notification System | Email/SMS alerts | Requires infrastructure | Production |

**Scope Control Principle**

> "When in doubt, leave it out. It's better to demo 6 features that work perfectly than 12 features with bugs."

The scope is intentionally constrained to ensure quality within the 12-week timeline. Any scope addition requires removing something of equal effort.

#### 11.2 Traffic-Light Indicators

Traffic lights provide instant visual status without requiring interpretation.

**Implementation Specification**

```typescript
type TrafficLightStatus = 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red';

interface TrafficLightConfig {
  metric: string;
  greenThreshold: number;
  yellowThreshold: number;
  direction: 'ascending' | 'descending'; // lower is better vs higher is better
}

const TRAFFIC_LIGHT_CONFIGS: TrafficLightConfig[] = [
  {
    metric: 'kmVariance',
    greenThreshold: 10,
    yellowThreshold: 15,
    direction: 'descending', // lower variance is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'fitPercentage',
    greenThreshold: 90,
    yellowThreshold: 80,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher fit % is better
  },
  {
    metric: 'allocationRate',
    greenThreshold: 95,
    yellowThreshold: 85,
    direction: 'ascending', // higher allocation is better
  },
];
```

**Visual Representation**

```
Green (рҹҹў):  Background: #238823, Border: #1a6b1a
Yellow (рҹҹЎ): Background: #FFBF00, Border: #cc9900
Red (рҹ”ҙ):    Background: #D2222D, Border: #a61b24
```

**Placement Rules**

- Traffic lights appear in the top-right of each KPI card
- Size: 12px diameter circle
- On hover: Show tooltip explaining status
- On click: Navigate to detail view for that metric

**Capacity Calculation**

```typescript
interface DepotCapacity {
  depotId: string;
  date: Date;
  tracksAvailable: number;
  tracksScheduled: number;
  utilizationPercent: number; // (scheduled / available) * 100
}

function getCapacityColor(utilization: number): string {
  if (utilization < 50) return COLORS.LOW;      // Light gray
  if (utilization < 80) return COLORS.MEDIUM;   // Amber
  return COLORS.HIGH;                            // Red
}
```

**Interaction**

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Hover cell | Tooltip: "Porto - Wednesday: 3/5 tracks scheduled (60%)" |
| Click cell | Navigate to Gantt view filtered to that depot/date |
| Click depot name | Navigate to depot detail page |

#### 12.1 Map Provider & Configuration

```typescript
L.tileLayer(
  'https://{s}.basemaps.cartocdn.com/dark_all/{z}/{x}/{y}{r}.png',

);
```

**Map Container Requirements**

- Full width of content area
- Minimum height: 500px

- Border radius: 8px
- No scroll inside map (panning only)

#### 12.2 Train Markers & Status Colors

**Marker Design**

```
Train Marker (16x16px SVG):
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ в–¶в”Җв”Җ  в”Ӯ  (Simplified train icon)
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Status Color Mapping**

| Status | Color | Hex Code | Meaning |
|--------|-------|----------|---------|
| Fit (In Service) | Green | `#2D8A4E` | Operating normally |
| Fit (Available) | Blue | `#2196F3` | Ready for allocation |
| Unfit | Red | `#E63946` | Requires maintenance |
| In Maintenance | Orange | `#FF9800` | Currently at depot |

**Marker Implementation**

```typescript
interface TrainMarker {
  trainId: string;
  position: [number, number]; // [lng, lat]
  status: 'in_service' | 'available' | 'unfit' | 'in_maintenance';
  heading?: number; // 0-360 degrees for rotation
  lastUpdate: Date;
}

function getMarkerColor(status: TrainMarker['status']): string {
  const colors = {
    in_service: '#2D8A4E',
    available: '#2196F3',
    unfit: '#E63946',
    in_maintenance: '#FF9800',
  };
  return colors[status];
}
```

**Marker Hover Behavior**

On hover, show abbreviated tooltip:
```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ

#### 12.5 Auto-Refresh Mechanism

**Refresh Strategy**

| Data | Refresh Interval | Method |
|------|-----------------|--------|
| Train positions | 30 seconds | Polling API |
| Depot capacity | 5 minutes | Polling API |
| Alerts | 30 seconds | Polling API |

**Implementation**

```typescript
// Auto-refresh with visibility check
useEffect(() => {
  if (!document.hidden) {
    const interval = setInterval(() => {
      refreshTrainPositions();
    }, 30_000);
    return () => clearInterval(interval);
  }
}, []);

// Pause refresh when tab is hidden
document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', () => {
  if (document.hidden) {
    pauseRefresh();
  } else {
    resumeRefresh();
  }
});
```

**Loading States**

During refresh, show subtle loading indicator (spinner in corner), not full-page loader.

#### 12.6 Clustering Behavior

**When to Cluster**

Cluster markers when:
- Zoom level < 10
- More than 2 trains within 50px screen distance

**Cluster Visual**

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ  5  в”Ӯ  (Number of trains in cluster)
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Cluster Interaction**

| Action | Result |
|--------|--------|
| Hover cluster | Show tooltip: "5 trains in this area" |
| Click cluster | Zoom in to show individual markers |
| Scroll in | Clusters break apart into markers |

**Cluster Configuration**

```typescript
const clusterConfig = {
  radius: 50,        // px
  maxZoom: 12,       // Don't cluster above this zoom
  minPoints: 2,      // Minimum to form cluster
};
```

#### 13.2 Recommendation Engine Integration

**Request Flow**

```
User selects service
        вҶ“
Frontend calls POST /api/recommend
        вҶ“
API forwards to Python solver
        вҶ“
Solver returns ranked list with justifications
        вҶ“
Frontend displays recommendations
```

**Recommendation API**

```typescript
// Request
interface RecommendRequest {
  serviceId: string;
  date: string;  // ISO date
  limit?: number; // Max recommendations (default 5)
}

// Response
interface RecommendResponse {
  recommendations: Recommendation[];
  computeTimeMs: number;
}

interface Recommendation {
  trainId: string;
  trainCode: string;        // UTE-3400-012
  score: number;            // 0-100
  justifications: Justification[];
  warnings?: string[];
}

interface Justification {
  type: 'km_balance' | 'depot_proximity' | 'maintenance' | 'aptitude' | 'dead_running';
  description: string;
  impact: 'positive' | 'neutral' | 'negative';
  weight: number;           // Contribution to score
}
```

**Solver Scoring Factors**

| Factor | Weight | Description |
|--------|--------|-------------|
| Km Balance | 40% | Distance from fleet average |
| Depot Proximity | 25% | End position near maintenance depot |
| Maintenance Window | 20% | Alignment with scheduled maintenance |
| Dead Running | 15% | Empty km to reach service start |
