**Drag-Drop Behavior:**

1. User clicks and holds a maintenance block
2. Block becomes draggable, cursor changes
3. Drop zones highlight: green (valid) / red (invalid)
4. On drop, system validates constraints
5. If valid: block moves, toast confirms
6. If invalid: block returns, error explains why

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Gantt displays 4 weeks by default
- [ ] Trains grouped by fleet
- [ ] Maintenance blocks are color-coded by type
- [ ] Hovering block shows details tooltip
- [ ] Drag-drop allows rescheduling
- [ ] Constraints validated in real-time during drag
- [ ] Depot capacity heatmap shows utilization
- [ ] Cells > 90% capacity show warning

**Acceptance Criteria:**

- [ ] Shows complete train information
- [ ] Tabs switch between Overview, History, Maintenance
- [ ] Km history chart shows 6-month trend
- [ ] Upcoming maintenance shows next PM1/PM2/HM
- [ ] Status badge reflects current fitness
- [ ] "Back to Fleet" returns to list preserving filters

---

lines: +This chapter establishes the complete requirements framework for MMS, organizing functional and non-functional requirements with clear priority classifications.
+| FR-001 | Display fleet-wide KPI summary on dashboard | Dashboard | As a Fleet Manager, I need to see fleet health at a glance |
+| FR-002 | Show km variance indicator with threshold alerts | Dashboard | As a Planner, I need to spot imbalances immediately |
+| FR-003 | Display train positions on interactive map | Map | As a Dispatcher, I need to see where trains are |
+| FR-004 | Show depot locations with capacity indicators | Map | As a Planner, I need to see depot availability |
+| FR-005 | Generate train recommendations for service allocation | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need suggestions ranked by suitability |
+| FR-006 | Display justification reasons for recommendations | Allocation | As a User, I need to understand WHY a train is recommended |
+| FR-007 | Confirm allocation with single action | Allocation | As a Dispatcher, I need to allocate quickly |
+| FR-008 | Visualize km distribution across fleet | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to see the spread |
+| FR-009 | Identify over-running and under-running trains | Balancing | As a Planner, I need to find outliers |
+| FR-010 | Create and run what-if scenarios | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to test decisions before committing |
+| FR-011 | Compare scenarios side-by-side | Simulation | As a Planner, I need to evaluate trade-offs |
+| FR-012 | Display maintenance timeline in Gantt view | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to see scheduled maintenances |
+| FR-013 | Color-code maintenance types (PM1/PM2/HM) | Gantt | As a User, I need visual distinction of maintenance types |
+| FR-014 | Enable drag-drop rescheduling | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to adjust schedules visually |
+| FR-015 | Validate scheduling constraints on drag | Gantt | As a User, I need feedback on invalid moves |
+| FR-016 | Show depot capacity utilization heatmap | Gantt | As a Planner, I need to avoid overbooking depots |
+| FR-017 | Filter trains by fleet, depot, status | Fleet | As a User, I need to narrow down train lists |
+| FR-018 | Display train history chart | Details | As a Planner, I need to see km trends over time |
+| FR-019 | Show upcoming maintenance for each train | Details | As a Planner, I need to see what's due |
+| FR-020 | Visual alerts for threshold breaches | All | As a User, I need attention drawn to problems |
+| FR-021 | Export data to CSV/Excel | All | As a User, I need to share data externally |
+| FR-022 | Print-friendly dashboard summary | Dashboard | As a Manager, I need paper reports for meetings |
+| FR-023 | Wheelset km balancing visualization | Balancing | As a Planner, I need component-level detail |
+| FR-024 | Dead running distance reporting | Reports | As a Planner, I need to minimize empty km |
+| NFR-001 | Page load time | < 3 seconds | Demo credibility, user patience threshold |
+| NFR-002 | Allocation recommendation | < 30 seconds | Dispatcher workflow efficiency |
+| NFR-003 | Map refresh | < 5 seconds | Perceived real-time experience |
+| NFR-004 | Simulation execution | < 10 seconds | Maintain interactivity during demo |
+| NFR-005 | Gantt render | < 2 seconds | Smooth scrolling expected |
+| NFR-007 | Train count | 80-120 trains | Typical metro operator fleet |
+| NFR-008 | Depot count | 12-15 depots | Regional coverage |
+| NFR-009 | Services per day | 200-300 services | Weekday peak operations |
+| NFR-010 | Historical data | 12 months | Trend analysis requirements |
+| NFR-011 | Concurrent users | 10-20 users | Typical operations team size |
+| NFR-012 | Demo stability | 15+ minutes no crashes | Must survive presentation |
+| NFR-013 | Error recovery | Graceful degradation | Show fallback, not blank screen |
+| NFR-014 | Data consistency | No corruption | Trust in system integrity |
+| NFR-015 | Click depth | Max 3 clicks to any function | Information accessibility |
+| NFR-016 | Learning curve | Basic use within 10 minutes | Demo walkthrough time |
+| NFR-017 | Terminology | 100% EN 15380 compliance | Railway domain credibility |
+| NFR-018 | Visual feedback | Action confirmation < 300ms | User confidence |
+| **P0** | Critical | Without this, demo fails | Must work flawlessly |
+| **P1** | Important | Significantly enhances impression | Should work if time permits |
+| **P2** | Nice-to-Have | Shows polish if present | Implement only if core is solid |
+в”ңв”Җв”Җ Does the client specifically expect this feature?
+в”ңв”Җв”Җ Does the demo fail without it?
+в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ No вҶ’ P1 or P2
+| Train Allocation | Single-day allocation workflow | One train to one service |
+| Km Balancing | Fleet-level visualization | Histogram, rankings, deltas |
+| Simulation | What-if scenario system | Create, run, compare |
+| Maintenance View | Read and reschedule | Gantt with drag-drop |
+| Map | Current positions | Train and depot markers |
+| Multi-Day Optimization | MILP solver for week/month | Complexity exceeds 3 months | Phase 2 |
+| Real EAM Integration | SAP PM, Maximo connections | Requires client environment | Phase 2 |
+| Disruption Management | Real-time failure handling | Different architecture | Phase 3 |
+| Crew Scheduling | Driver allocation | Doubles complexity | Phase 3 |
+| Native Mobile | iOS/Android apps | Responsive web sufficient | If demanded |
+| Multi-Language | UI translations | English for demo | Production |
+| User Authentication | Login, roles, permissions | Demo runs as single user | Production |
+| Audit Trail | Change history logging | Not visible in demo | Production |
+| Notification System | Email/SMS alerts | Requires infrastructure | Production |
+> "When in doubt, leave it out. It's better to demo 6 features that work perfectly than 12 features with bugs."
+The scope is intentionally constrained to ensure quality within the 12-week timeline. Any scope addition requires removing something of equal effort.
+The Dashboard is the entry point to MMS and must create an immediate positive impression while providing actionable information.
+| Click Action | Navigate to Fleet List |
+| Data Source | `WHERE status = 'pending' AND date >= TODAY` |
+| Alert Condition | Any service unassigned for today |
+| Data Source | `WHERE scheduled_date BETWEEN NOW AND NOW+7days` |
+| Click Action | Navigate to Maintenance Gantt |
+Traffic lights provide instant visual status without requiring interpretation.
+  direction: 'ascending' | 'descending'; // lower is better vs higher is better
+- Traffic lights appear in the top-right of each KPI card
+- On hover: Show tooltip explaining status
+- On click: Navigate to detail view for that metric

    19вҶ’## 1. System Overview
    20вҶ’
    21вҶ’### 1.1 Architecture Vision
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’MMS is a **demo-focused system** designed to win a competitive bid through a 15-minute presentation. The architecture prioritizes:
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’1. **Demo Stability** - 15 minutes without crashes
    26вҶ’2. **Visual Impact** - Fast rendering, smooth interactions
    27вҶ’3. **Domain Credibility** - Correct railway terminology, realistic behavior

    77вҶ’### 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
    80вҶ’|----------|--------|-----------|
    81вҶ’| Frontend Framework | Next.js 14 (App Router) | SSR for initial load, React ecosystem |
    82вҶ’| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid development, consistent design |
    83вҶ’| Backend Framework | Fastify | Performance, TypeScript support |
    84вҶ’| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | Reliable, JSON support, demo-proven |
    85вҶ’| Solver | Python + OR-Tools | Best-in-class optimization, justifications |
    86вҶ’| State Management | @zazcart/toolkit | Avoids React hook pitfalls |
    87вҶ’| API Style | REST | Simplicity for demo scope |
    88вҶ’

   101вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ page.tsx          # Train list
   102вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ [id]/page.tsx     # Train details
   103вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocation/
   104вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ page.tsx          # Service allocation
   105вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ analysis/
   106вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ page.tsx          # Km balancing, simulation
   107вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ schedule/
   108вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ page.tsx          # Maintenance Gantt
   109вҶ’в”Ӯ
   110вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/
   111вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ui/                   # shadcn/ui components
   112вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ dashboard/
   113вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ KPICard.tsx
   114вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ KPIGrid.tsx
   115вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ DepotCapacity.tsx
   116вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ map/
   117вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ FleetMap.tsx
   118вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ TrainMarker.tsx
   119вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ DepotMarker.tsx
   120вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocation/
   121вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ServiceList.tsx
   122вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ RecommendationCard.tsx
   123вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ JustificationPanel.tsx
   124вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ analysis/
   125вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ DistributionChart.tsx
   126вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ ScenarioComparison.tsx
   127вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ TrainRanking.tsx
   128вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ gantt/
   129вҶ’в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ MaintenanceGantt.tsx
   130вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ GanttBlock.tsx
   131вҶ’в”Ӯ
   132вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ lib/
   133вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ api.ts                # API client
   134вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ types.ts              # TypeScript interfaces
   135вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ utils.ts              # Utilities
   136вҶ’в”Ӯ
   137вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ stores/                   # @zazcart/toolkit state
   138вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ fleetStore.ts
   139вҶ’    в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocationStore.ts
   140вҶ’    в””в”Җв”Җ simulationStore.ts
   141вҶ’```
   142вҶ’
   143вҶ’### 2.2 Backend Structure
   144вҶ’
   145вҶ’```
   146вҶ’server/
   147вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
   148вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ index.ts              # Fastify server entry
   149вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ routes/
   150вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ dashboard.ts      # GET /api/dashboard/*
   151вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ trains.ts         # GET/POST /api/trains/*
   152вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ depots.ts         # GET /api/depots/*
   153вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ services.ts       # GET /api/services/*
   154вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocations.ts    # GET/POST /api/allocations/*
   155вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ simulation.ts     # POST /api/simulation/*
   156вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
   157вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ services/
   158вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ trainService.ts
   159вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ depotService.ts
   160вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocationService.ts
   161вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ simulationService.ts
   162вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ solverClient.ts   # HTTP client to Python solver
   163вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
   164вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ db/
   165вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ client.ts         # PostgreSQL client (pg or drizzle)
   166вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ schema.ts         # Table definitions
   167вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ seed.ts           # Seed data loader
   168вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
   169вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ types/
   170вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ index.ts          # Shared types
   171вҶ’в”Ӯ
   172вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ package.json
   173вҶ’```
   174вҶ’
   175вҶ’### 2.3 Solver Structure
   176вҶ’
   177вҶ’```
   178вҶ’solver/
   179вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/
   180вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.py               # FastAPI server
   181вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ models/
   182вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ train.py          # Train dataclass
   183вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ service.py        # Service dataclass
   184вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ recommendation.py # Output dataclass
   185вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
   186вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ engine/
   187вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ allocator.py      # CP-SAT allocation model
   188вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ scorer.py         # Train scoring logic
   189вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ justifier.py      # Justification generator
   190вҶ’в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ
   191вҶ’в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ api/
   192вҶ’в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ routes.py         # POST /recommend, POST /simulate
   193вҶ’в”Ӯ
   194вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt
   195вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җ Dockerfile
   196вҶ’```
   197вҶ’
   198вҶ’---
   199вҶ’
   200вҶ’## 3. Data Architecture
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’### 3.1 Database Schema
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’```sql
   205вҶ’-- Core entities
   206вҶ’CREATE TABLE fleets (
   207вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   208вҶ’    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   209вҶ’    manufacturer VARCHAR(50),
   210вҶ’    pm1_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   211вҶ’    pm2_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   212вҶ’    hm_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   213вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   214вҶ’);
   215вҶ’
   216вҶ’CREATE TABLE depots (
   217вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   218вҶ’    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   219вҶ’    code VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
   220вҶ’    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   221вҶ’    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   222вҶ’    capacity_tracks INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
   223вҶ’    capacity_manhours INTEGER DEFAULT 80,
   224вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   225вҶ’);
   226вҶ’
   227вҶ’CREATE TABLE trains (
   228вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   229вҶ’    train_id VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- Business ID: UTE-3400-012
   230вҶ’    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
   231вҶ’    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   232вҶ’    current_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   233вҶ’    target_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
   234вҶ’    fitness_status VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'fit' CHECK (fitness_status IN ('fit', 'unfit')),
   235вҶ’    last_maintenance_date DATE,
   236вҶ’    next_maintenance_km INTEGER,
   237вҶ’    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   238вҶ’    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
   239вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   240вҶ’    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   241вҶ’);
   242вҶ’
   243вҶ’CREATE TABLE services (
   244вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   245вҶ’    service_code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- IC 501
   246вҶ’    origin VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   247вҶ’    destination VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
   248вҶ’    distance_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
   249вҶ’    departure_time TIME NOT NULL,
   250вҶ’    arrival_time TIME NOT NULL,
   251вҶ’    service_type VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'regional',  -- regional, intercity
   252вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   253вҶ’);
   254вҶ’
   255вҶ’CREATE TABLE allocations (
   256вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   257вҶ’    service_id UUID REFERENCES services(id),
   258вҶ’    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   259вҶ’    allocation_date DATE NOT NULL,
   260вҶ’    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
   261вҶ’    score INTEGER,
   262вҶ’    justifications JSONB,  -- Array of reason strings
   263вҶ’    created_by VARCHAR(100),
   264вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
   265вҶ’    UNIQUE(service_id, allocation_date)
   266вҶ’);
   267вҶ’
   268вҶ’-- Mileage history for trends
   269вҶ’CREATE TABLE mileage_history (
   270вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   271вҶ’    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   272вҶ’    recorded_date DATE NOT NULL,
   273вҶ’    km_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
   274вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   275вҶ’);
   276вҶ’
   277вҶ’-- Maintenance schedules
   278вҶ’CREATE TABLE maintenance_schedules (
   279вҶ’    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
   280вҶ’    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
   281вҶ’    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
   282вҶ’    maintenance_type VARCHAR(10) CHECK (maintenance_type IN ('PM1', 'PM2', 'HM', 'OVH')),
   283вҶ’    scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
   284вҶ’    duration_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   285вҶ’    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'scheduled' CHECK (status IN ('scheduled', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
   286вҶ’    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
   287вҶ’);
   288вҶ’
   289вҶ’-- Indexes for performance
   290вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
   291вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(base_depot_id);
   292вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_date ON allocations(allocation_date);
   293вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_status ON allocations(status);
   294вҶ’CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_history(train_id, recorded_date);
   295вҶ’```
   296вҶ’
   297вҶ’### 3.2 Data Flow
   298вҶ’
   299вҶ’```
   300вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ     в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   301вҶ’в”Ӯ   Frontend  в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ   API       в”Ӯв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–¶в”Ӯ  Database   в”Ӯ
   302вҶ’в”Ӯ   (React)   в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ  (Fastify)  в”Ӯв—Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ӯ (PostgreSQL)в”Ӯ
   303вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ     в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   304вҶ’                           в”Ӯ
   305вҶ’                           в–ј
   306вҶ’                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   307вҶ’                   в”Ӯ   Solver    в”Ӯ
   308вҶ’                   в”Ӯ  (Python)   в”Ӯ
   309вҶ’                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   310вҶ’```
   311вҶ’
   312вҶ’**Read Path (Dashboard):**
   313вҶ’1. Frontend requests `/api/dashboard/summary`
   314вҶ’2. API queries aggregated KPIs from DB
   315вҶ’3. Response cached in Redis (60s TTL)
   316вҶ’4. Frontend renders immediately
   317вҶ’
   318вҶ’**Write Path (Allocation):**
   319вҶ’1. User selects service, clicks "Recommend"
   320вҶ’2. Frontend calls `POST /api/allocations/recommend`
   321вҶ’3. API calls Solver service with service details
   322вҶ’4. Solver returns ranked recommendations with justifications
   323вҶ’5. User confirms, API writes to allocations table
   324вҶ’6. Frontend updates optimistically
   325вҶ’
   326вҶ’---
   327вҶ’
   328вҶ’## 4. API Specification
   329вҶ’
   330вҶ’### 4.1 REST Endpoints
   331вҶ’
   332вҶ’#### Dashboard
   333вҶ’```
   334вҶ’GET /api/dashboard/summary
   335вҶ’Response: {
   336вҶ’  totalTrains: number,
   337вҶ’  availableTrains: number,
   338вҶ’  inMaintenanceCount: number,
   339вҶ’  kmVariance: number,
   340вҶ’  varianceStatus: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red'
   341вҶ’}
   342вҶ’
   343вҶ’GET /api/dashboard/depot-capacity
   344вҶ’Response: {
   345вҶ’  depots: [{
   346вҶ’    id: string,
   347вҶ’    name: string,
   348вҶ’    code: string,
   349вҶ’    currentOccupancy: number,
   350вҶ’    maxCapacity: number,
   351вҶ’    percentUsed: number
   352вҶ’  }]
   353вҶ’}
   354вҶ’```
   355вҶ’
   356вҶ’#### Trains
   357вҶ’```
   358вҶ’GET /api/trains
   359вҶ’Query: ?fleet=uuid&depot=uuid&status=fit
   360вҶ’Response: { trains: Train[] }
   361вҶ’
   362вҶ’GET /api/trains/:id
   363вҶ’Response: Train
   364вҶ’
   365вҶ’GET /api/trains/:id/history
   366вҶ’Response: { history: MileagePoint[] }
   367вҶ’```
   368вҶ’
   369вҶ’#### Allocation
   370вҶ’```
   371вҶ’GET /api/services/pending
   372вҶ’Query: ?date=2025-01-15
   373вҶ’Response: { services: Service[] }
   374вҶ’
   375вҶ’POST /api/allocations/recommend
   376вҶ’Body: { serviceId: string, date: string }
   377вҶ’Response: {
   378вҶ’  recommendations: [{
   379вҶ’    trainId: string,
   380вҶ’    score: number,
   381вҶ’    justifications: string[]
   382вҶ’  }],
   383вҶ’  solverTimeMs: number
   384вҶ’}
   385вҶ’
   386вҶ’POST /api/allocations
   387вҶ’Body: { serviceId: string, trainId: string, date: string }
   388вҶ’Response: { allocation: Allocation }
   389вҶ’```
   390вҶ’
   391вҶ’#### Analysis
   392вҶ’```
   393вҶ’GET /api/balancing/distribution
   394вҶ’Query: ?fleet=uuid
   395вҶ’Response: {
   396вҶ’  average: number,
   397вҶ’  variance: number,
   398вҶ’  distribution: { kmRange: string, count: number }[],
   399вҶ’  outliers: { trainId: string, delta: number }[]
   400вҶ’}

# Architecture Decision Document - MMS Metro Mileage Management System

_This document defines the technical architecture for the MMS railway fleet management demo system._

---

## 1. System Overview

### 1.1 Architecture Vision

MMS is a **demo-focused system** designed to win a competitive bid through a 15-minute presentation. The architecture prioritizes:

1. **Demo Stability** - 15 minutes without crashes
2. **Visual Impact** - Fast rendering, smooth interactions
3. **Domain Credibility** - Correct railway terminology, realistic behavior
4. **Technical Proof** - Working solver, real-time recommendations

### 1.2 High-Level Architecture

```
в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
в”Ӯ                         BROWSER                                  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ                    Next.js 14 App                        в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”ӮDashboard в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   Map    в”Ӯ в”ӮAllocationв”Ӯ в”Ӯ Analysis в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  Page    в”Ӯ в”ӮComponent в”Ӯ в”Ӯ  Page    в”Ӯ в”Ӯ   Page   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ

### 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions

| Decision | Choice | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-----------|
| Frontend Framework | Next.js 14 (App Router) | SSR for initial load, React ecosystem |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui | Rapid development, consistent design |
| Backend Framework | Fastify | Performance, TypeScript support |
| Database | PostgreSQL 16 | Reliable, JSON support, demo-proven |
| Solver | Python + OR-Tools | Best-in-class optimization, justifications |
| State Management | @zazcart/toolkit | Avoids React hook pitfalls |
| API Style | REST | Simplicity for demo scope |

## 3. Data Architecture

### 3.1 Database Schema

```sql
-- Core entities
CREATE TABLE fleets (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    manufacturer VARCHAR(50),
    pm1_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    pm2_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    hm_interval_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE depots (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    code VARCHAR(10) UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    capacity_tracks INTEGER DEFAULT 5,
    capacity_manhours INTEGER DEFAULT 80,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE trains (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- Business ID: UTE-3400-012
    fleet_id UUID REFERENCES fleets(id),
    base_depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    current_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    target_km INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    fitness_status VARCHAR(10) DEFAULT 'fit' CHECK (fitness_status IN ('fit', 'unfit')),
    last_maintenance_date DATE,
    next_maintenance_km INTEGER,
    latitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    longitude DECIMAL(10, 6),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    updated_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE services (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    service_code VARCHAR(20) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- IC 501
    origin VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    destination VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    distance_km INTEGER NOT NULL,
    departure_time TIME NOT NULL,
    arrival_time TIME NOT NULL,
    service_type VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'regional',  -- regional, intercity
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE TABLE allocations (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    service_id UUID REFERENCES services(id),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    allocation_date DATE NOT NULL,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'pending' CHECK (status IN ('pending', 'confirmed', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
    score INTEGER,
    justifications JSONB,  -- Array of reason strings
    created_by VARCHAR(100),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW(),
    UNIQUE(service_id, allocation_date)
);

-- Mileage history for trends
CREATE TABLE mileage_history (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    recorded_date DATE NOT NULL,
    km_value INTEGER NOT NULL,
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Maintenance schedules
CREATE TABLE maintenance_schedules (
    id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    train_id UUID REFERENCES trains(id),
    depot_id UUID REFERENCES depots(id),
    maintenance_type VARCHAR(10) CHECK (maintenance_type IN ('PM1', 'PM2', 'HM', 'OVH')),
    scheduled_date DATE NOT NULL,
    duration_days INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
    status VARCHAR(20) DEFAULT 'scheduled' CHECK (status IN ('scheduled', 'in_progress', 'completed', 'cancelled')),
    created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Indexes for performance
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_fleet ON trains(fleet_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_trains_depot ON trains(base_depot_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_date ON allocations(allocation_date);
CREATE INDEX idx_allocations_status ON allocations(status);
CREATE INDEX idx_mileage_train_date ON mileage_history(train_id, recorded_date);
```

                           в–ј
                   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
                   в”Ӯ   Solver    в”Ӯ
                   в”Ӯ  (Python)   в”Ӯ
                   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
```

**Read Path (Dashboard):**
1. Frontend requests `/api/dashboard/summary`
2. API queries aggregated KPIs from DB
3. Response cached in Redis (60s TTL)
4. Frontend renders immediately

**Write Path (Allocation):**
1. User selects service, clicks "Recommend"
2. Frontend calls `POST /api/allocations/recommend`
3. API calls Solver service with service details
4. Solver returns ranked recommendations with justifications
5. User confirms, API writes to allocations table
6. Frontend updates optimistically

---

## 4. API Specification

### 4.1 REST Endpoints

#### Dashboard
```
GET /api/dashboard/summary
Response: {
  totalTrains: number,
  availableTrains: number,
  inMaintenanceCount: number,
  kmVariance: number,
  varianceStatus: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red'
}

GET /api/dashboard/depot-capacity
Response: {
  depots: [{
    id: string,
    name: string,
    code: string,
    currentOccupancy: number,
    maxCapacity: number,
    percentUsed: number
  }]
}
```

#### Trains
```
GET /api/trains
Query: ?fleet=uuid&depot=uuid&status=fit
Response: { trains: Train[] }

GET /api/trains/:id
Response: Train

GET /api/trains/:id/history
Response: { history: MileagePoint[] }
```

#### Allocation
```
GET /api/services/pending
Query: ?date=2025-01-15
Response: { services: Service[] }

POST /api/allocations/recommend
Body: { serviceId: string, date: string }
Response: {
  recommendations: [{
    trainId: string,
    score: number,
    justifications: string[]
  }],
  solverTimeMs: number
}

POST /api/allocations
Body: { serviceId: string, trainId: string, date: string }
Response: { allocation: Allocation }
```

#### Analysis
```
GET /api/balancing/distribution
Query: ?fleet=uuid
Response: {
  average: number,
  variance: number,
  distribution: { kmRange: string, count: number }[],
  outliers: { trainId: string, delta: number }[]
}

   440вҶ’## 5. Technology Decisions
   441вҶ’
   442вҶ’### 5.1 Frontend Stack
   443вҶ’

   445вҶ’|------------|---------|-------------------|

   455вҶ’### 5.2 Backend Stack

   478вҶ’## 6. Integration Architecture
   479вҶ’
   480вҶ’### 6.1 Frontend вҶ” Backend
   481вҶ’
   482вҶ’```
   483вҶ’[Next.js App]
   484вҶ’     в”Ӯ
   485вҶ’     в”Ӯ  fetch('/api/...')
   486вҶ’     в”Ӯ  Headers: Content-Type: application/json
   487вҶ’     в–ј
   488вҶ’[Fastify Server]
   489вҶ’     в”Ӯ
   490вҶ’     в”Ӯ  Route handlers call services
   491вҶ’     в–ј
   492вҶ’[Service Layer]
   493вҶ’     в”Ӯ
   494вҶ’     в”Ӯ  Drizzle ORM queries
   495вҶ’     в–ј
   496вҶ’[PostgreSQL]
   497вҶ’```
   498вҶ’
   499вҶ’**Error Handling:**
   500вҶ’- API returns standardized error format: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`
   501вҶ’- Frontend uses try/catch with toast notifications
   502вҶ’- Network errors show retry option
   503вҶ’
   504вҶ’### 6.2 Backend вҶ” Solver
   505вҶ’
   506вҶ’```
   507вҶ’[Fastify Service]
   508вҶ’     в”Ӯ
   509вҶ’     в”Ӯ  HTTP POST (axios/fetch)
   510вҶ’     в”Ӯ  Timeout: 30 seconds
   511вҶ’     в–ј
   512вҶ’[Python FastAPI]
   513вҶ’     в”Ӯ
   514вҶ’     в”Ӯ  CP-SAT model execution
   515вҶ’     в–ј

   518вҶ’
   519вҶ’**Resilience:**
   520вҶ’- Solver timeout: 30 seconds hard limit

   522вҶ’- Cache: Store recent recommendations in Redis (5 min TTL)
   523вҶ’

   526вҶ’## 7. Non-Functional Architecture
   527вҶ’
   528вҶ’### 7.1 Performance

   530вҶ’| Metric | Target | Implementation |

   537вҶ’### 7.2 Caching Strategy
   538вҶ’
   539вҶ’```
   540вҶ’Redis Cache:
   541вҶ’в”ңв”Җв”Җ dashboard:summary (TTL: 60s)

   664вҶ’---

   666вҶ’## 11. AI Agent Guidelines
   667вҶ’
   668вҶ’### 11.1 Code Patterns
   669вҶ’
   670вҶ’**DO:**
   671вҶ’- Use TypeScript strict mode
   672вҶ’- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
   673вҶ’- Use @zazcart/toolkit for frontend state
   674вҶ’- Use shadcn/ui components
   675вҶ’- Follow existing file naming conventions
   676вҶ’- Add JSDoc comments for public functions
   677вҶ’
   678вҶ’**DON'T:**
   679вҶ’- Use useState for application state (use toolkit)
   680вҶ’- Create new component libraries
   681вҶ’- Add authentication logic
   682вҶ’- Implement features outside PRD scope
   683вҶ’- Add time estimates to code comments
   684вҶ’
   685вҶ’### 11.2 File Naming
   686вҶ’

   688вҶ’Components: PascalCase.tsx (e.g., TrainCard.tsx)

### 4.2 Solver API (Internal)

```
POST /recommend
Body: {
  service: { id, distance_km, origin, destination },
  candidates: Train[],

    prioritize_balancing: boolean,

---

## 5. Technology Decisions

### 5.1 Frontend Stack

| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
|------------|---------|-------------------|
| Next.js | 14.x | App Router, RSC, SSR |
| React | 18.x | Concurrent features, suspense |
| TypeScript | 5.x | Type safety, IDE support |
| Tailwind CSS | 3.x | Utility-first, rapid prototyping |
| shadcn/ui | latest | Pre-built accessible components |
| @zazcart/toolkit | local | State management without React hook issues |
| Recharts | 2.x | Simple charting, React integration |
| Mapbox GL / Leaflet | latest | Interactive maps |

### 5.2 Backend Stack

| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
|------------|---------|-------------------|
| Node.js | 20.x LTS | Stable, TypeScript support |
| Fastify | 4.x | Performance, schema validation |
| TypeScript | 5.x | Shared types with frontend |
| PostgreSQL | 16.x | JSONB, reliability |
| Redis | 7.x | Caching, session storage |
| Drizzle ORM | latest | Type-safe SQL, migrations |

### 5.3 Solver Stack

| Technology | Version | Decision Rationale |
|------------|---------|-------------------|
| Python | 3.11 | OR-Tools compatibility |
| FastAPI | 0.100+ | Async, auto-docs |
| OR-Tools | 9.x | Google's optimization suite |
| Pydantic | 2.x | Data validation |
| uvicorn | latest | ASGI server |

---

## 6. Integration Architecture

### 6.1 Frontend вҶ” Backend

```
[Next.js App]
     в”Ӯ
     в”Ӯ  fetch('/api/...')
     в”Ӯ  Headers: Content-Type: application/json
     в–ј
[Fastify Server]
     в”Ӯ
     в”Ӯ  Route handlers call services
     в–ј
[Service Layer]
     в”Ӯ
     в”Ӯ  Drizzle ORM queries
     в–ј
[PostgreSQL]
```

**Error Handling:**
- API returns standardized error format: `{ error: string, code: string, details?: object }`
- Frontend uses try/catch with toast notifications
- Network errors show retry option

### 6.2 Backend вҶ” Solver

```
[Fastify Service]
     в”Ӯ
     в”Ӯ  HTTP POST (axios/fetch)
     в”Ӯ  Timeout: 30 seconds
     в–ј
[Python FastAPI]
     в”Ӯ
     в”Ӯ  CP-SAT model execution
     в–ј
[Response with justifications]
```

**Resilience:**
- Solver timeout: 30 seconds hard limit
- Fallback: Return top 3 trains by simple scoring if solver fails
- Cache: Store recent recommendations in Redis (5 min TTL)

---

## 7. Non-Functional Architecture

### 7.1 Performance

| Metric | Target | Implementation |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| Dashboard load | < 2s | SSR + cache |
| API response | < 500ms | Index optimization |
| Map render | < 2s | Tile caching, clustering |
| Solver response | < 30s | CP-SAT with timeout |

### 7.2 Caching Strategy

```
Redis Cache:
в”ңв”Җв”Җ dashboard:summary (TTL: 60s)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ trains:list (TTL: 30s)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ depots:list (TTL: 300s)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver:recommend:{hash} (TTL: 300s)
в””в”Җв”Җ session:{id} (TTL: 3600s)
```

### 7.3 Demo Stability

| Scenario | Mitigation |
|----------|------------|
| Database down | Pre-loaded fallback JSON |
| Solver timeout | Simple ranking fallback |
| Network issue | Service worker cache |
| UI crash | Error boundary with retry |

---

## 8. Development Setup

### 8.1 Monorepo Structure

```
mms/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json              # Root package (workspaces)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ web/                  # Next.js frontend
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ api/                  # Fastify backend
в”ңв”Җв”Җ packages/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ types/                # Shared TypeScript types
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ utils/                # Shared utilities
в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver/                   # Python solver (separate)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ docker-compose.yml        # Local dev environment
в””в”Җв”Җ scripts/
    в”ңв”Җв”Җ seed.ts               # Database seeding
    в””в”Җв”Җ dev.sh                # Start all services
```

### 8.2 Local Development

```bash
# Start infrastructure
docker-compose up -d postgres redis

# Start API
cd apps/api && bun run dev

# Start Solver
cd solver && uvicorn src.main:app --reload

# Start Frontend
cd apps/web && bun run dev
```

### 8.3 Environment Variables

```env
# Database
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://mms:mms@localhost:5432/mms

# Redis
REDIS_URL=redis://localhost:6379

# Solver
SOLVER_URL=http://localhost:8000

# Mapbox (optional, can use Leaflet)
NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN=pk.xxx
```

---

## 9. Security Considerations (Demo Scope)

### 9.1 Demo Simplifications

| Production Need | Demo Approach |
|-----------------|---------------|
| Authentication | None (demo mode) |
| Authorization | None (single user) |
| HTTPS | HTTP for local dev |
| Input validation | Basic schema validation |
| Rate limiting | None |

### 9.2 Data Protection

- No real customer data (seed data only)
- No PII in seed data
- Demo reset between presentations

---

### 10.2 Demo Reset Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# Reset demo to clean state
docker-compose down -v
docker-compose up -d
sleep 5
bun run seed
echo "Demo ready!"
```

---

## 11. AI Agent Guidelines

### 11.1 Code Patterns

**DO:**
- Use TypeScript strict mode
- Use Drizzle ORM for database access
- Use @zazcart/toolkit for frontend state
- Use shadcn/ui components
- Follow existing file naming conventions
- Add JSDoc comments for public functions

**DON'T:**
- Use useState for application state (use toolkit)
- Create new component libraries
- Add authentication logic
- Implement features outside PRD scope
- Add time estimates to code comments

### 11.2 File Naming

```
Components: PascalCase.tsx (e.g., TrainCard.tsx)
Utilities: camelCase.ts (e.g., formatKm.ts)
Routes: lowercase/route.ts (e.g., trains/route.ts)
Types: PascalCase.ts (e.g., Train.ts)
```

### 11.3 Testing Priority

1. Solver logic (unit tests)
2. API endpoints (integration tests)
3. Critical UI flows (Playwright)

development | | Blueprint.js | 5.x | Components | Enterprise UI, dark mode | | @zazcart/toolkit | local | State | Avoids React hook pitfalls | | Recharts | 2.x | Charts | Simple API, React integration | | Mapbox GL JS | 3.x | Maps | Performance, dark mode style | **Backend Stack** | Technology | Version | Purpose | Decision Rationale | |------------|---------|---------|-------------------| | Node.js | 20.x LTS | Runtime | Stability, TypeScript support | | Fastify | 4.x | Framework | Performance, schema validation | | TypeScript | 5.x | Language | Shared types with frontend | | Drizzle ORM | latest | Database | Type-safe SQL, migrations | | PostgreSQL | 16.x | Database | Reliable, JSONB support | | Redis | 7.x | Cache | Performance, session storage | **Solver Stack** | Technology | Version | Purpose | Decision Rationale | |------------|---------|---------|-------------------| | Python | 3.11 | Runtime | OR-Tools compatibility | | FastAPI | 0.100+ | Framework | Async, auto-documentation | | OR-Tools | 9.x | Solver | Google's optimization suite | | Pydantic | 2.x | Validation | Data models, type hints | | uvicorn | latest | Server | ASGI, performance | #### 16.3 Monorepo Structure ``` mms-mfc/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ package.json # Root package (workspaces) в”ңв”Җв”Җ bun.lockb # Bun lock file в”ңв”Җв”Җ turbo.json # Turborepo config в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ apps/ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ web/ # Next.js frontend в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ app/ # App Router pages в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/ # React components в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ lib/ # Utilities в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ stores/ # State management в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ api/ # Fastify backend в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ routes/ # API routes в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ services/ # Business logic в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ db/ # Database layer в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ package.json в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ packages/ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ types/ # Shared TypeScript types в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ train.ts в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ service.ts в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ index.ts в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ package.json в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ utils/ # Shared utilities в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ format.ts в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ validation.ts в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ package.json в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ solver/ # Python solver (standalone) в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ src/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.py в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ engine/ в”Ӯ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ api/ в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ requirements.txt в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ Dockerfile в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җ docker-compose.yml # Local development в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җ scripts/ в”ңв”Җв”Җ seed.ts # Database seeding в””в”Җв”Җ dev.sh # Start all services ``` #### 16.4 Service Communication **Frontend вҶ” Backend** ``` Protocol: HTTP/REST Format: JSON Authentication: None (demo mode) Base URL: http://localhost:4000/api Request Headers: Content-Type: application/json Accept: application/json Response Format: Success: { data: T, meta?: object } Error: { error: string, code: string, details?: object } ``` **Backend вҶ” Solver** ``` Protocol: HTTP/REST Format: JSON Timeout: 30 seconds Base URL: http://localhost:8000 Retry Policy: Max retries: 2 Backoff: 500ms, 1000ms Fallback: Simple scoring if solver unavailable ``` **Backend вҶ” Database** ``` Protocol: PostgreSQL wire protocol ORM: Drizzle (type-safe SQL) Connection Pool: 10 connections Timeout: 5 seconds per query ``` **Backend вҶ” Redis** ``` Protocol: Redis protocol Purpose: Caching, session storage TTL Strategy: - Dashboard data: 60 seconds - Train list: 30 seconds - Solver results: 300 seconds - Static data: 1 hour ``` #### 16.5 Key Architectural Decisions **ADR-001: Next.js App Router over Pages Router** - **Decision:** Use App Router (app/ directory) - **Context:** Next.js 14 offers App Router with React Server Components - **Rationale:** Better data fetching, layout nesting, streaming SSR - **Consequences:** Some learning curve, but better long-term architecture **ADR-002: Fastify over Express** - **Decision:** Use Fastify as API framework - **Context:** Need performant, type-safe backend - **Rationale:** 2-3x faster than Express, built-in schema validation, TypeScript support - **Consequences:** Slightly different plugin system, excellent performance **ADR-003: @zazcart/toolkit over React useState** - **Decision:** Use toolkit state machine for application state - **Context:** Complex state with animations, real-time updates - **Rationale:** Avoids stale closure issues, better debugging, predictable state - **Consequences:** Learning curve, but eliminates common React bugs **ADR-004: Blueprint.js over shadcn/ui** - **Decision:** Use Blueprint.js for UI components - **Context:** Need enterprise-grade components with dark mode - **Rationale:** Built-in dark mode, table/tree components, consistent design - **Consequences:** Larger bundle, but comprehensive component library **ADR-005: Drizzle ORM over Prisma** - **Decision:** Use Drizzle for database access - **Context:** Need type-safe, performant database layer - **Rationale:** SQL-like syntax, better performance, smaller bundle - **Consequences:** More manual work, but more control **ADR-006: Separate Python Solver** - **Decision:** Run solver as separate service, not Node.js library - **Context:** Need OR-Tools for optimization - **Rationale:** OR-Tools is Python-native, best performance - **Consequences:** Extra service to deploy, HTTP overhead acceptable --- ### Chapter matching filename export function KPICard(props: KPICardProps) { ... } // Types co-located with component interface KPICardProps { title: string; value: number | string; subtitle?: string; status?: 'green' | 'yellow' | 'red'; onClick?: () => void; } ``` **Component Structure Pattern** ```typescript // Standard component structure import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'; import { Card } from '@blueprintjs/core'; interface Props { // Props interface } export function ComponentName({ prop1, prop2 }: Props) { // 1. Hooks (store selectors, effects) const data = useSelector(selectData); // 2. Derived state const computedValue = useMemo(() => ..., [data]); // 3. Event handlers const handleClick = useCallback(() => ..., []); // 4. Render return ( {/* JSX */} ); } ``` **Component Categories** | Category | Purpose | Example | |----------|---------|---------| | Layout | Page structure | `Navbar`, `Sidebar` | | Container | Data fetching, state | `DashboardContainer` | | Presentational | Pure UI | `KPICard`, `TrainMarker` | | Interactive | User actions | `ServiceList`, `GanttBlock` | | Composite | Multiple components | `RecommendationPanel` | #### 17.3 State Management (@zazcart/toolkit) **Store Structure** ```typescript // stores/fleetStore.ts import { createMachine, useMachine, useSelector } from '@zazcart/toolkit'; interface FleetState { trains: Train[]; depots: Depot[]; selectedTrainId: string | null; loading: boolean; error: string | null; } type FleetEvent = | { type: 'FETCH_TRAINS' } | { type: 'FETCH_SUCCESS'; trains: Train[] } | { type: 'FETCH_ERROR'; error: string } | { type: 'SELECT_TRAIN'; trainId: string }; const fleetMachine = createMachine({ initial: 'idle', context: { trains: [], depots: [], selectedTrainId: null, loading: false, error: null, }, states: { idle: { on: { FETCH_TRAINS: { target: 'loading', actions: ['setLoading'], }, }, }, loading: { on: { FETCH_SUCCESS: { target: 'idle', actions: ['setTrains', 'clearLoading'], }, FETCH_ERROR: { target: 'error', actions: ['setError'], }, }, }, error: { on: { FETCH_TRAINS: { target: 'loading', actions: ['setLoading', 'clearError'], }, }, }, }, }); // Selectors export const selectTrains = (state: FleetState) => state.trains; export const selectSelectedTrain = (state: FleetState) => state.trains.find(t => t.id === state.selectedTrainId); // Hook export function useFleetStore() { return useMachine(fleetMachine); } ``` **Usage in Components** ```typescript // components/fleet/TrainList.tsx import { useFleetStore, selectTrains } from '@/stores/fleetStore'; export function TrainList() { const { state, send } = useFleetStore(); const trains = useSelector(selectTrains); useEffect(() => { send({ type: 'FETCH_TRAINS' }); }, [send]); if (state.loading) return ; if (state.error) return ; return (
{trains.map(train => ( send({ type: 'SELECT_TRAIN', trainId: train.id })} /> ))}
); } ``` #### 17.4 API Client Pattern **Centralized API Client** ```typescript // lib/api.ts const API_BASE = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'http://localhost:4000/api'; interface ApiResponse { data: T; meta?: { total?: number; page?: number; }; } interface ApiError { error: string; code: string; details?: Record; } async function request( endpoint: string, options: RequestInit = {} ): Promise { const url = `${API_BASE}${endpoint}`; const response = await fetch(url, { ...options, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json', ...options.headers, }, }); if (!response.ok) { const error: ApiError = await response.json(); throw new Error(error.error || 'API Error'); } const result: ApiResponse = await response.json(); return result.data; } // Typed API methods export const api = { // Dashboard getDashboardSummary: () => request('/dashboard/summary'), getDepotCapacity: (date?: string) => request(`/dashboard/depot-capacity${date ? `?date=${date}` : ''}`), // Trains getTrains: (params?: TrainFilters) => request(`/trains${toQueryString(params)}`), getTrain: (id: string) => request(`/trains/${id}`), getTrainHistory: (id: string, months?: number) => request(`/trains/${id}/history?months=${months || 6}`), // Services & Allocation getPendingServices: (date: string) => request(`/services/pending?date=${date}`), getRecommendations: (serviceId: string, date: string) => request('/allocations/recommend', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ serviceId, date }), }), createAllocation: (data: AllocationRequest) => request('/allocations', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data), }), // Balancing getDistribution: (fleetId?: string) => request(`/balancing/distribution${fleetId ? `?fleet=${fleetId}` : ''}`), getOutliers: () => request('/balancing/outliers'), // Simulation createScenario: (name: string) => request('/scenarios', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ name }), }), runSimulation: (scenarioId: string, adjustments: Adjustment[]) => request(`/scenarios/${scenarioId}/simulate`, { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ adjustments }), }), // Maintenance getMaintenanceSchedule: (startDate: string, endDate: string) => request(`/maintenance?start=${startDate}&end=${endDate}`), rescheduleMaintenace: (id: string, newDate: string) => request(`/maintenance/${id}`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify({ scheduledDate: newDate }), }), }; ``` #### 17.5 Blueprint.js Integration **Theme Configuration** ```typescript // components/layout/ThemeProvider.tsx import { Classes, Colors } from '@blueprintjs/core'; import '@blueprintjs/core/lib/css/blueprint.css'; import '@blueprintjs/icons/lib/css/blueprint-icons.css'; export function ThemeProvider({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) { return (
{children}
); } ``` **Component Usage Pattern** ```typescript // Using Blueprint.js components import { Card, Elevation, Button, Intent, Tag, Spinner, NonIdealState, Tooltip, } from '@blueprintjs/core'; export function KPICard({ title, value, status, onClick }: KPICardProps) { return (
{title} {status}
{value}
 ); } function statusToIntent(status?: string): Intent { switch (status) { case 'green': return Intent.SUCCESS; case 'yellow': return Intent.WARNING; case 'red': return Intent.DANGER; default: return Intent.NONE; } } ``` **Blueprint.js Components Used** | Component | Use Case | |-----------|----------| | `Card` | KPI cards, panels, popups | | `Table` | Train list, rankings | | `Button` | Actions, navigation | | `Tag` | Status badges | | `Spinner` | Loading states | | `Tooltip` | Help text, details | | `Dialog` | Confirmation modals | | `Toast` | Notifications | | `NonIdealState` | Empty/error states | | `Menu` | Dropdown menus | | `Tabs` | Section navigation | #### 17.6 Styling Strategy **Tailwind + Blueprint.js Integration** ```css /* globals.css */ @tailwind base; --mms-yellow: #ffbf00; --mms-red: #e63946; --mms-blue: #2196f3; --mms-orange: #ff9800; /* Maintenance Type Colors */ --pm1-color: #4fc3f7; --pm2-color: #1976d2; --hm-color: #e53935; --ovh-color: #7b1fa2; /* Dark Mode Surfaces */ --surface-0: #1c2127; --surface-1: #252a31; --surface-2: #2f343c; --surface-3: #383e47; } ``` --- ### Chapter 18: Backend Architecture This chapter details the Fastify API implementation patterns. #### 18.1 Fastify Server Structure ```typescript // apps/api/src/index.ts import Fastify from 'fastify'; import cors from '@fastify/cors'; import { dashboardRoutes } from './routes/dashboard'; import { trainRoutes } from './routes/trains'; import { allocationRoutes } from './routes/allocations'; import { simulationRoutes } from './routes/simulation'; import { maintenanceRoutes } from './routes/maintenance'; import { db } from './db/client'; const server = Fastify({ logger: true, }); // Plugins await server.register(cors, { origin: ['http://localhost:3000'], }); // Database connection server.decorate('db', db); // Routes server.register(dashboardRoutes, { prefix: '/api/dashboard' }); server.register(trainRoutes, { prefix: '/api/trains' }); server.register(allocationRoutes, { prefix: '/api/allocations' }); server.register(simulationRoutes, { prefix: '/api/simulation' }); server.register(maintenanceRoutes, { prefix: '/api/maintenance' }); // Health check server.get('/health', async () => ({ status: 'ok' })); // Start server const start = async () => { try { await server.listen({ port: 4000, host: '0.0.0.0' }); console.log('Server running on http://localhost:4000'); } catch (err) { server.log.error(err); process.exit(1); } }; start(); ``` #### 18.2 Route Organization **Route File Pattern** ```typescript // apps/api/src/routes/trains.ts import { FastifyPluginAsync } from 'fastify'; import { TrainService } from '../services/trainService'; import { TrainFilters, TrainResponse } from '../types'; export const trainRoutes: FastifyPluginAsync = async (fastify) => { const trainService = new TrainService(fastify.db); // GET /api/trains fastify.get<{ Querystring: TrainFilters; Reply: { data: TrainResponse[] }; }>('/', { schema: { querystring: { type: 'object', properties: { fleet: { type: 'string' }, depot: { type: 'string' }, status: { type: 'string', enum: ['fit', 'unfit'] }, }, }, }, }, async (request, reply) => { const trains = await trainService.getTrains(request.query); return { data: trains }; }); // GET /api/trains/:id fastify.get<{ Params: { id: string }; Reply: { data: TrainResponse }; }>('/:id', async (request, reply) => { const train = await trainService.getTrainById(request.params.id); if (!train) { return reply.status(404).send({ error: 'Train not found', code: 'NOT_FOUND' }); } return { data: train }; }); // GET /api/trains/:id/history fastify.get<{ Params: { id: string }; Querystring: { months?: number }; Reply: { data: MileagePoint[] }; }>('/:id/history', async (request, reply) => { const months = request.query.months || 6; const history = await trainService.getTrainHistory(request.params.id, months); return { data: history }; }); }; ``` **Route Summary** | Method | Path | Handler | Description | |--------|------|---------|-------------| | GET | /api/dashboard/summary | getDashboardSummary | KPI metrics | | GET | /api/dashboard/depot-capacity | getDepotCapacity | Depot utilization | | GET | /api/trains | getTrains | List trains with filters | | GET | /api/trains/:id | getTrainById | Single train details | | GET | /api/trains/:id/history | getTrainHistory | Mileage history | | GET | /api/services/pending | getPendingServices | Unallocated services | | POST | /api/allocations/recommend | getRecommendations | Solver recommendations | | POST | /api/allocations | createAllocation | Create allocation | | GET | /api/balancing/distribution | getDistribution | Km distribution | | GET | /api/balancing/outliers | getOutliers | Over/under runners | | POST | /api/scenarios | createScenario | New scenario | | POST | /api/scenarios/:id/simulate | runSimulation | Execute simulation | | GET | /api/maintenance | getSchedule | Maintenance timeline | | PATCH | /api/maintenance/:id | reschedule | Move maintenance | #### 18.3 Service Layer Pattern **Service Class Structure** ```typescript // apps/api/src/services/trainService.ts import { eq, and, gte, lte, sql } from 'drizzle-orm'; import { db } from '../db/client'; import { trains, fleets, depots, mileageHistory } from '../db/schema'; export class TrainService { constructor(private db: typeof db) {} async getTrains(filters: TrainFilters): Promise { let query = this.db .select({ id: trains.id, trainId: trains.trainId, currentKm: trains.currentKm, fitnessStatus: trains.fitnessStatus, fleetName: fleets.name, depotName: depots.name, latitude: trains.latitude, longitude: trains.longitude, }) .from(trains) .leftJoin(fleets, eq(trains.fleetId, fleets.id)) .leftJoin(depots, eq(trains.baseDepotId, depots.id)); if (filters.fleet) { query = query.where(eq(trains.fleetId, filters.fleet)); } if (filters.depot) { query = query.where(eq(trains.baseDepotId, filters.depot)); } if (filters.status) { query = query.where(eq(trains.fitnessStatus, filters.status)); } return query; } async getTrainById(id: string): Promise { const [train] = await this.db .select() .from(trains) .leftJoin(fleets, eq(trains.fleetId, fleets.id)) .leftJoin(depots, eq(trains.baseDepotId, depots.id)) .where(eq(trains.id, id)); if (!train) return null; // Calculate delta from fleet average const avgResult = await this.db .select({ avg: sql`avg(${trains.currentKm})` }) .from(trains) .where(eq(trains.fleetId, train.trains.fleetId)); const fleetAverage = avgResult[0]?.avg || 0; const deltaPercent = ((train.trains.currentKm - fleetAverage) / fleetAverage) * 100; return { ...train.trains, fleet: train.fleets, depot: train.depots, deltaPercent: Math.round(deltaPercent * 10) / 10, }; } async getTrainHistory(trainId: string, months: number): Promise { const startDate = new Date(); startDate.setMonth(startDate.getMonth() - months); return this.db .select({ date: mileageHistory.recordedDate, km: mileageHistory.kmValue, }) .from(mileageHistory) .where( and( eq(mileageHistory.trainId, trainId), gte(mileageHistory.recordedDate, startDate) ) ) .orderBy(mileageHistory.recordedDate); } } ``` #### 18.4 Database Access (Drizzle ORM) **Schema Definition** ```typescript // apps/api/src/db/schema.ts import { pgTable, uuid, varchar, integer, decimal, timestamp, date, time, jsonb, index, uniqueIndex, } from 'drizzle-orm/pg-core'; export const fleets = pgTable('fleets', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(), manufacturer: varchar('manufacturer', { length: 50 }), pm1IntervalKm: integer('pm1_interval_km').notNull(), pm2IntervalKm: integer('pm2_interval_km').notNull(), hmIntervalKm: integer('hm_interval_km').notNull(), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }); export const depots = pgTable('depots', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), name: varchar('name', { length: 100 }).notNull(), code: varchar('code', { length: 10 }).notNull(), latitude: decimal('latitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }), longitude: decimal('longitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }), capacityTracks: integer('capacity_tracks').default(5), capacityManhours: integer('capacity_manhours').default(80), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ uniqueIndex('depots_code_idx').on(table.code), ]); export const trains = pgTable('trains', { id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(), trainId: varchar('train_id', { length: 20 }).notNull(), fleetId: uuid('fleet_id').references(() => fleets.id), baseDepotId: uuid('base_depot_id').references(() => depots.id), currentKm: integer('current_km').default(0), targetKm: integer('target_km').default(0), fitnessStatus: varchar('fitness_status', { length: 10 }).default('fit'), lastMaintenanceDate: date('last_maintenance_date'), nextMaintenanceKm: integer('next_maintenance_km'), latitude: decimal('latitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }), longitude: decimal('longitude', { precision: 10, scale: 6 }), createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(), updatedAt: timestamp('updated_at').defaultNow(), }, (table) => [ uniqueIndex('trains_train_id_idx').on(table.trainId), index('trains_fleet_idx').on(table.fleetId), index('trains_depot_idx').on(table.baseDepotId), ]); // ... additional tables (services, allocations, mileageHistory, maintenanceSchedules) ``` **Database Client** ```typescript // apps/api/src/db/client.ts import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/node-postgres'; import { Pool } from 'pg'; import * as schema from './schema'; const pool = new Pool({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL, max: 10, }); export const db = drizzle(pool, { schema }); ``` #### 18.5 Caching Strategy (Redis) **Cache Configuration** ```typescript // apps/api/src/cache/redis.ts import Redis from 'ioredis'; const redis = new Redis(process.env.REDIS_URL || 'redis://localhost:6379'); export const cache = { async get(key: string): Promise { const data = await redis.get(key); return data ? JSON.parse(data) : null; }, async set(key: string, value: T, ttlSeconds: number): Promise { await redis.setex(key, ttlSeconds, JSON.stringify(value)); }, async del(key: string): Promise { await redis.del(key); }, async invalidatePattern(pattern: string): Promise { const keys = await redis.keys(pattern); if (keys.length > 0) { await redis.del(...keys); } }, }; // Cache key patterns export const CACHE_KEYS = { DASHBOARD_SUMMARY: 'dashboard:summary', DEPOT_CAPACITY: (date: string) => `depot:capacity:${date}`, TRAINS_LIST: (hash: string) => `trains:list:${hash}`, TRAIN_DETAIL: (id: string) => `train:${id}`, SOLVER_RESULT: (hash: string) => `solver:${hash}`, }; // TTL values in seconds export const CACHE_TTL = { DASHBOARD: 60, // 1 minute TRAINS: 30, // 30 seconds DEPOT: 300, // 5 minutes SOLVER: 300, // 5 minutes STATIC: 3600, // 1 hour }; ``` **Cache Usage in Service** ```typescript // Using cache in service layer async getDashboardSummary(): Promise { // Try cache first const cached = await cache.get(CACHE_KEYS.DASHBOARD_SUMMARY); if (cached) return cached; // Fetch from database const summary = await this.computeDashboardSummary(); // Store in cache await cache.set(CACHE_KEYS.DASHBOARD_SUMMARY, summary, CACHE_TTL.DASHBOARD); return summary; } ``` #### 18.6 Error Handling **Error Types** ```typescript // apps/api/src/errors/index.ts export class AppError extends Error { constructor( public statusCode: number, public code: string, message: string, public details?: Record ) { super(message); this.name = 'AppError'; } } export class NotFoundError extends AppError { constructor(resource: string, id: string) { super(404, 'NOT_FOUND', `${resource} with id ${id} not found`); } } export class ValidationError extends AppError { constructor(message: string, details?: Record) { super(400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR', message, details); } } export class ConflictError extends AppError { constructor(message: string) { super(409, 'CONFLICT', message); } } export class SolverError extends AppError { constructor(message: string) { super(503, 'SOLVER_ERROR', message); } } ``` **Global Error Handler** ```typescript // Error handler plugin fastify.setErrorHandler((error, request, reply) => { if (error instanceof AppError) { return reply.status(error.statusCode).send({ error: error.message, code: error.code, details: error.details, }); } // Unexpected error fastify.log.error(error); return reply.status(500).send({ error: 'Internal Server Error', code: 'INTERNAL_ERROR', }); }); ``` --- ### Chapter 19: Python Solver Architecture This chapter details the OR-Tools optimization engine. #### 19.1 OR-Tools CP-SAT Model **Solver Structure** ```python # solver/src/engine/allocator.py from ortools.sat.python import cp_model from dataclasses import dataclass from typing import List, Tuple @dataclass class Train: id: str train_code: str current_km: int fleet_average: int depot_id: str latitude: float longitude: float fitness_status: str @dataclass class Service: id: str service_code: str distance_km: int origin: str destination: str origin_lat: float origin_lng: float dest_lat: float dest_lng: float class AllocationSolver: def __init__(self, trains: List[Train], service: Service, config: dict): self.trains = trains self.service = service self.config = config self.model = cp_model.CpModel() def solve(self) -> List[dict]: # Filter fit trains only candidates = [t for t in self.trains if t.fitness_status == 'fit'] if not candidates: return [] # Score each candidate scored = [] for train in candidates: score = self._calculate_score(train) justifications = self._generate_justifications(train) scored.append({ 'train_id': train.id, 'train_code': train.train_code, 'score': score, 'justifications': justifications, 'dead_running_km': self._calculate_dead_running(train), }) # Sort by score descending scored.sort(key=lambda x: x['score'], reverse=True) # Return top 5 return scored[:5] def _calculate_score(self, train: Train) -> int: score = 100 # Start with perfect score # Factor 1: Km Balance (40% weight) delta_pct = abs(train.current_km - train.fleet_average) / train.fleet_average balance_penalty = min(delta_pct * 100, 40) # Max 40 points penalty # Bonus for under-running trains (we want to use them more) if train.current_km < train.fleet_average: balance_penalty = balance_penalty * 0.5 # Half penalty for under-runners score -= balance_penalty # Factor 2: Dead Running (15% weight) dead_km = self._calculate_dead_running(train) dead_penalty = min((dead_km / 100) * 15, 15) # Max 15 points penalty score -= dead_penalty # Factor 3: Depot Proximity (25% weight) dest_to_depot = self._distance_to_nearest_depot(train) proximity_penalty = min((dest_to_depot / 50) * 25, 25) score -= proximity_penalty # Factor 4: Maintenance Alignment (20% weight) # (Simplified - in real implementation, check maintenance schedule) maintenance_bonus = 0 score += maintenance_bonus return max(0, min(100, int(score))) def _calculate_dead_running(self, train: Train) -> int: """Calculate empty km to reach service origin""" # Haversine distance approximation from math import radians, cos, sin, sqrt, atan2 lat1, lon1 = radians(train.latitude), radians(train.longitude) lat2, lon2 = radians(self.service.origin_lat), radians(self.service.origin_lng) dlat = lat2 - lat1 dlon = lon2 - lon1 a = sin(dlat/2)**2 + cos(lat1) * cos(lat2) * sin(dlon/2)**2 c = 2 * atan2(sqrt(a), sqrt(1-a)) # Earth radius in km return int(6371 * c) def _distance_to_nearest_depot(self, train: Train) -> int: # Simplified - would need depot list return 10 # Placeholder def _generate_justifications(self, train: Train) -> List[str]: justifications = [] # Km balance justification delta_pct = ((train.current_km - train.fleet_average) / train.fleet_average) * 100 if delta_pct < -5: justifications.append(f"Km {abs(delta_pct):.0f}% below fleet average - prioritizing for balance") elif delta_pct > 5: justifications.append(f"Km {delta_pct:.0f}% above fleet average - acceptable given other factors") else: justifications.append("Km within optimal balance range") # Dead running justification dead_km = self._calculate_dead_running(train) if dead_km < 20: justifications.append(f"Only {dead_km}km dead running to service start") elif dead_km < 50: justifications.append(f"{dead_km}km dead running - acceptable trade-off") else: justifications.append(f"вҡ пёҸ {dead_km}km dead running - consider alternatives") # Depot proximity justification justifications.append(f"Service ends near maintenance depot") return justifications ``` #### 19.2 Scoring Algorithm **Score Breakdown** | Factor | Weight | Scoring Logic | |--------|--------|--------------| | Km Balance | 40% | Lower delta from average = higher score | | Dead Running | 15% | Shorter empty km = higher score | | Depot Proximity | 25% | Closer to maintenance depot = higher score | | Maintenance Alignment | 20% | Aligned with scheduled maintenance = higher score | **Score Calculation** ```python def calculate_final_score(factors: dict) -> int: """ Calculate weighted score from factors. Each factor is 0-100, final score is weighted average. """ weights = { 'km_balance': 0.40, 'dead_running': 0.15, 'depot_proximity': 0.25, 'maintenance_alignment': 0.20, } score = sum(factors[k] * weights[k] for k in weights) return max(0, min(100, int(score))) ``` #### 19.3 Justification Generation **Justification Templates** ```python # solver/src/engine/justifier.py JUSTIFICATION_TEMPLATES = { 'km_balance_under': "Km {delta:.0f}% below fleet average - prioritizing for balance", 'km_balance_over': "Km {delta:.0f}% above average - acceptable given other factors", 'km_balance_optimal': "Km within optimal balance range (Вұ{threshold}%)", 'dead_running_low': "Only {km}km dead running to service start", 'dead_running_medium': "{km}km dead running - acceptable trade-off for km balancing", 'dead_running_high': "вҡ пёҸ {km}km dead running - consider alternatives if available", 'depot_close': "Service ends {km}km from {depot} maintenance depot", 'depot_far': "Service ends {km}km from nearest depot", 'maintenance_aligned': "PM{type} scheduled in {days} days at {depot} - natural alignment", 'maintenance_window': "Maintenance due within service window - good timing", 'maintenance_none': "No immediate maintenance requirements", 'aptitude_confirmed': "Train certified for {line} line operations", } class Justifier: def generate(self, train: Train, service: Service, scores: dict) -> List[str]: justifications = [] # Always include km balance (most important) km_just = self._justify_km_balance(train, scores['km_balance']) justifications.append(km_just) # Include depot proximity depot_just = self._justify_depot(train, service, scores['depot_proximity']) justifications.append(depot_just) # Include maintenance if relevant if scores.get('maintenance_alignment', 0) > 50: maint_just = self._justify_maintenance(train) justifications.append(maint_just) # Include dead running (as warning if high) dead_just = self._justify_dead_running(train, service, scores['dead_running']) justifications.append(dead_just) return justifications[:4] # Max 4 justifications def _justify_km_balance(self, train: Train, score: int) -> str: delta = ((train.current_km - train.fleet_average) / train.fleet_average) * 100 if delta < -10: return JUSTIFICATION_TEMPLATES['km_balance_under'].format(delta=abs(delta)) elif delta > 10: return JUSTIFICATION_TEMPLATES['km_balance_over'].format(delta=delta) else: return JUSTIFICATION_TEMPLATES['km_balance_optimal'].format(threshold=10) # ... other justification methods ``` #### 19.4 API Interface (FastAPI) ```python # solver/src/main.py from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException from pydantic import BaseModel from typing import List, Optional import time from engine.allocator import AllocationSolver, Train, Service app = FastAPI(title="MMS Solver", version="1.0.0") class TrainInput(BaseModel): id: str train_code: str current_km: int fleet_average: int depot_id: str latitude: float longitude: float fitness_status: str class ServiceInput(BaseModel): id: str service_code: str distance_km: int origin: str destination: str origin_lat: float origin_lng: float dest_lat: float dest_lng: float class RecommendRequest(BaseModel): service: ServiceInput candidates: List[TrainInput] constraints: Optional[dict] = {} class Recommendation(BaseModel): train_id: str train_code: str score: int justifications: List[str] dead_running_km: int class RecommendResponse(BaseModel): recommendations: List[Recommendation] solver_time_ms: int @app.post("/recommend", response_model=RecommendResponse) async def recommend(request: RecommendRequest): start_time = time.time() try: # Convert input to domain objects trains = [Train(**t.dict()) for t in request.candidates] service = Service(**request.service.dict()) # Run solver solver = AllocationSolver(trains, service, request.constraints or {}) results = solver.solve() solver_time = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000) return RecommendResponse( recommendations=[Recommendation(**r) for r in results], solver_time_ms=solver_time ) except Exception as e: raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e)) @app.get("/health") async def health(): return {"status": "ok", "solver": "ready"} ``` #### 19.5 Fallback Mechanisms **Simple Scoring Fallback** ```python # solver/src/engine/fallback.py def simple_score(trains: List[Train], service: Service) -> List[dict]: """ Fallback scoring when CP-SAT times out. Uses simple heuristics without optimization. """ scored = [] for train in trains: if train.fitness_status != 'fit': continue # Simple km delta score delta_pct = abs(train.current_km - train.fleet_average) / train.fleet_average score = max(0, 100 - int(delta_pct * 100)) # Bonus for under-runners if train.current_km < train.fleet_average: score = min(100, score + 10) scored.append({ 'train_id': train.id, 'train_code': train.train_code, 'score': score, 'justifications': [ f"Km delta: {int(delta_pct * 100)}% from average", "Scored using simple heuristic (solver timeout)" ], 'dead_running_km': 0, # Not calculated in fallback }) scored.sort(key=lambda x: x['score'], reverse=True) return scored[:5] ``` **Timeout Handler** ```python # In allocator.py import asyncio async def solve_with_timeout(solver: AllocationSolver, timeout_sec: int = 30): try: return await asyncio.wait_for( asyncio.to_thread(solver.solve), timeout=timeout_sec ) except asyncio.TimeoutError: # Use fallback return simple_score(solver.trains, solver.service) ``` --- ### Chapter 20: Database Design This chapter provides the complete database specification. #### 20.1 Entity-Relationship Diagram ``` identifier | | service_id | UUID | FK вҶ’ services(id) | Allocated service | | train_id | UUID | FK вҶ’ trains(id) | Allocated train | | allocation_date | DATE | NOT NULL | Date of allocation | | status | VARCHAR(20) | DEFAULT 'pending' | pending/confirmed/completed/cancelled | | score | INTEGER | | Solver score (0-100) | | justifications | JSONB | | Array of reason strings | | created_by |
